title: Survey of Accounting

subject: ACCT

description: User-oriented approach to the study of financial accounting and reporting topics related to business decisions.
subject: ACCT

title: Introduction to Managerial Accounting

subject: ACCT
prereq: Prerequisites: ACCT 225.

title: Survey of Commercial Law
description: Basic legal concepts and the judicial system, with emphasis on business law.
subject: ACCT

title: Survey of Federal Taxation
description: Federal tax law and preparation of individual income tax returns. Not allowed as an upper-division elective by accounting majors and not open for students who received credit for ACCT 403 .
subject: ACCT

title: Financial Accounting I
description: Accounting theory and practice as it relates to preparation of financial statements.
subject: ACCT

subject: ACCT
prereq: Prerequisites: ACCT 401.

title: Auditing I
description: Principles of auditing necessary to evaluate the integrity of accounting systems and financial reporting.
subject: ACCT
prereq: Prerequisites: ACCT 404.

description: Advanced topics in accounting theory and practice as it relates to the preparation of financial statements.
subject: ACCT

subject: ACCT

subject: ACCT

subject: ACCT

description: Analysis of current topics, issues, and practices in various areas of accounting. May be repeated as content varies by title.
subject: ACCT

description: Supervised work experience as approved by the department. Pass-Fail only. Internship contract required.
subject: ACCT
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in ACCT 401.

description: Accounting theory and practice as it relates to the preparation and analysis of sustainability reports and disclosures.
subject: ACCT

description: Advanced topics in accounting theory and practice as it relates to the preparation of financial statements.

description: Basic concepts of taxation of C Corporations, S Corporations, partnerships, and tax administration procedures.
subject: ACCT

description: The study of legal issues affecting accountants and managers.

prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in ACCT 324.

description: Accounting principles and procedures for governmental units and nonprofit organizations.

title: International Financial Reporting
description: Study of the principles and application of international financial reporting standards.
subject: ACCT
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in ACCT 405.

title: Special Topics in Accounting
description: Analysis of current topics, issues and practices in various areas of accounting. May be repeated as content varies by title.
subject: ACCT

title: Master of Accountancy Student Development
description: Skills and strategies for Master of Accountancy students at the University of South Carolina.
subject: ACCT

title: Application of Advanced Databases to Accounting and Business
description: The integration, configuration, and operation of accounting information within enterprise resource planning and other databases as applied to current business practices.
subject: ACCT

title: Analytics in Accounting
description: This business analytics course focuses on data analytics applied to accounting. The proliferation of data requires that accountants and other professionals understand the character of large datasets and their implications for decision-making. The course provides students with an understanding of data analytic thinking and terminology, as well as practical experience with related tools and techniques. Students will attain the skills necessary to translate accounting and business data into decision-making information.
subject: ACCT
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MGSC 291 and ACCT 404.

title: System and Organization Controls
description: A survey of system and organization controls (SOC) reporting over financial controls (SOC 1) and Trust Services Criteria (SOC 2) in a public accounting and consulting environment.
subject: ACCT
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in ACCT 404 and ACCT 406.

title: Essentials of Accounting for MBA Students
description: Overview of essential financial and managerial accounting topics relevant to MBA students seeking to increase their understanding of the production, interpretation, and communication of accounting information to internal and external users.
subject: ACCT

title: Financial Accounting for Professional MBA Students
description: Accounting concepts and practices necessary to understand and use the information in corporate financial statements.
subject: ACCT

title: Managerial Accounting for Professional MBA Students
description: Explores the types of decisions managers make and the types of accounting information that are helpful in making these decisions. Emphasizes the techniques and data used for planning, controlling, and evaluating operations.
subject: ACCT
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in ACCT 725.

title: Financial Accounting
description: Directs attention to accounting concepts, conventions, and assumptions for an understanding of the content and underlying principles of financial statements.
subject: ACCT

title: Managerial Accounting
description: Directs attention to an understanding of the manner in which accounting aids management by providing information for decision-making and control of operations.
subject: ACCT
prereq: Prerequisites: ACCT 728.

title: International Accounting
description: A study of the international dimensions of accounting, including such topics as the patterns of accounting development found in other nations, the promulgation of worldwide accounting standards, and the accounting problems associated with multinational corporate operations.
subject: ACCT
prereq: Prerequisites: ACCT 728 and ACCT 729.

title: Federal Taxes and Management Decisions
description: Fundamentals of taxation with attention upon federal income tax provisions and the consequences of business decisions.
subject: ACCT
prereq: Prerequisites: ACCT 728 and ACCT 729.

title: Auditing II
description: Advanced topics in independent, internal, and governmental auditing.
subject: ACCT

title: Accounting Regulation and Financial Reporting Issues
description: Examination of the theoretical, practical, economic, and political aspects of accounting regulation and financial reporting issues.
subject: ACCT

title: Accounting Research and Communication
description: Research on accounting and auditing issues, and the oral and written communication of accounting and auditing processes and research results.
subject: ACCT

title: Cost/Managerial Accounting III
description: A critical examination of contemporary cost accounting theory and practice.
subject: ACCT

title: Information Technology Assurance, Control, and Security
description: Governance, control, and audit of information technology.
subject: ACCT

title: Accounting Information Systems from a Strategic Perspective
description: Design and implement of accounting information systems to achieve strategic objectives.
subject: ACCT
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in ACCT 404.

title: Financial Statement Analysis
description: Analysis of financial statements for profitability and risk assessment and for firm and segment valuation.
subject: ACCT
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in DMSB 717 or ACCT 726 or ACCT 729 or equivalent.

title: Enterprise Resource Planning
description: Business process integration within Enterprise Resource Planning systems including the use and management of the enterprise core modules within ERP software implemented companies.
subject: ACCT

title: Special Topics in Accounting
description: A study of selected accounting topics.
subject: ACCT

title: Independent Study in Professional Accounting
description: Individually arranged studies in specialized areas of professional accounting.
subject: ACCT

title: Accounting for Management Control
description: Concepts and techniques of accounting and budgeting for management control in the modern organization. Topics include cost control, budgetary control, and performance and evaluation.
subject: ACCT
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in ACCT 726 or ACCT 729.

title: Fraud Examination and Investigation
description: An introduction into fraud investigation and forensic accounting. Covers many of the steps of a forensic investigation with a focus on interviewing techniques and evidence collection and processing.
subject: ACCT

title: Auditing and Information Technology
description: The use of information technology for auditing business entities and other organizations.
subject: ACCT

title: Data Analytics for Accounting and Auditing
description: Analysis of data in a variety of methods and formats to produce accounting and auditing information to improve decision-making.
subject: ACCT
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in ACCT 745.

title: Accounting Information Systems for Strategic Management
description: Understand, design, and implement accounting information systems to effectively control and protect information for strategic management decision making.
subject: ACCT

title: Tax Issues for Tax Professionals
description: In-depth concepts of taxation of C Corporations, S Corporations, partnerships, gift and estates, and tax administration procedures.
subject: ACCT

title: Tax Research and Communication
description: Techniques of tax research focusing on advanced tax topics, tax administration, and procedures before the Internal Revenue Service, including oral and written communication of research results.
subject: ACCT

title: Advanced Business Entity Tax Issues
description: Advanced concepts of taxation of corporations and partnerships.
subject: ACCT
prereq: Prerequisites: B or better in ACCT 749.

title: Advanced Individual Tax Issues
description: Estate and gift tax, Estate planning, trust taxation, executive compensation, stock options, retirement accounts, AMT, and other individual tax issues.
subject: ACCT
prereq: Prerequisites: B or better in ACCT 749.

title: Multijurisdictional Tax Issues
description: Global, State, and local taxation issues, including (but not limited to) transfer pricing, foreign tax credits, subpart F, tax treaties, different types of taxes, nexus, and corporate income tax.
subject: ACCT
prereq: Prerequisites: B or better in ACCT 749.

title: Taxation of Corporate Reorganizations
description: An examination of the tax aspects of corporate mergers and reorganizations.
subject: ACCT
prereq: Prerequisites: ACCT 750.

title: Advanced Estate and Income Planning
description: Advanced estate planning techniques, including executive compensation.
subject: ACCT

title: Seminar in Special Tax Topics
description: Addresses current tax problems and tax planning opportunities.
subject: ACCT
prereq: Prerequisites: ACCT 750.

title: Doctoral Seminar in Accounting Research
description: Seminar for beginning doctoral students that provides an overview of research topic areas, methods, and designs currently used in accounting.
subject: ACCT

title: Doctoral Seminar in Financial Accounting
description: Research methods and issues related to financial accounting topics.
subject: ACCT

title: Doctoral Seminar in Managerial Accounting
description: Research methods and issues related to managerial accounting topics.
subject: ACCT

title: Doctoral Seminar in Auditing and Accounting Information Systems
description: Research methods and issues related to auditing and accounting information systems topics.
subject: ACCT

title: Doctoral Seminar in Contemporary Accounting Topics
description: Research methods and issues related to contemporary accounting topics.
subject: ACCT

title: Directed Doctoral Research Project in Accounting
description: Formulation of concepts, synthesis of literature, development of a research design, and conduct of any appropriate empirical tests.
subject: ACCT

title: US Air Force Heritage and Values I
description: Survey course introducing students to the U.S. Air Force and AFROTC. Topics include mission and organization of the Air Force, officership, professionalism, military customs and courtesies, and officer career opportunities.
subject: AERO

title: Leadership Laboratory 101
description: Provides cadets the basic skills/knowledge to be functional members of the cadet corps, and activities to build camaraderie and esprit-de-corps. Includes mandatory physical fitness program.
subject: AERO

title: US Air Force Heritage and Values II
description: Continuation of AERO 101 . Additional topics include Air Force core values, leadership principles, group leadership dynamics, and an introduction to verbal and written communications skills.
subject: AERO

title: Leadership Laboratory 102
description: Continuation of AERO 101L . Exposure to additional information on an Air Force career. Scenarios and problems teach followership and leadership skills. Includes mandatory physical fitness program.
subject: AERO

title: Team and Leadership Fundamentals I
description: Fundamental introduction to leadership and team-building.
subject: AERO

title: Leadership Laboratory 201
description: Preparation of students for summer training at an Air Force base; teaching drill and other leadership experiences. Includes mandatory physical fitness program.
subject: AERO

title: Team and Leadership Fundamentals II
description: Continuation of AERO 201 . This course continues to build fundamental leadership and team building skills.
subject: AERO

title: Leadership Laboratory 202
description: Continuation of AERO 201L . Focuses on AFROTC Honor Code, Field Training Manual/procedures, and expeditionary skills required at field training. Includes mandatory physical fitness program.
subject: AERO

title: Leading People and Effective Communication
description: Tools and strategies for effective leadership. Topics include improving writing and public speaking skills used for briefing as well as ethics training.
subject: AERO

title: Leadership Laboratory 301
description: Provides cadets opportunities to develop leadership and followership skills, as well as sharpen their planning, organization, and communication ability. Includes mandatory physical fitness program.
subject: AERO

title: Leading People and Effective Communication II
description: Continuation of AERO 301 . Advanced ethics and leadership skills designed to prepare the cadet to be an officer and a leader.
subject: AERO
prereq: Prerequisites: AERO 301.

title: Leadership Laboratory 302
description: Continuation of AERO 301L . Allows intermediate cadet leaders to further develop leadership and management skills essential in Air Force officers. Includes mandatory physical fitness program.
subject: AERO
prereq: Prerequisites: AERO 301L.

title: National Security/Leadership Responsibilities/Commissioning Preparation
description: Study of U. S. Constitution, the Armed Forces, civilian control of the military, elements of national security, USAF doctrine, Total Force, the Joint environment, terrorism, and regional and cultural studies.
subject: AERO
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in AERO 302 and AERO 302L.

title: Leadership Laboratory 401
description: Provides senior cadet leaders opportunities to develop leadership and supervisory skills, and to effectively manage resources toward mission accomplishment. Includes mandatory physical fitness program.
subject: AERO
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in AERO 302 and AERO 302L.

title: National Security / Leadership Responsibilities / Commissioning Preparation II
description: Continuation of AERO 401 . Topics include national security policy and process, air and space power operations, roles of the military in society and current domestic and international issues affecting the military profession.
subject: AERO
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in AERO 401 and AERO 401L.

title: Leadership Laboratory 402
description: Continuation of AERO 401L . Allows senior cadet leaders advanced opportunities to hone their leadership in preparation for entering active duty. Includes mandatory physical fitness program.
subject: AERO
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in AERO 401 and AERO 401L.

title: Extended Cadet Leader Leadership Laboratory
description: Provides extended cadet leaders opportunities to continue developing leadership, managerial, and communication skills, and to mentor junior cadet corps members. Includes mandatory physical fitness program.
subject: AERO
prereq: Prerequisites: AERO 402L.

title: Introduction into Aerospace Engineering
description: Historical overview of air and space flight. Principles of flight and characterization of the atmosphere and space. Vehicle concepts, and an introduction to aerodynamics, materials, structures, propulsion, flight mechanics, control, aircraft systems, and design.
subject: AESP

title: Aerodynamics I Incompressible Flow
description: Fundamentals of inviscid, incompressible flow. Derivation of basic equations for lift, drag and aerodynamic moments through dimensional analysis. Two dimensional flow over airfoils. Airfoil characteristics. Thin airfoil theory, finite wing theory. Wing and body interactions. Aerodynamic drag characteristics. Boundary layers.
subject: AESP
prereq: Prerequisites: D or better in MATH 242; D or better in EMCH 290.

title: Air-Breathing and Rocket Propulsion Technologies
description: Introduction to aircraft and rocket engines with emphasis on the performance and characteristics of various types of propulsion systems, including turbojet, turbofan, turboprop, ramjet, scramjet and liquid solid propellant rockets.
subject: AESP
prereq: Prerequisites: D or better in AESP 365.

title: Aerospace Systems
description: Fundamentals of flight control systems, engine control systems, fuel systems, hydraulic systems, landing gears, electrical systems, environmental control systems, emergency systems, avionics and rotary wing systems. Aerospace systems design and development methodology.
subject: AESP
prereq: Prerequisites: D or better in AESP 101, ENCP 101.

title: Aerospace Laboratory II
description: Introduction to experimental determination of structures, propulsion and systems aspects of aerospace engineering. Oral and written presentations and reports.
subject: AESP
prereq: Prerequisites: D or better in AESP 361.

title: Aerodynamics II: Compressible Flow
description: Thermodynamics of compressible flow. One dimensional steady flow; isentropic flow, normal shock waves oblique and curved shocks. Nozzle and diffuser design. Raleigh and Fanno flows and their application in propulsion devices.
subject: AESP
prereq: Prerequisites: D or better in EMCH 290 or ENCP 290 and AESP 265.

title: Aircraft Design Part I Basics
description: Aircraft mission analysis; Conceptual aircraft design; Weight estimation; Wing design; Payload compartment design; Stabilizer and control surface design; engine selection; aircraft systems design; performance analysis; trade studies; design verification; design documentation and presentation.
subject: AESP
prereq: Prerequisites: AESP 265.

title: Flight and Orbital Mechanics
description: Derivation of the general equations of motion (EoM) for aircraft and space flight. Solution of Aircraft EoM for cruise flight and flight maneuvers including coordinated turns, takeoff and landing. Solution of EoM for orbital mechanics problems including transfer trajectories. Calculation of required specific impulses. Design of interplanetary trajectories.
subject: AESP
prereq: Prerequisites: D or better in EMCH 310 or ENCP 210.

title: Design I
description: Techniques for managing, planning and executing engineering projects. Computer based analysis and synthesis techniques. Manufacturing and assembly techniques. Statistical methods to support manufacturing, safety and reliability based design. Engineering Ethics. Social impact of engineering decisions and projects. Effective written and oral communication.
subject: AESP
prereq: Prerequisites: D or better in AESP 350 and EMCH 577.

title: Special Problems: Aerospace Engineering
description: Special Problems (1-3) Individual investigation or studies of special topics related with aerospace engineering.
subject: AESP

title: Flight Dynamics and Control
description: Flight Dynamics and Control is a three-credit course that covers the dynamics of aircraft motion, methods of analysis and design for stability and control, longitudinal motions, lateral-directional motions, and coupled longitudinal and lateral-directional motions.
subject: AESP
prereq: Prerequisites: EMCH 330 or ENCP 330, AESP 420.

title: Aerospace Propulsion
description: Propulsion system performance and cycle analysis. Momentum theorem, thrust and propulsive efficiency. Thermodynamics of compressible flow with heat and work addition. Combustion Processes. Components and principles of turbojet, turbofan, ramjet and SCRAM jet engines. Combustors and afterburners. Introduction to rocket engines.
subject: AESP
prereq: Prerequisites: D or better in AESP 365.

title: Freedom Papers: Narratives of Race and Nation
description: A study of the United States founding documents that emphasizes how the experiences of African American citizens throughout history and culture shape the country’s values, norms, and ideals.
subject: AFAM

title: Introduction to African American Studies: Social and Historical Foundations
description: Introduction to the key debates, figures, and concepts that are fundamental to the interdisciplinary study of the historical, political, and social development of black life in America.
subject: AFAM

title: Introduction to African American Religions
description: The variety of religious traditions of African Americans, with emphasis on the contexts in which they developed.
subject: AFAM

title: Convergence and Divergence in African American and Jewish Relations: Historical and Contemporary
description: An examination of African American and Jewish American inter-ethnic, historical and contemporary connections and disconnections. Implications for educational, social, and social settings are considered.
subject: AFAM

title: African-American Cultures
description: An examination of African-American cultures in the New World.
subject: AFAM

title: African-American Feminist Theory
description: An interdisciplinary survey of the contributions of African-American women to feminist theory.
subject: AFAM

title: Psychology and the African-American Experience
description: Psychological theory and research as it applies to African Americans. Explores Africentric and other perspectives and roles of culture, racism, and historical phenomena.
subject: AFAM

title: Black Experience in the United States to 1865
description: The social, cultural, economic, and political life of black people in the United States to 1865.
subject: AFAM

title: Black Experience in the United States since 1865
description: The social, cultural, economic, and political life of black people in the United States since 1865.
subject: AFAM

title: The American Civil Rights Movement
description: Examination of the origins of Jim Crow and the multi-faceted struggle against it, and other forms of racial inequality, in the American South and the rest of the US since the early 20th century.
subject: AFAM

title: Religions of the African Diaspora
description: Explore development/theologies of African/African Diaspora religions; examine misunderstandings; arrive at a more sophisticated and nuanced vision of these religions and the people who hold them.
subject: AFAM

title: Environmental Racism and Justice
description: History of the environmental justice movement and the unequal distribution of environmental harms on low income, minority, and historically marginalized groups.
subject: AFAM

title: Antiracist Education
description: Basic concepts, issues, and practices of antiracist education. Topics include individual and institutional racism, overt and covert racism, curriculum, textbooks, power relationships, teacher-student relationships, and privacy.
subject: AFAM

title: Introduction to U.S. Racial and Ethnic Politics
description: Survey of theories of the impact of race, ethnicity, and racism on American politics, and analysis of major policies and racial group experience regarding American citizenship.
subject: AFAM

title: Race and Ethnic Relations
description: Theoretical and empirical approaches related to race/ethnicity and the current state of race relations in America, with some attention to global issues.
subject: AFAM

title: African-American Politics
description: African-American politics from the colonial period to the present. Emphasis on voting rights and strategies to advance black representation.
subject: AFAM

title: Music of the African World
description: An introduction to traditional and popular music styles from several cultures and regions in Africa as well as the African diaspora.
subject: AFAM

title: Cultural History of Hip Hop Music
description: Roots of rap/hip hop music from African bardic tradition to African American vernacular traditions and development as a musical genre; rap’s musical and verbal traits and political ideologies; hip hop’s influence on mainstream American society and global youth.
subject: AFAM

title: Lemonade and Beyond: Black Women's Agency and Community Building Through Musical Performance
description: A survey of the musical and artistic contributions of Black women from several musical traditions and eras, partially inspired by the visual album Lemonade released by Beyoncé Knowles Carter in 2016.
subject: AFAM

title: African American Art
description: A study of fine art, craft, and material culture made by people of African descent in the United States from the 17th century to the present, accounting for African American artistic innovation and creativity as it has influenced art production globally.
subject: AFAM

title: Working Through a Visual Archive of Blackness: Performative Practices of Making and Presenting
description: An introduction to Black creative art making, community building, performance, and critique as a means of creating new visual cultures. Aims to critically examine race and representations through the mediums of visual art, dance and movement cannons, performance art, photography, architecture, and film.
subject: AFAM

title: Race and Science Fiction
description: Draws on science fiction to understand the contemporary history of American racial and ethnic politics and to speculate about the significance of race in America’s political future.
subject: AFAM

title: Special Topics in African American Society and History
description: Reading and research on selected social and historical topics in African American studies. May be repeated as content varies by title.
subject: AFAM

title: Special Topics in African-American Arts and Cultures
description: Reading and research on selected arts and cultural topics in African-American studies. May be repeated as content varies by title.
subject: AFAM

title: Independent Study
description: Contract approved by instructor, advisor, and department chair is required for undergraduate students.
subject: AFAM

title: African American Political Thought
description: Survey of many of the major schools of historic and contemporary African American political thought.
subject: AFAM

title: Slavery, Literature Culture
description: Interdisciplinary analysis of how the experiences of enslaved people are represented through fiction, autobiography, film, art, and new media.
subject: AFAM

title: African-American Literature I: to 1903
description: Representative works of African-American writers to 1903.
subject: AFAM
prereq: Prerequisites: ENGL 101 and ENGL 102.

title: African-American Literature II: 1903 – Present
description: Representative works of African-American writers from 1903 to the present.
subject: AFAM
prereq: Prerequisites: ENGL 101, ENGL 102, and one course between ENGL 270 - ENGL 292.

title: African Literature
description: Authors and literary forms representative of Africa.
subject: AFAM
prereq: Prerequisites: ENGL 101, ENGL 102.

title: Caribbean Literature
description: Authors and literary forms representative of the Caribbean.
subject: AFAM
prereq: Prerequisites: ENGL 101, ENGL 102.

title: African-American English
description: Linguistic examination of the structure, history, and use of African-American English, as well as literary presentations, language attitudes, and issues relating to education and the acquisition of Standard English.
subject: AFAM
prereq: Prerequisites: ENGL 101 and ENGL 102, and one course between ENGL 270-ENGL 292.

title: AfroAsian Connections in American Culture
description: An examination of the political cracks, cultural changes, and liberation-focused collaborations between African Americans and Asian Americans in the contemporary period, including recent theoretical frameworks that bring Blackness and Asianness into view together in a post-Civil Rights era.
subject: AFAM

title: African American Women in History: Rebels, Radicals, Revolutionaries and Black Girl Magic
description: A survey of the social and political developments in the United States through the diverse experiences of African American women from slavery to the twenty-first century. Situates the heroism, activism, and feminism of African American women within a global context.
subject: AFAM

title: Jim Crow: Histories Revivals
description: This course critically examines the continuities and discontinuities between Jim Crow and our current historical and political moment.
subject: AFAM

title: Black Activism
description: Critical review of theories of community organizing, grassroots activism, and social movements, and examination of contemporary forms of black activism.
subject: AFAM

title: African-American Rhetoric
description: African-American rhetoric as manifested in speeches, essays, and other rhetorical artifacts.
subject: AFAM
prereq: Prerequisites: WGST 111 or WGST 112 or ANTH 102.

title: Black Women Writers
description: An examination of literature by and about black women, including fiction, poetry, drama, and autobiography. This study will focus on issues that emerge from the creative representations of black women and the intersections of race, gender, sexuality, and class that interrogate what is both particular and universal experiences.
subject: AFAM
prereq: Prerequisites: ENGL 101, ENGL 102.

title: The Black Arts Movement
description: Analysis of the poetry, drama, art, music, and essays of the Black Arts Movement during the 1960s and 1970s, and their impact on American politics, history, and culture.
subject: AFAM
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in ENGL 101 and ENGL 102.

title: Miscarriages of Justice
description: An interdisciplinary examination of the historical and current functioning of the American criminal justice system and the ways that society has created and applied different practices and standards of justice with regard to marginalized populations.
subject: AFAM
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in AFAM 201.

title: Race, Gender, and Graphic Novels
description: Representations of race and gender in comics with a special emphasis on the experiences of African Americans.
subject: AFAM

title: An Anthropological View of Blacks in Film
description: Cultural representations, constructions, production, and consumption of African-American identity in the popular culture medium of feature films.
subject: AFAM

title: African American Theatre
description: The major movements, figures, plays, and critical strategies that have marked the development of African American theatre in the 19th, 20th, and 21st centuries.
subject: AFAM
prereq: Prerequisites: ENGL 101, ENGL 102, and one course between ENGL 270 - ENGL 292.

title: Culture and Identity in the African Diaspora
description: Students will explore the African Diaspora as a social, cultural, and historical formation with Africa at its center, focusing on US, Latin American, and Caribbean African-descended communities.
subject: AFAM

title: Advanced Topics in African American Studies
description: Reading and research on selected topics in African American Studies. May be repeated as content varies by title.
subject: AFAM

title: Anesthesiology Elective
description: The anesthesia clinical rotation will provide the medical students with hands on experiences at airway management including bag/mask ventilation, laryngoscopy, and intubation. The student will learn basic principles of anesthetic management and the indications for different general and regional anesthetic techniques. He/she will also experience a practical review and refinement of pharmacology and physiology in the clinical setting. Students will also receive an overview of acute and chronic pain management.
subject: ANES

title: Advanced Focus in Anesthesiology
description: Designed for students pursuing Anesthesiology residency, the anesthesia clinical rotation will provide the medical students with hands on experiences at airway management including bag/mask ventilation, laryngoscopy, and intubation. The student will learn basic principles of anesthetic management and the indications for different general and regional anesthetic techniques. He/she/they will also experience a practical review and refinement of pharmacology and physiology in the clinical setting. Students will also receive an overview of acute and chronic pain management.
subject: ANES

title: Focus in Anesthesiology
description: This 2-week/2-credit version of this course is intended for all other M4s (those NOT pursuing Anesthesiology Residency). The two-week anesthesia clinical rotation will provide medical students with an experience in airway management including bag/mask ventilation, direct laryngoscopy, and intubation. Students will learn basic principles of anesthetic management and the indications for different general and regional anesthetic techniques. The student will also experience a practical review and refinement of pharmacology and physiology in the peri-operative setting. Students will also receive a brief overview of acute and chronic pain management.
subject: ANES

title: Anesthesiology Away Rotation
description: The student will complete an intensive study of Anesthesiology at another hospital or practice. This course must be approved through the VSAS application service or office of the M3/M4 Director.
subject: ANES

title: Primates, People, and Prehistory
description: An exploration of human origins, human evolution, human prehistory, and cultural existence from its less complex forms to early civilizations. An introduction to the concepts, methods, and data of physical, biological, and archaeological anthropology.
subject: ANTH

title: Understanding Other Cultures
description: An exploration and comparison of selected contemporary cultures, including their languages. An introduction to the concepts, methods, and data of socio-cultural anthropology and anthropological linguistics.
subject: ANTH

title: Human Origins: An Introduction to Biological Anthropology
description: An introduction to the science of biological anthropology, a sub-field of anthropology that emphasizes a focus on humanity and its origin from a biological perspective, employing laboratory components to complement and reinforce lecture materials.
subject: ANTH

title: Anthropological Inquiry in Undergraduate Research
description: Introduces research-based learning in anthropology from a four-field perspective. To encourage self-reflective, professional thinking and provide experience and practice in professional skills and applications in anthropology.
subject: ANTH

title: Comparing Cultures Through Film
description: Human behavior in differing cultural contexts through ethnographic films of social relations in selected societies.
subject: ANTH

title: Anthropology of Magic and Religion
description: A comparative examination of such topics as ritual, cosmology, revitalization movements, magic, witchcraft, myth, and possession.
subject: ANTH

title: Gender and Culture
description: Anthropological study of gender, with emphasis on cross-cultural investigation of the interaction of biological, cultural, and environmental factors including intersections of race, social class, and sexuality as influences gender behavior.
subject: ANTH

title: Anthropology of Globalization and Development
description: Examine cross-cultural definitions and experiences of globalization and development, through topics including colonial legacies of inequality, migration, land use, economic restructuring, media, consumption, tourism, health, and participatory development.
subject: ANTH

title: Introduction to Folklore
description: Folk expression as shaped by various cultures; fieldwork methodology and anthropological theory.
subject: ANTH

title: Human Life Cycles in Different Cultures
description: Childhood, maturity, old age, and gender socialization within the family.
subject: ANTH

title: Learning Across Cultures
description: Classroom ethnography, bilingualism, cultural minorities, communication across cultural boundaries. Films, videotapes, and fieldwork in classroom settings.
subject: ANTH

title: Food and Culture
description: Biological and cultural interactions affecting foodways around the world, and associated ethical issues.
subject: ANTH

title: Ethnobotany: Plants and Peoples
description: Anthropological overview of the interactions between cultures around the world and the plants that affect them, from cultural, biological, archaeological, and linguistic points of view.
subject: ANTH

title: Drinking in Culture: Anthropology of Alcohol
description: Cultures of alcohol production and consumption from ancient times to the present, including relationships among social roles of alcohol, technological innovations, agriculture, and economy in an anthropological perspective.
subject: ANTH

title: Violence and Peace: Anthropological Perspectives
description: Violence and peace in current events, cultural practices, historical periods, and everyday experiences. The ethics shaping violence and peace-making strategies. Classroom discussions and lectures analyzing harm and wellbeing. Themes addressing the Values, Ethics, and Social Responsibility (VSR) Carolina Core component, including colonialism, environmental exploitation, bondage, mass extinctions, and racism.
subject: ANTH

title: Great Discoveries in Archaeology
description: Survey of key archaeological discoveries from around the world.
subject: ANTH

title: Forensics of Sherlock Holmes
description: Forensic methods of Sherlock Holmes within the context of modern forensic science. Aspects of forensic science including history of the discipline, forensic pathology, entomology, print analyses, crime scene analysis, forensic anthropology, early scientific theory, and anthropological theory of Holmes.
subject: ANTH

title: Modernity Archaeology and the Recent Past
description: Explores the last five centuries of world history, using artifacts and archival sources. Evidence such as probate records, bottles, and geophysical maps are analyzed to discover the age of sites and answers to questions about topics such as colonialism, race, technology, piracy, class, Native Americans, industrialization, slavery, inequality, capitalism, and gender.
subject: ANTH

title: Indigenous Caribbean Archaeology
description: Historical archaeology and ethnography of the Casimiroid, Ortoiroid, Saladoid, Ostionoid, Taino and Carib indigenous culture of the Caribbean from 4,000 BC to 1524 AD. Emphasis on social complexity, religion, art and political organization to illustrate the diversity and richness of Amerindian Caribbean life until their rapid decline after European contact.
subject: ANTH

title: Archaeology in Film and Popular Culture
description: Archaeological images and ideas in modern popular culture, including film and fiction.
subject: ANTH

title: Biblical Archaeology
description: The fundamental elements of human culture as it relates to biblical archaeology. The defining characteristics of different kinds of society through interdependency of language and culture. The affects of modern world interests in defining / redefining this area.
subject: ANTH

title: Forbidden Archaeology: Fantasies, Frauds, and Mysteries of the Human Past
description: History and basis of several popular “fringe” ideas about the human past that utilize archaeological information: giants, Ice Age civilizations, and pre-Columbian transoceanic contact.
subject: ANTH

title: North American Prehistory
description: Prehistoric anthropology in North America from the first arrival of man through the beginning of European acculturation.
subject: ANTH

title: Southeastern Archaeology
description: Major cultural milestones and lifeways experienced by Indians in the archeological record of the southeastern U.S., including colonization, religion, trade, invention of pottery, and place-making.
subject: ANTH

title: Diversity in the United States
description: Application of techniques and insights of social and cultural anthropology to selected cultural settings in contemporary USA.
subject: ANTH

title: Contemp Cultures of South Carolina
description: Application of the methods techniques of socio-cultural anthropology to the contemporary cultures of SC. Examination of contrasts such as low country and up country, black and white, and rich and poor as they are manifested in cultural patterns.
subject: ANTH

title: Caribbean Cultures
description: Ethnographic approach to Caribbean cultures and societies. Topics include colonial histories and experience, gender and race relations, beliefs and religious life, verbal arts, literature, and Creole language.
subject: ANTH

title: Cultures of Africa
description: A comparative study of ethnographic data on African cultures with emphasis upon its significance for broader anthropological theory.
subject: ANTH

title: Cultures of Islam
description: Diversity of lifestyles and institutions of Islam from Morocco to Indonesia, with attention to everyday life in small communities.
subject: ANTH

title: Middle Eastern Cultures
description: A consideration of selected problems in the social and cultural life of peoples in the Middle East with emphasis on non-Arab populations.
subject: ANTH

title: South Asian Cultures
description: Society and culture in South Asia; economic and political institutions, kinship, and religion as they pertain to the daily lives of people in the Subcontinent. Emphasis on India. Bangladesh, Nepal, Pakistan, and Sri Lanka also included.
subject: ANTH

title: Southeast Asian Cultures
description: Social and cultural patterns of the region and how they influence current developments, especially Indonesia, Thailand, Vietnam, and the Philippines.
subject: ANTH

title: Chinese Popular Culture
description: An overview of Chinese popular culture with an introduction to broad anthropological frameworks concerning popular culture.
subject: ANTH

title: Japanese Cultures
description: An exploration of Japanese values and the institutions that shape Japanese behavior through analysis or rural and urban community studies and how Japanese people present themselves.
subject: ANTH

title: American Indian Nations Today: From Hard Times to Hard Rock
description: Contemporary Indian Country in anthropological, historical, cultural, economic, and political contexts.
subject: ANTH

title: Planet of the Apes: Behavior and Biology
description: A survey of field and laboratory investigations of the comparative anatomy and behavior of nonhuman primates.
subject: ANTH

title: Human Variation
description: The biocultural processes of human variation.
subject: ANTH

title: Basic Forensic Anthropology
description: Survey of the basic scientific methods and applications of forensic anthropology.
subject: ANTH

title: Medical Experimentation and the Black Body
description: A cross-disciplinary study of how the bodies of Africans and African Americans were used in medical experimentation, starting in the late 18th century and continuing to the present.
subject: ANTH

title: Anthropology of Nonverbal Communication
description: Body language, facial expressions, gestures, use of interpersonal space, and other nonverbal systems of communication and behavior in terms of pertinent theories, research methodology, findings, and cross-cultural implications.
subject: ANTH

title: Language and Popular Culture
description: Linguistic anthropological study of forms of language through the lens of popular culture. Explore the ethnography of communication through play and performance, discursive and semiotic practices, and varieties of language invoked in popular cultural forms that provide resources for cultural reproduction and contestation.
subject: ANTH

title: Cross-Cultural Communication
description: This course introduces students to the fields of interactional sociolinguistics and linguistic anthropology. Students will learn how they approach the study of cross-cultural and intercultural forms of (mis)communication within the context of globally interconnected people, places, and systems of communication.
subject: ANTH

title: Humans Going Nuclear: Atomic Bombs, Cold War, and the Fallout
description: Ethnographic study of the Cold War, nuclear culture, and its aftermath.
subject: ANTH

title: Special Topics in Biological Anthropology
description: Topics of special interest in biological anthropology. May be taken more than once as topics change.
subject: ANTH

title: Special Topics in Sociocultural Anthropology
description: Topics of special interest in sociocultural anthropology. May be taken more than once as topics change.
subject: ANTH

title: Special Topics in Linguistic Anthropology
description: Topics of special interest in linguistic anthropology. May be taken more than once as topics change.
subject: ANTH

title: Selected Topics in Anthropology
description: Topics of special interest. May be taken more than once as topics change.
subject: ANTH

title: Disease, Health, and Social Inequities
description: Course focuses on political and economic processes contributing to the unequal access to health and social inequalities.
subject: ANTH

title: Latin American Cultures
description: Comparative study of selected Latin American cultures with emphasis on their significance for a broader anthropological theory.
subject: ANTH

title: African-American Cultures
description: An examination of African-American cultures in the New World.
subject: ANTH

title: Ethical Dilemmas in Anthropology
description: An examination of ethical decision-making encountered in the practice of anthropology.
subject: ANTH

title: Material Culture
description: Material aspects of cultures from artifact production in historical societies to contemporary industrial crafts; the cultural context of artifacts; fieldwork; relevant anthropological theories.
subject: ANTH

title: Principles of Archaeology
description: Introduction to principles, methods, and theory of archaeology, including prehistoric and historic case studies.
subject: ANTH

title: Archaeology Theory
description: This course charts the history of ideas in archaeology, over the past century, as a means of understanding current directions in archaeological thinking and current applications in archaeological practice.
subject: ANTH
prereq: Prerequisites: ANTH 319.

title: South Carolina Archaeology
description: Prehistoric and historic archaeology of South Carolina.
subject: ANTH

title: Field School in Archaeology
description: Archaeological field techniques, laboratory analysis and data interpretation.
subject: ANTH
prereq: Prerequisites: ANTH 319.

title: Field School in Ethnography
description: Designing and carrying out ethnographic research including project design, data collection, analysis and description.
subject: ANTH

title: Ethnoarchaeology
description: Current research on use of modern material culture in archaeological analysis.
subject: ANTH

title: Prehistoric Civilizations of the New World
description: Study of Mesoamerican and South American civilizations, particularly the Mayan, Aztec, and Inca states. Processes of state formation as reflected in archaeological data.
subject: ANTH

title: Ancient Civilizations
description: Causes for the rise and fall of several civilizations; ideological and ecological factors, unique events, and personalities versus general processes.
subject: ANTH

title: Mesoamerican Prehistory
description: Cultural development and variation in Mesoamerica from the first arrival of man to the arrival of Europeans. Particular attention to cultural continuities from prehistoric times.
subject: ANTH

title: Environmental Anthropology: Cross-cultural Perspectives on Environmental Change
description: Cross-cultural perspectives on environmental issues.
subject: ANTH

title: Anthropology of Work
description: Techniques, customs, verbal expressions, and expressive styles of workers in a variety of occupational cultures.
subject: ANTH

title: Anthropology Development
description: An examination of political and economic change in contemporary peasant communities.
subject: ANTH

title: The Family in Cross-Cultural Perspective
description: Kinship, systems of descent, marriage, and domestic organization in different cultures. Variations in childrearing practices, gender, and other aspects of social relations in kin groups.
subject: ANTH

title: Anthropology of Law and Conflict
description: Understanding human behavior through the examination of cultural norms, mechanisms of social control, and social conflict.
subject: ANTH

title: Language, Culture, and Society
description: Language in its social setting. The relationship between linguistic categories and culture categories. Language and cognition.
subject: ANTH

title: Anthropology of Art
description: Sculpture, drama, ceramics, weaving, music, and other arts from tribal societies will be discussed in terms of the religious, social, and aesthetic principles that underlie their production, use, and interpretation.
subject: ANTH

title: Psychological Anthropology
description: Cultural differences and pan-cultural similarities in such psychological features as personality and cognition.
subject: ANTH

title: Theories of Culture
description: Theory and practice of ethnology/sociocultural anthropology, based on a wide range of simple and complex societies.
subject: ANTH

title: Anthropology of Sex
description: An overview of human sexuality in different cultures in regions across the globe; an examination of anthropological frameworks for sexuality that draws on historical and modern cultural conceptions.
subject: ANTH

title: Becoming Human
description: The processes of homonoid development with a review of the basic principles of physical and behavioral evolution using the fossil record and the evolving ecological and psychosocial contexts.
subject: ANTH

title: Medicine, Disease, and Slavery
description: An interdisciplinary study of the health of enslaved African Americans during the nineteenth century by focusing on the conceptions, experiences, and dynamics of the relationship between slaves, medicine, healing, and their masters in the Antebellum American South.
subject: ANTH

title: Ethnography of Communication
description: Ethnographic analysis of communication in human groups and institutions.
subject: ANTH

title: Introduction to Language Sciences
description: Introduction to the linguistic component of human cognition. Properties of speech, the organization of language in the mind/brain, cross-linguistic universals, child language acquisition, and aspects of adult language processing.
subject: ANTH

title: Cognitive and Social Aspects of Bilingualism
description: Bilingual language development, social and cultural aspects of bilingualism, the bilingual brain, bilingualism throughout the lifespan.
subject: ANTH

title: Anthropology of Global Health
description: An in-depth exploration of global health issues through the lens of anthropology with cross-cultural perspectives.
subject: ANTH

title: Gender and Globalization
description: Examines the dialectic between globalization and the social construction of gender. Topics include the global assembly line, transnational markets for domestic labor and sex workers, and global feminist alliances.
subject: ANTH
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in ANTH 102 or WGST 112.

title: Cultures, Pregnancy, and Birth
description: Anthropological study of pregnancy and birth with a cross-cultural focus comparing the United States to other nations. Examination of cultural factors such as prenatal care, dietary practices, taboos, birth location, practitioners, and birthing styles.
subject: ANTH

title: Selected Topics in Anthropology
description: Topics of special interest. May be taken more than once as topics change.
subject: ANTH

title: Global Women's Health
description: This course examines health concerns important in the lives of women around the world through an overview of contemporary issues and challenges in the field of global health, broadly construed.
subject: ANTH

title: Toxic Environments and Invisible Harms
description: An anthropological approach to the study of environmental health, toxicity, and environmental injustice in comparative context.
subject: ANTH

title: African-American English
description: Linguistic examination of the structure, history, and use of African-American English, as well as literary presentations, language attitudes, and issues relating to education and the acquisition of Standard English.
subject: ANTH
prereq: Prerequisites: ENGL 101 and ENGL 102, and one course between ENGL 270-ENGL 292.

title: Internship in Anthropology
description: Supervised internship/experiential learning experience relevant to one of the four fields of anthropology.
subject: ANTH
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in ANTH 102; C or better in ANTH 101 or ANTH 161.

title: Senior Thesis
description: Directed research resulting in a written report.
subject: ANTH
prereq: Prerequisites: GPA of 3.00.

title: In the Tradition of Anthropology
description: A seminar synthesizing the major with an examination of anthropology as a field of inquiry.
subject: ANTH

title: Gender Issues in China
description: Anthropology of gender in Chinese-speaking cultures in Chinese-speaking Asia.
subject: ANTH

title: Anthropological Ethnobotany
description: Survey of how each anthropological subfield studies the interrelationships between plants and peoples. Application of methods, including interviewing and data analysis.
subject: ANTH

title: Tradition and Transformations in Islamic Cultures
description: Islam as a dynamic cultural tradition: emphasis on the tension between Islamization and the larger Islamic tradition.
subject: ANTH

title: An Anthropological View of Blacks in Film
description: Cultural representations, constructions, production, and consumption of African-American identity in the popular culture medium of feature films.
subject: ANTH

title: Visual Cultures
description: Survey of visual anthropology including theoretical frameworks of ways of seeing, ethnographic photography and filmmaking, contemporary technologies, and their effects on culture.
subject: ANTH

title: Field Problems in Ethnology
description: A two-semester class and field session. Research design, field methods, interpretation of data, and the development of theory from the data.
subject: ANTH

title: Ethnoecology
description: Seminar exploring human-plant-animal-natural interactions within an anthropological framework.
subject: ANTH

title: North American Archaeology
description: Prehistoric and historic archaeology.
subject: ANTH

title: Prehistoric Archaeology of South America
description: Prehistoric archaeology of the South American continent.
subject: ANTH

title: Conflict Archaeology
description: Anthropological and archaeological theories and methods in the study of conflict, war, and warfare. Causes, effects, outcomes of sustained social acts of violence of groups, tribes, states, and nations. Evolutionary, biological, social origins of warfare. History, strategy, and tactics, battlefield archaeology.
subject: ANTH

title: Public Archaeology
description: Philosophy and mechanics of modern archaeological Cultural Resource Management (CRM). CRM legislation, regulation, and process. Contemporary issues and problems in Public Archaeology including Native American reburial negotiations, conflict resolution, ethics, looting, business practices, standards, contexts and protection.
subject: ANTH

title: Field Problems in Archaeology
description: Archaeological field methods and techniques such as excavation, flotation, sampling, surveying, photography, and remote sensing.
subject: ANTH
prereq: Prerequisites: ANTH 320.

title: Archaeological Laboratory Methods
description: Laboratory on basic prehistoric and historic artifact analysis, including analytical methods, laboratory equipment, and data interpretation. May be repeated.
subject: ANTH

title: Medical Anthropology: Fieldwork
description: Application of observation techniques, field notes, informant interviewing, and secondary data analysis to interpreting differential perceptions of health problem solving in the community and clinic.
subject: ANTH

title: Medical Anthropology
description: Socio-cultural factors in health, illness, healing, and in medical systems. Cross-cultural and ethnographic evidence for public health research and program applications.
subject: ANTH

title: Anthropological Approaches to Narrative and Performance
description: The ways people from various cultures reflect on, reinforce, and construct their social realities through narrating, which will be considered as both artistic expression and social action.
subject: ANTH

title: Language and Gender
description: Approaches to gender and language emphasizing the social grounding of both; how language reflects sociocultural values and is a tool for constructing different types of social organization.
subject: ANTH

title: Language and Globalization
description: Anthropological approach to issues of language and globalization. Linguistic consequences of globalization under consideration include communicative patterns, linguistic change, and language and political economy.
subject: ANTH

title: Psychological Anthropology
description: Psychological aspects of behavior from a cross-cultural perspective.
subject: ANTH

title: Human Osteology
description: An intensive examination of the human skeleton and techniques for anthropological interpretation.
subject: ANTH

title: Health and Disease in the Past
description: Varieties and effects of disease patterns among past populations illustrating biological, environmental, and cultural interrelationships.
subject: ANTH

title: Human Identification in Forensic Anthropology
description: Theories and methodologies necessary for the identification of human skeletal remains in a forensic setting.
subject: ANTH

title: Nutritional Anthropology
description: Nutritional problems in developing nations. Measures of nutritional status. Social, economic, and environmental aspects of food consumption and nutrition. Biocultural responses to food deprivation and undernutrition.
subject: ANTH

title: International Development and the Environment
description: Intersections of international development and environmental change; study of general theoretical perspectives balanced with case studies from the Global South.
subject: ANTH

title: Ethnographic Film
description: Problems in conveying and interpreting ethnographic information on film or tape. Includes syntax, suitability of subject matter to the medium, irrelevant or distracting information, and observer bias.
subject: ANTH

title: Temporal Processes in Culture
description: Clocks, cycles, and contingencies as they affect human societies now and have done so in the past. Theories and models from biology and the other natural sciences will be used to interpret the history of culture.
subject: ANTH

title: Economic Anthropology
description: A cross-cultural study of the economic behavior of pre-literate and literate societies.
subject: ANTH

title: Archaeology of the African Diaspora
description: Foodways, architecture, crafts, and narrative of African-American cultures.
subject: ANTH

title: Advanced Topics in the Anthropological Study of Social Organization
description: Selected recent theoretical and methodological developments in the study of social organization.
subject: ANTH

title: Anthropology of Migration: Displacement, Borders, and Health
description: Biological, sociocultural, and health-related dimensions of migration, focusing on the movement of people across borders through a biocultural anthropological lens.
subject: ANTH

title: Cultural Ecology
description: An interdisciplinary approach to prehistoric, historic, and contemporary relationships between the development of socio-cultural configurations and ecosystems.
subject: ANTH

title: Culture and Identity in the African Diaspora
description: Students will explore the African Diaspora as a social, cultural, and historical formation with Africa at its center, focusing on US, Latin American, and Caribbean African-descended communities.
subject: ANTH

title: Globalization and Cultural Questions
description: This course examines cultural understandings of and responses to globalization, examining topics such as its history and theories, migration, economic integration and inequality, identity, social movements, and the environment.
subject: ANTH

title: Discourse, Gender, and Politics of Emotion
description: Anthropological approach to issues of discourse, gender, and emotion. Issues under consideration include the social control, force, and forms of emotional discourse and the relationship between emotion and culture from gender-oriented perspectives.
subject: ANTH

title: Selected Topics
description: Topics of special interest. May be taken more than once as topics change.
subject: ANTH

title: Survey of Linguistics
description: Survey of core areas of linguistics and extensions to closely related disciplines. Introduction to the linguistic component of human cognition. Formal description and analysis of the general properties of speech and language, the organization of language in the mind/brain, and cross-linguistic typology and universals.
subject: ANTH

title: Physical Anthropology and Archaeology for Teachers
description: Human origins, human evolution, human prehistory, and cultural existence from its less complex forms to early civilizations. An introduction to the concepts, methods, and data of physical, biological, and archaeological anthropology primarily for teachers. May be taken with, or independently of, ANTH 702 .
subject: ANTH

title: Social and Linguistic Anthropology for Teachers
description: Selected contemporary cultures, including their languages. An introduction to the concepts, methods, and data of sociocultural anthropology and anthropological linguistics, primarily for teachers. May be taken with, or independently of, ANTH 701 .
subject: ANTH

title: Anthropological Inquiry
description: A discussion of the general topics of anthropological inquiry, theories, and methods.
subject: ANTH

title: Anthropological Connections
description: Faculty representing subdisciplines of anthropology will explore with students the connections between subfields, theoretical and regional perspectives, and analyses of the past and present.
subject: ANTH
prereq: Prerequisites: ANTH 703.

title: Engendering Global Capitalism
description: The origins of global capitalism, the nature of money and debt, the roles of gender, race and class in social formations, and the relationship between production and reproduction.
subject: ANTH

title: Professionalism and Ethics
description: Presentations of critical skills to achieve career goals in a variety of anthropological applications, develop portfolios, prepare competitive job applications, and produce effective grant proposals. Ethics issues in anthropological research publishing and teaching.
subject: ANTH

title: Thesis Skills Seminar
description: Skills needed for writing a master’s thesis in anthropology, including literature review, current theory, research design, data analysis, and written presentation.
subject: ANTH

title: Teaching Practicum in Anthropology
description: Uses the context of leading discussions in ANTH 101 and 102 to introduce and explore issues relating to pedagogy. Restricted to TA's for ANTH 101 and ANTH 102 .
subject: ANTH

title: Seminar in European Archaeology
description: Consideration and critique of current research in European archaeology.
subject: ANTH

title: Field Problems in Ethnology
description: Advanced graduate seminar on methods of ethnology, including research design, field methods, and interpretation of data, and the development of theory from data. Includes class and field sessions.
subject: ANTH

title: Development of Anthropological Archaeology
description: Anthropological archaeology: history, theory, contemporary issues, and relationship to other disciplines.
subject: ANTH

title: Community Anthropology for Professionals
description: Those skills of social/cultural anthropology and anthropological linguistics which can aid practitioners in health, law, education, and other professional fields to function in community settings. Emphasis on cultural and sub-cultural differences in South Carolina, the Southeast, and the United States.
subject: ANTH

title: Summer Field School in Archaeology
description: Experience in supervising archaeological research, making field decisions, and directing the collection, processing, and interpretation of archaeological data in the field.
subject: ANTH

title: Summer Field School in Ethnography
description: Experience in designing and carrying out ethnographic research including project design, data collection, analysis, and description.
subject: ANTH

title: Visual Anthropology Research
description: Exploring the range of anthropological research utilizing visual records (still photographs and video/film) including theoretical underpinnings and hands-on practice: how and why to use visual records in research.
subject: ANTH

title: Cultural Theory through Ethnography
description: Theories of culture presented through ethnographies from different parts of the world. Issues in writing, reading, and interpreting ethnographic information.
subject: ANTH

title: Current Issues in Archaeology
description: Review of theoretical trends in American archaeology.
subject: ANTH

title: Ethnology for Archaeologists
description: Ethnographic data important to archaeological thinking; archaeological models resting on ethnographic data. Emphasis on variation of ethnographic data.
subject: ANTH

title: Public Archaeology
description: The legal, philosophical, and ethical foundations of archaeology in the United States. Considerations on relating archaeology to the non-professional.
subject: ANTH

title: Research Practicum in Archaeology
description: Observation and participation in the on going management of archaeological resources.
subject: ANTH

title: Research Practicum in Conservation Archaeology
description: Observation and participation in the on going management of archaeological resources.
subject: ANTH

title: Seminar in Historical Archaeology
description: Advanced seminar on theoretical considerations and methodological approaches to the study of historical archaeological materials.
subject: ANTH

title: Language as Social Action
description: Examines language as a social, cultural, and political matrix. Topics include ideology, gender, race, power, agency, and resistance. Students will apply linguistic theories in their own analyses of everyday speech.
subject: ANTH

title: Introduction to Linguistic Anthropology
description: A comprehensive introduction to linguistic anthropology, its relationship(s) to sociolinguistics, discourse analysis, and conversation analysis. Contributions made to social theory and theories of language and discourse will be understood.
subject: ANTH
prereq: Prerequisites: LING 600.

title: Archaeological Laboratory Analysis
description: Methods and techniques necessary to operationalize and test archaeological hypotheses in a laboratory context.
subject: ANTH

title: Archaeological Research Design and Analysis
description: An overview of skills required to design and organize archaeological field and laboratory research.
subject: ANTH

title: Analysis of Conversation
description: Types of interactive organization found within conversation and the methods and procedures used by participants to achieve order.
subject: ANTH

title: Biocultural Adaptation
description: Approaches to human adaptation emphasizing the interaction of biology and culture. Studies of biocultural adaptation to environmental, social, and economic constraints. Research design and methodology in adaptation studies.
subject: ANTH

title: Bioarchaeology Principles
description: Methods and theories of application of physical anthropological data to archaeological problems.
subject: ANTH

title: Biological Anthropology Principles and Theory
description: Major theories and principles of biological anthropology.
subject: ANTH

title: Migration and Culture
description: Theories of migration; peopling of the earth; family structure and migration in different economic regimes and cultures; seasonal and cyclical patterns.
subject: ANTH

title: Gender and Culture
description: Different cultures’ ideas about gender and use of gender to organize social groups in a wide range of societies, including American subcultures.
subject: ANTH

title: Exploring Ethnohistory
description: Cross-cultural study of history. Includes theoretical perspectives and cases from the Americas, Europe, Africa, and Asia.
subject: ANTH

title: Seminar in Environmental Anthropology and Development
description: Findings of ecological and economic anthropology applied to problems of contemporary development. Emphasis on less developed countries.
subject: ANTH

title: Anthropology of Art
description: Anthropological examination of the art of small-scale societies with attention, where appropriate, to the art of more complex societies.
subject: ANTH

title: Cinema and Archaeology
description: Critical examination of films dealing with archaeological subjects.
subject: ANTH

title: Ethnography of Communication
description: Ethnographic analysis of communication in groups and institutions in different cultures.
subject: ANTH

title: Human Interaction
description: Introduction to basic research on how human beings interact with each other and an historically constituted material world.
subject: ANTH

title: Language Ideology: The Political Economy of Language Beliefs and Practices

title: Introduction to Nurse Anesthesia Practice

subject: ASNR

description: This course is designed to further explore the Doctor of Nurse Anesthesia Practice degree to include the roles of the CRNA in relation to the patient, the profession, and the health care system.
subject: ASNR

description: Fundamentals in research and statistics with emphasis on statistics, research methods, and evaluation of current anesthesia research, to prepare the learner for topics relevant to the Doctorate in Nurse Anesthesia.
subject: ASNR

description: This course provides instruction and practice in the techniques necessary to assess health and to evaluate symptoms to identify disease.
subject: ASNR

title: Leadership in Nurse Anesthesia Practice
description: Leadership in nurse anesthesia practice and complex health organizations.
subject: ASNR

title: Health Policy, Ethics, and Legal Concepts for the DNAP Nurse Anesthetist
description: Historical and current concepts in health policy, ethics, and legal concepts for the DNAP Nurse Anesthetist.
subject: ASNR

title: Health Systems for the Nurse Anesthetist
description: An overview of varied health system theories, emphasizing the role of the nurse anesthetist in contributing to patient care within these systems.
subject: ASNR

title: Health Care Informatics for the Nurse Anesthetist
description: Student will explore health informatics and health information management principles – particularly as applicable to the nurse anesthetist’s role in the healthcare system and to data management for research and practice improvement.
subject: ASNR

title: Clinical Topics in Nurse Anesthesia Practice I
description: Introduction to pain management concepts and the use of ultrasound guided techniques in treatment.
subject: ASNR

title: Basic Anesthesia Principles I
description: Application of basic anesthesia principles to the practice of nurse anesthesia.
subject: ASNR
prereq: Prerequisites: ASNR 700.

subject: ASNR
prereq: Prerequisites: ASNR 761.

description: This course covers advanced concepts in nurse anesthesia practice.
subject: ASNR
prereq: Prerequisites: ASNR 762.

description: This course provides a thorough orientation to the clinical area and application of beginning anesthesia concepts to patients through simulation and precepted in-hospital assignments.
subject: ASNR

subject: ASNR
prereq: Prerequisites: ASNR 763.

title: Clinical Practicum I
description: The first of five clinical practicums focusing on general anesthesia.
subject: ASNR
prereq: Prerequisites: ASNR 771.

title: Clinical Practicum II
description: The second of five clinical field experiences including general and specialty anesthesia rotations.
subject: ASNR
prereq: Prerequisites: ASNR 773.

description: The third of five clinical field experiences including general and specialty anesthesia rotations.
subject: ASNR
prereq: Prerequisites: ASNR 775.

description: The fourth of five clinical field experiences including general and specialty anesthesia rotations.
subject: ASNR
prereq: Prerequisites: ASNR 777.

description: The fifth of five clinical field experiences including general and specialty anesthesia rotations.
subject: ASNR

subject: ASNR
prereq: Prerequisites: ASNR 700.

description: This course addresses the professional role responsibilities, challenges and issues for nurse anesthetists.
subject: ASNR

description: Application of physiology and pathophysiology to nurse anesthesia practice.
subject: ASNR
prereq: Prerequisites: ASNR 763 and PHPH 701.

title: Integration of Concepts Across Nurse Anesthesia Practice
description: Review of concepts with the purpose of creating broad view connections of anesthesia principles.
subject: ASNR
prereq: Prerequisites: ASNR 761, ASNR 762, ASNR 763, ASNR 795, ASNR 798.

title: Specialty Focus Simulation I
description: Use of simulation to learn and practice various peripheral nerve blocks and airway block placement with ultrasound and nerve stimulator techniques. Preparatory work outside of simulation time is required.
subject: ASNR

title: Specialty Focus Simulation II
description: High fidelity simulation activities to practice response to critical events in nurse anesthesia practice. Preparatory work outside of simulation is required.
subject: ASNR

title: Evidence-Based Decisions and Nurse Anesthesia
description: Analysis of evidence-based practice needed to formulate recommendations for nurse anesthesia practice and implementation of these recommendations into practice settings.
subject: ASNR

title: Clinical Topics in Nurse Anesthesia Practice II
description: Exploring the contributors to patient safety – including systems and human factors – and how to safely manage critical events in the perioperative period.
subject: ASNR

description: Students will present for peer review and critique their completed doctoral project. Presentation of project will meet the requirement for dissemination of their terminal project.
subject: ASNR
prereq: Prerequisites: ASNR 772 and ASNR 899.

title: Professional Role of the DNAP Nurse Anesthetist II

subject: ASNR

title: DNAP Project I
description: DNAP project Preparation for Nurse Anesthesia students.
subject: ASNR

title: DNAP Project II
description: DNAP Project Preparation for Nurse Anesthesia students.
subject: ASNR

title: Introduction to Astronomy
description: An introduction to the solar system and universe accomplished with interactive lectures, demonstrations, and laboratory experience. Designed primarily for the non-science major.
subject: ASTR

title: Astrobiology: Quest For Alien Life
description: Life beyond earth, search for habitable planets, search for extraterrestrial intelligence.
subject: ASTR

title: Introduction to Astronomy II: The Dark Universe
description: Astronomical topics including stellar death, black holes, dark matter, dark energy and cosmology. Astronomical techniques and application of the scientific method in astronomy.
subject: ASTR
prereq: Prerequisites: ASTR 101 or SCHC 115.

title: Galactic Astrophysics
description: Galactic Astrophysics presents the physical processes for the formation, structure, and evolution of the Milky Way and the structure and properties of the interstellar medium.
subject: ASTR
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in ASTR 201 and PHYS 212.

title: Introduction to Radio Astronomy
description: Nature of the sun, planets; galactic and extragalactic sources at radio wavelengths; quasars; techniques, detectors, and telescopes.
subject: ASTR
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in ASTR 201; C or better in MATH 115 or equivalent; C or better in PHYS 202 or PHYS 212.

title: Introduction to Relativistic Astrophysics
description: Final states of stellar evolution; white dwarfs, neutron stars, black holes. Cosmology.
subject: ASTR
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in ASTR 201; C or better in MATH 115; C or better in PHYS 202 or PHYS 212.

title: Undergraduate Research
description: Introduction to and application of the methods of research. A written report on work accomplished is required at the end of each semester.
subject: ASTR

title: Observational Astronomy
description: Observational techniques, scientific method application, and astronomical data analysis taken from international telescopes.
subject: ASTR
prereq: Prerequisites: A minimum grade of C in CSCE 106 (Python) and ASTR 101 or ASTR 201; or graduate student standing.

title: Advanced Observational Astronomy
description: Development of a combination of observational techniques and facility at reduction of data. A maximum of eight hours per week of observation, data reduction, and consultation. Offered each semester by arrangement with the department.
subject: ASTR

title: Advanced Observational Astronomy
description: A continuation of ASTR 533 . Up to eight hours per week of observation, data reduction, and consultation.
subject: ASTR

title: Introduction to Astrophysics
description: The basics of observational techniques, structure and evolution of stars, interstellar medium and star formation, structure and properties of the Milky Way and nearby galaxies, and generation and transfer of radiation in astrophysical environments.
subject: ASTR
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in PHYS 307 or graduate student standing.

title: Topics in Astronomy
description: Readings and research on selected topics in astronomy. Course content varies and will be announced in the schedule of classes by title.
subject: ASTR

title: First Aid and CPR
description: Knowledge and skills necessary to meet the needs of situations when emergency care is critical. Includes American Red Cross CPR/AED and First Aid certification. Encompasses skills for adult, child, and infant CPR/AED, breathing emergencies, and first aid procedures for emergency situations.
subject: ATEP

title: First Aid and CPR Lab
description: Skill development to meet guidelines for certification. Skills include AED, adult, child, and infant CPR, breathing emergencies, and first aid.
subject: ATEP

title: Emergency Medical Response
