description: Advanced topics in microeconomics including general equilibrium theory and welfare economics.
subject: ECON
prereq: Prerequisites: ECON 786.

title: Game Theory
description: This course teaches the fundamentals of game theory and strategic interaction. Concepts of normal and extensive form games, Nash Equilibrium, and subgame perfect equilibrium are defined and used to show how outcomes are achieved between two parties.
subject: ECON

title: Topics in Microeconomics
description: Examination of new theories and applications in microeconomics. Analysis of advances in theory and the application of theory to new new problems of consumer behaviour, industrial organization, and public economics.
subject: ECON

title: Mathematical Economics
description: This course is to acquaint students with the mathematical methods currently being used in micro- and macroeconomic theory. Topics covered are the use in economics of point set topology, nonlinear programming, differential equations, calculus of variations, and control theory.
subject: ECON
prereq: Prerequisites: ECON 523.

title: History of Economic Thought
description: An analysis of the development of economic theory with special emphasis on the evolution of alternative methodologies.
subject: ECON

title: Welfare Economics
description: An endeavor to formulate propositions by which alternative economic situations open to society may be ranked on the scale of better or worse.
subject: ECON
prereq: Prerequisites: ECON 788.

title: Macroeconomic Theory I
description: The modern theories of income determination, inflation, unemployment, and interest rates. Theories of consumption, investment, government expenditure, and taxation are presented.
subject: ECON

title: Macroeconomic Theory II
description: Recent developments in macro-econometrics, dynamic models, and related topics.
subject: ECON
prereq: Prerequisites: ECON 785 or the equivalent.

title: Monetary Theory
description: Monetary theory, monetary policy, and monetary reform. Theory of central banking and monetary equilibrium, and related topics.
subject: ECON
prereq: Prerequisites: course in money and banking and intermediate economic theory.

title: Money and Banking
description: An in-depth study of the operation and economic significance of the monetary system (money, commercial banks, and the central bank) and monetary policy. Emphasis is on theory and empirical hypothesis testing as related to above topics.
subject: ECON

title: Econometrics and Regression I
description: A treatment of single equation estimating techniques for the simple linear model, various nonlinear models, and the general linear model.
subject: ECON
prereq: Prerequisites: ECON 792 or equivalent.

title: Econometrics and Regression II
description: Topics in generalized least squares, autocorrelation, distributed lag models, principal components, identification, and simultaneous estimating techniques.
subject: ECON
prereq: Prerequisites: ECON 831.

title: Computational Methods for Economists
description: Theory and application of computational methods used to solve and estimate economic models. Solutions to economic models using numerical techniques and high-performance computing. Estimation of empirical models using custom built functions and numerical optimization.
subject: ECON

title: Economic History
description: Examination and interpretation of the record of events and trends in history usingthe concepts and tools of economic theory. Analysis of the effect of economic events on the course of world history.
subject: ECON

title: Environmental Economics
description: Fundamentals of environmental and resource economics. Including concepts of pollution, instrument choice, management of renewable and non-renewable resources, and valuation techniques for environmental public goods.
subject: ECON

title: Health Economics
description: A critical introduction to the application of economic analysis to problems in the health care field. Selective surveys of the related scientific literature will be covered.
subject: ECON

title: International Trade Theory
description: Theory of international values, comparative advantage, and the gains from trade; theory of commercial policy, tariff structure, and welfare and trade.
subject: ECON
prereq: Prerequisites: ECON 621.

title: Industrial Organization
description: Examination of industrial pricing, output, and investment practices in relation to the theory of the firm. Consideration of public aspects of these practices.
subject: ECON

title: Labor Economics I
description: Topics studied include wage theory; the processes of wage determination; the impact of unions and collective bargaining on wage levels, prices, employment, and income distribution; bargaining theory and union-management relations; the union as an economic institution.
subject: ECON

title: Labor Economics II
description: A descriptive and analytic study of manpower as an economic resource in the United States.
subject: ECON

title: Economics of Education
description: A survey of the contribution of economics to educational issues, including the value of educational investments, effect of education on economic growth, input-output analysis, and economics of educational finance.
subject: ECON

title: Public Finance I
description: A general survey of public finance, including public expenditures; the structure, incidence, and effects of taxes; public goods; and fiscal federalism.
subject: ECON

title: Public Finance II
description: An analysis of budgets and budget policy; tax incidence and effects; debt and debt management.
subject: ECON

title: Urban Economics
description: An analysis of economic forces affecting urbanization and the economic processes influencing urban form and structure. Spatial concepts are considered in addition to the traditional microeconomic and macroeconomic concepts.
subject: ECON

title: Location Theory
description: Spatial and economic structures of regions and regional economic development. Topical review of partial and general equilibrium models of land use, regional economic growth; income determination at a regional level (regional accounts and input-output models); regional policy alternatives and their efficiency.
subject: ECON

title: Sampling Techniques
description: Statistical designs and techniques for survey investigations. Mathematical development of sampling systems; sampling units; sample size; estimation; costs; non-sampling problems. Methods of obtaining and reporting information.
subject: ECON
prereq: Prerequisites: ECON 692 or equivalent.

title: Third-Year Seminar 1
description: Research methods in Economics. The design and execution of a research paper in Economics. Preparation for writing a dissertation in Economics.
subject: ECON

title: Third-Year Seminar 2
description: Research methods in Economics. The design and execution of a research paper in Economics. Preparation for writing a dissertation in Economics.
subject: ECON

title: Advanced Topics in Management Science
description: Topics will be selected from: nonlinear programming, stochastic programming, integer programming, spectral analysis, decision theory, Markov processes, Box-Jenkins methods.
subject: ECON

title: Econometrics and Regression II
description: Topics in generalized least squares, autocorrelation, distributed lag models, principal components, identification, and simultaneous estimating techniques.
subject: ECON
prereq: Prerequisites: ECON 795.

title: Special Topics in Economics
description: Selected readings, research and trends in economics. May be repeated up to three times as content varies by title.
subject: ECON

title: School and Community Relationships
description: Development of constructive relationships between schools and the communities they serve. Emphasis on research findings in communication.
subject: EDAD

title: Introduction to Systems Analysis and Improvement Science
description: Theories and strategies for fostering systems analysis and improvement science through a continuous improvement approach for educational leaders within their contexts.
subject: EDAD

title: Practicum in School Administration
description: An internship in schools at the appropriate level for certification. Will include field experience seminars. Should be taken as last course in degree program.
subject: EDAD

title: Practicum in School Administration
description: An internship in schools at the appropriate level for certification. Will include field experience seminars. Should be taken as last course in degree program.
subject: EDAD

title: Practicum in School Administration
description: An internship in schools at the appropriate level for certification. Will include field experience seminars. Should be taken as last course in degree program.
subject: EDAD

title: Professional Negotiation in Elementary and Secondary Schools
description: An advanced course designed for administrators and prospective administrators. Content for the course ranges from theoretical bases for negotiation through application of specific skills in the negotiating process. Also includes a survey of legislative enactments in various selected states.
subject: EDAD

title: Practicum in Educational Administration
description: Open primarily to students seeking district-level administrative experiences.
subject: EDAD

title: Career Planning and Development
description: Introduction to the career planning and development process through an understanding of one’s own career journey. Overview of how counselors assist individuals in the career decision process.
subject: EDCE

title: Counseling Through a Multicultural Lens: Understanding Self and Others
description: Introduction to counseling aspects and counseling skills that focus on the understanding of self and others from a multicultural perspective.
subject: EDCE

title: Interpersonal Communication Skills
description: Introduction to effective interpersonal communication skills.
subject: EDCE

title: Introduction to the Counseling Profession
description: Overview of and introduction to areas of the counseling profession.
subject: EDCE

title: Love and Healthy Relationships
description: Introduction to knowledge necessary for fostering satisfying and healthy interpersonal relationships, with an emphasis on the application of skills for intimate partnerships.
subject: EDCE

title: Family Counseling
description: A comparative study of the major theories in the field of family counseling.
subject: EDCE

title: Educators in Industry
description: The course is designed to provide counselors, teachers, and administrators with increased awareness of a wide variety of work experiences.
subject: EDCE

title: Introduction to Counseling
description: Orientation to the profession of counseling including its historical, social, and cultural foundations. Declaration of the minor in counseling or admission to the Ed.S. in Counselor Education.
subject: EDCE

title: Wellness and Mental Health
description: An overview of the characteristics of optimal holistic wellness and human functioning. Practical application of theoretically and empirically supported wellness models and interventions to enhance social, emotional, mental, physical, and spiritual well-being.
subject: EDCE

title: Theory and Practice of College Mentoring
description: Emphasis on current professional approaches to college mentoring and development of leadership and mentoring skills. Students must participate in Minority Assistance Program. Upper division undergraduate standing or admission to a graduate program and consent of instructors.
subject: EDCE

title: Seminar in Counseling
description: A seminar focusing on current professional issues in counseling.
subject: EDCE

title: Communication Skills in Counseling
description: Human relations principles applied to the counseling interview.
subject: EDCE

title: Counseling Student Athletes
description: Issues facing student athletes regarding their personal and career development beyond athletics.
subject: EDCE

title: Cross-Cultural Counseling
description: Basic concepts and procedures related to cross-cultural counseling; relevant issues which constitute the core of counseling effectively from a cross-cultural perspective; the development of specific learning strategies through which counselor trainees acquire fundamental skills of cross-cultural counseling. This course number and title will be offered with a specific perspective each time, such as blacks, women, ethnic groups, etc.
subject: EDCE

title: Counseling Parents of Exceptional Children
description: Principles and techniques of counseling parents of exceptional children.
subject: EDCE

title: Counselor as Consultant
description: History, theories, and practices of consultation and counseling program coordination.
subject: EDCE
prereq: Prerequisites: EDCE 510.

title: Theory and Procedures of Group Counseling
description: A comparative study of major theories of group counseling and related research with emphasis on group interaction within a counseling setting. Laboratory applications expedite understanding of theory and practice.
subject: EDCE
prereq: Prerequisites: EDCE 721, EDCE 722.

title: Educational Measurement
description: The history of educational and psychological measurement. Consideration of concepts such as validity and reliability of educational and psychological measures, the rationale of the development and use of instruments for educational purposes.
subject: EDCE

title: Assessment in Counseling
description: Knowledge and application of assessment techniques and instruments utilized in school, career, individual, and family counseling.
subject: EDCE

title: Career Development
description: Career development principles and practices in school and other settings.
subject: EDCE

title: Critical Issues in School Counseling
description: Study of school counseling programs, including school counseling issues; program development, implementation, and evaluation; and current trends.
subject: EDCE

title: Counseling Through Play
description: Interpreting play and using play techniques to facilitate the counseling process.
subject: EDCE
prereq: Prerequisites: EDCE 510, EDCE 600.

title: Professional, Legal and Ethical Issues in Counseling
description: Ethical concerns and legal mandates and constraints related to the counseling profession including issues regarding education, supervision, research, and policy development.
subject: EDCE
prereq: Prerequisites: EDCE 510.

title: Advanced Family Counseling
description: Advanced study of children within the context of family counseling theories.
subject: EDCE
prereq: Prerequisites: EDCE 503 or equivalent.

title: Comprehensive Developmental School Counseling
description: Study of the elements of and practices in a comprehensive developmental school counseling program.
subject: EDCE
prereq: Prerequisites: EDCE 510.

title: Clinical Mental Health Counseling
description: Surveying required knowledge and sills for the clinical mental counselor.
subject: EDCE

title: Sexuality Counseling
description: An overview of a family systems approach to understanding and treating clinical issues related to human sexuality.
subject: EDCE
prereq: Prerequisites: EDCE 503, EDPY 705.

title: Leaders in Counselor Education
description: Survey and analysis of the works of prominent leaders in counselor education (i.e., behavioral, cognitive, existential approaches). Course content varies and will be announced in the schedule of classes by title.
subject: EDCE

title: Career Practicum
description: A supervised experience in an approved setting that develops and assesses the individual's facilitation skills in career development.
subject: EDCE
prereq: Prerequisites: EDCE 502, EDCE 600, EDCE 700, EDCE 706, and EDCE 707.

title: Theories of Counseling
description: An introduction to counseling theories and models.
subject: EDCE
prereq: Prerequisites: EDCE 600.

title: Techniques of Counseling
description: Emphasis on development of techniques for assisting clients’ personal, social, spiritual, and career development.
subject: EDCE
prereq: Prerequisites: EDCE 510, EDCE 700, EDCE 712, and EDCE 720.

title: Group Procedures in Counseling
description: Group process and dynamics applied to counseling in group settings.
subject: EDCE
prereq: Prerequisites: EDCE 510 and EDCE 720.

title: Counseling Supervision Theory
description: Comparative study of major approaches to counseling supervision and related research with emphasis on historical foundations of supervision, supervisee characteristics, and application of concepts and techniques to specific practice settings.
subject: EDCE

title: Techniques of Clinical Mental Health Counseling
description: Assisting clients with personal, social, spiritual and career development.
subject: EDCE
prereq: Prerequisites: EDCE 600 and EDCE 720.

title: Counseling and Spirituality
description: Current knowledge about counseling and spirituality, including theories of spiritual development, assessment of spirituality, and best practices interventions with children and families.
subject: EDCE

title: Special Topics in Counseling
description: Advanced study of children within the context of family counseling theories.
subject: EDCE
prereq: Prerequisites: EDCE 503 or equivalent.

title: Advanced Techniques in School Counseling
description: This course is designed to help students understand the connection between theory and practice and give them an opportunity to try out this new knowledge. This additional work in both theory and practice will help students to be better prepared for their practicum and internship experiences.
subject: EDCE
prereq: Prerequisites: EDCE 721.

title: Practicum in Human Development and Counseling
description: Supervised counseling experience in an approved institution or agency.
subject: EDCE

title: Elementary School Counseling Practicum
description: Supervised counseling experience in an approved elementary school setting. Approved elementary school counseling practicum application.
subject: EDCE

title: Marriage, Couples, and Family Counseling Practicum
description: Supervised counseling experience in an approved institution or agency. Full admission into a counselor program and program specifics as approved by faculty.
subject: EDCE

title: Practicum in Play Therapy
description: Supervised counseling experience in an approved setting focusing on play therapy, counseling through play and expressive arts therapy.
subject: EDCE
prereq: Prerequisites: EDCE 709; EDCE 809; EDPY 705; EDCE 810; EDCE 811.

title: Secondary School Counseling Practicum
description: Supervised counseling experience in an approved secondary school setting. Approved secondary school counseling practicum application.
subject: EDCE

title: Practicum in Clinical Mental Health Counseling
description: Supervised counseling experience in an approved mental health agency or practice. Full admission into the counselor education program and program specific courses as approved by program faculty.
subject: EDCE

title: Internship in Clinical Mental Health Counseling
description: Supervised counseling experience in an approved clinical mental health setting.
subject: EDCE
prereq: Prerequisites: EDCE 803.

title: Counseling Internship
description: Counseling experience will be gained in a work setting similar to that in which a counselor will eventually be employed. Internship application must be submitted early in the semester preceding enrollment.
subject: EDCE
prereq: Prerequisites: EDCE 802.

title: Elementary School Counseling Internship
description: Counseling experience in an elementary school setting.
subject: EDCE
prereq: Prerequisites: EDCE 802S and approved internship application.

title: Marriage, Couples and Family Counseling Internship
description: Counseling experience will be gained in a work setting similar to that in which a counselor will eventually be employed.
subject: EDCE
prereq: Prerequisites: EDCE 802F and approved internship application.

title: Secondary School Counseling Internship
description: Supervised counseling experience in an approved secondary school setting.
subject: EDCE
prereq: Prerequisites: EDCE 802E.

title: Advanced Career Development
description: Theories of career development and career decision making. Critique of career development programs in institutions and agencies. Students may repeat the course for up to a total of 3 credit hours.
subject: EDCE
prereq: Prerequisites: EDCE 707.

title: Advanced Counseling Through Play
description: Development of advanced theoretical and skill-based competencies in the practices of play therapy.
subject: EDCE
prereq: Prerequisites: EDCE 709, EDCE 802.

title: Theory and Practice of Play Therapy
description: Basic concepts and practices related to the history of play therapy, developmental issues and ethical considerations in working theory to the practices of play therapy.
subject: EDCE
prereq: Prerequisites: EDCE 510, EDCE 600.

title: Creative Arts in Counseling
description: An examination of the history, rationale, theories, research and techniques of using the creative arts in counseling. Particular attention will be given to the therapeutic values or visual and verbal arts.
subject: EDCE
prereq: Prerequisites: EDCE 510, EDCE 600.

title: Counseling Skills Assessment Lab
description: Emphasis on assessment of counseling skill development and application of theory to practice through supervised work with clients in a laboratory setting in preparation for field-based practicum.
subject: EDCE

title: Professional Issues in Counseling
description: Emphasis on ethical issues related to counseling practice, research, writing, and continuing education including assessment and development of professional writing skills.
subject: EDCE

title: Advanced Transcultural Counseling
description: Advanced principles and practices for transcultural counseling.
subject: EDCE
prereq: Prerequisites: EDCE 700.

title: Advanced Practicum
description: Doctoral level supervised counseling experience in field settings relevant to students professional goals.
subject: EDCE

title: Advanced Counseling Theory
description: Emphasis on formulation and evaluation of the theoretical basis for approaches to counseling including, study of historical and contemporary perspectives.
subject: EDCE
prereq: Prerequisites: EDCE 802.

title: Empirical Basis of Counseling
description: An analysis of the empirical basis of counseling practice and theory with attention to special problems related to counseling research.
subject: EDCE

prereq: Prerequisites: graduate course in adult learning or development.

title: History of Higher Education
description: Development of environments, institutions, and individuals relevant to higher education since the 17th century. Covers foundational history as relevant to contemporary administration, students, faculty, curricula, and policies at institutional, state, and federal levels.
subject: EDHE

title: Student Affairs in Higher Education
description: Objectives and philosophy of student affairs, organizations and administration of student affairs divisions, and current trends and issues.
subject: EDHE

title: The American College Student
description: Study of theories of college student development and learning and application of theories to enhance administrative practices in American higher education. Also examines the impact of the college environment on students.
subject: EDHE

title: The Ideas of American Higher Education
description: Analysis of competing ideas of higher education with the purpose of helping students construct consistent sets of beliefs about values in higher education as a guide to understanding administrative and academic decisions.
subject: EDHE
prereq: Prerequisites: EDHE 730.

title: The Community/Technical College
description: Introduction to historical and current events shaping two-year college missions, programs, clienteles, and services. Preparation to assume student services and instructional positions within two-year colleges.
subject: EDHE

title: Academic Advising in Higher Education
description: A comprehensive introduction to the field of academic advising with special emphasis on the topic Appreciative Advising.
subject: EDHE

title: Financial Aspects of Higher Education
description: Survey of principles and practices of financing higher education institutions, including revenue generation and asset allocation. The course reviews methods of budgeting and business processes utilized by colleges and universities.
subject: EDHE

title: Legal Aspects of Higher Education
description: Especially for faculty members and administrators in post-secondary institutions. Emphasis on techniques of legal research, constitutional provisions, statutory laws, court decisions, and regulations as they affect administration of higher education.
subject: EDHE

title: Principles of College Teaching
description: Designed for prospective teachers in institutions of higher education. Considers the practice of teaching from philosophical, empirical, conceptual, and practical vantage points to prepare instructors for a changing and diverse student population.
subject: EDHE

title: Equity and Diversity in Higher Education
description: Survey of major topics related to social justice, diversity and inclusion in post-secondary institutions.
subject: EDHE

title: Equity and Access in Higher Education
description: The legal, educational and public policy issues that affect access to higher education in America.
subject: EDHE

title: Seminar on Ethical Issues in Higher Education
description: Examination of contemporary ethical issues and problems confronted by higher education administrators.
subject: EDHE

title: Strategic Planning for Data-Informed Decision Making
description: Overview of strategic planning processes of higher education institutions.
subject: EDHE

title: Enrollment Management in Higher Education
description: Review of enrollment management strategies in higher education, including recruitment and retention. Recruitment strategies such as admissions marketing strategies, communications plans, branding, and forecasting are addressed, as well as effective institutional retention strategies.
subject: EDHE

title: Capstone for Higher Education Leadership
description: Culminating seminar focused upon the principles and competencies of higher education leadership.
subject: EDHE
prereq: Prerequisites: Students enrolling in EDHE 745 should have completed a majority of the required coursework for the MEd in Higher Education Leadership with a B or better prior to registering for this course at the approval of their advisor.

title: Program Design and Implementation
description: An analysis of the theories, processes, and issues underlying the design and implementation of programs for learners in a post-secondary or professional context.
subject: EDHE

title: Staff Development and Talent Management
description: Review of the history, concepts, current techniques, and issues in staff development and training. Examination and application of skills required by the training practitioner and learning specialist.
subject: EDHE

title: Independent Study
description: Independent Study Contract required.
subject: EDHE

title: Organization, Administration, and Governance of Higher Education
description: Application of organization and administrative theory to post-secondary institutions of education, with emphasis on policy implementation.
subject: EDHE

title: Internship in Higher Education and Student Affairs
description: Internship experience in higher education and student affairs offices. Students are placed in college, university, or agency administration offices under joint supervision of administrative personnel of these offices and faculty members. Prospectus must be submitted at least one month before start of the internship.
subject: EDHE

title: Special Topics in Higher Education
description: Selected topical problems in higher education for advanced graduate students interested in the administration of higher education or college teaching. Possible topics include, evaluation, accountability, management, the learning society, the financial crisis, coordination vs. autonomy. May be used on a program of study up to three times.
subject: EDHE

title: Contemporary Trends/Issues in Higher Education
description: Overview of the major trends and issues confronting American higher education.
subject: EDHE

title: Internship in College Teaching
description: Designed to provide opportunity for supervised teaching experience in UNIV 101 - The Student in the University. Students will teach a UNIV 101 section as an instructor with supervision by an experienced instructor. Students will also participate in regular seminar meetings related to college teaching.
subject: EDHE
prereq: Prerequisites: Students must have been accepted to serve as a University 101 instructor or Graduate Leader in order to enroll in this course.

title: Leadership in Higher Education
description: Leadership theory and practice as applied to programs, units, and institutions in higher education. Addresses leadership strategies, options, characteristics, traits, and styles.
subject: EDHE

title: Higher Education and Student Affairs Practicum I
description: Supervised experiences in different aspects of higher education and student affairs administration through work in various administrative offices at USC and other colleges.
subject: EDHE

title: Higher Education and Student Affairs Practicum II
description: Additional opportunities for supervised experiences in higher education and student affairs administration.
subject: EDHE
prereq: Prerequisites: EDHE 837.

title: Institutional Assessment in Higher Education
description: Concepts, models, and practice of institutional assessment. Student participation in an actual assessment project.
subject: EDHE

title: Comparative Higher Education
description: Introduces students to the study of higher education in other countries, including policy and governance, finance, student life, the professoriate and related issues in comparative perspective. Includes a study abroad component with additional fees.
subject: EDHE

title: Independent Study
description: Restricted to doctoral students. Independent Study Contract required.
subject: EDHE

title: Law and Policy Studies in Education
description: Policy issues affecting public and private educational institutions across the PK-20 continuum (pre-school through higher education).
subject: EDLP

title: The Teacher as Manager
description: To help teachers, principals, and other personnel solve school problems by identifying and applying selected management techniques.
subject: EDLP

title: Resources for Teaching and Learning
description: An introduction to educational technology, its increasing importance in the total school program, and its relationship to learning theories and communication.
subject: EDLP

title: The Effective Teacher
description: Use of theory and research to understand and improve classroom teaching. Emphasis on teacher reflection and decision-making. The administrative role in enhancing effectiveness is highlighted.
subject: EDLP

title: Introduction to Education Leadership
description: A survey of basic principles of school leadership, the conceptual and structural organization of public education, and an introduction to leading educational improvement.
subject: EDLP

title: School Leadership
description: A study of interpersonal relations and communication within an educational organization and between the school and the community.
subject: EDLP

title: School Personnel Administration
description: Personnel management in the public schools with attention to such issues as teacher supply, recruitment, selection, staff development, supervision, teacher welfare, legal rights/liabilities of school personnel.
subject: EDLP

title: Supervision of Instruction
description: An introduction to the functioning of an educational supervisor. Emphasis on the improvement of instruction and instructional programs.
subject: EDLP

title: School Finance and Business Management
description: Financial and business management functions of school administration. Local/state/national funding issues, economics and politics of school finance, budget preparation, accounting/auditing/plant operation/maintenance from school level.
subject: EDLP
prereq: Prerequisites: At least two of the following EDLP 700, EDLP 701, EDLP 702, EDLP 703.

title: Legal Basis of Educational Organization and Administration
description: Emphasis on techniques of legal research, the legal relationships between the federal and state government as they relate to school district organization and administration, as well as legal case studies in all major areas of administrative concern.
subject: EDLP
prereq: Prerequisites: At least two of the following EDLP 700, EDLP 701, EDLP 702, EDLP 703.

title: The Principalship
description: Principles, problems, competencies, and practices involved in the administration of schools.
subject: EDLP
prereq: Prerequisites: Completion 15 hours of EDLP courses prior to enrolling.

title: The Elementary School Principal in Practice I
description: One of two courses in a required two-semester internship in the elementary schools.
subject: EDLP

title: The Elementary School Principal in Practice II
description: One of two courses in a required two-semester internship in the elementary schools.
subject: EDLP

title: The High School Principal in Practice I
description: One of two courses in a required two-semester internship in the high schools.
subject: EDLP

title: The High School Principal in Practice II
description: One of two courses in a required two-semester internship in the high schools.
subject: EDLP

title: Professional Leadership and Mentoring
description: The teacher Professional Leadership and Mentoring course focuses on coaching practices with strong evidence for improving teacher practice and student outcomes which include: observation, modeling, performance feedback, and relationship-building strategies.
subject: EDLP

title: Teacher Leader Practicum
description: The Teacher Leader Practicum provides the student with opportunities to develop specific teacher leadership and coaching dispositions, knowledge, and skills considered essential to successful leadership through field-based experience (practice).
subject: EDLP
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in EDLP 710.

title: PK-12 School Principal in Practice I
description: The first of two courses in a required two-semester internship in PK-12 school settings.
subject: EDLP
prereq: Prerequisites: Completion of 15 hours of approved EDLP courses.

title: PK-12 School Principal in Practice II
description: The second of two courses in a required two-semester internship in PK-12 school settings.
subject: EDLP
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in EDLP 712.

title: Leadership in Systems: Organizational and Institutional Theory
description: An exploration of how educational leadership is conceptualized and practiced in schools, districts, and community colleges/universities as organizations and societal institutions.
subject: EDLP

title: Inclusive Talent Management in Education– Cultivating a Work Climate for Inclusive Excellence
description: A course designed to empower leaders to cultivate a culture of belonging that promotes a positive work climate that results in improved employee engagement and retention.
subject: EDLP

title: Data Informed Decision Making
description: Using data to inform decision-making for education leaders and scholars.
subject: EDLP

title: Improvement Science and Action Research
description: Using action research strategies and an improving science framework for continuous improvement.
subject: EDLP

title: Introduction to Economics of Education
description: An introduction to the role of economics in evaluating education and education policy where the theory behind selected topics and the empirical evidence and methods used by economists will be discussed.
subject: EDLP

title: Ethical and Social Justice Leadership
description: An exploration of ethical and social justice perspectives in educational leadership, with attention to developing a personal and professional code of ethics. The role of care, professional practice, and social justice in ethics, and in moral leadership across the P-20 continuum (K12 and Higher Education).
subject: EDLP

title: Anti-racist Leadership
description: An introduction to ideas supporting anti-racist educational leadership across the P-20 educational pipeline. Specific topics include contested definitions of racism, policy, praxis, and anti-racist research, scholarship and leadership.
subject: EDLP

title: Understanding the Self to promote Anti-Bias and Inclusive Leadership
description: A course designed to prompt students to self-reflect on who they are (including their assumptions and biases), so they can effectively lead an inclusive work environment.
subject: EDLP

title: Opportunities and Challenges Associated with Inclusive Leadership
description: This course examines how leaders can navigate the challenges associated with promoting inclusive leadership, and highlights the opportunities associated with such efforts to promote a sense of belonging for all in the workforce.
subject: EDLP

title: Organizational Change for Inclusive Excellence
description: A course designed to empower leaders to navigate and lead change to promote inclusive excellence.
subject: EDLP

title: Advanced School Law
description: A seminar designed to give teachers and school administrators an opportunity to explore key legal issues.
subject: EDLP
prereq: Prerequisites: EDLP 705.

title: Computer Management in Educational Institutions
description: Open to advanced graduate students of education. History of the management, movement, and application of techniques and processes for managing the modern educational institution, emphasizing computer technology.
subject: EDLP

title: Advanced Methods of Instructional Supervision
description: An analysis of leadership techniques necessary to produce instructional improvement in educational organizations and of the technical methodology that distinguishes instructional supervision from other positions of school leadership.
subject: EDLP
prereq: Prerequisites: EDLP 703 or equivalent and employment in a position requiring supervisory responsibilities.

title: Educational Finance
description: A study of principles of financing public education, analyses of revenue sources from all levels of government, existing plan of financing and possible alternatives for financing schools from district level.
subject: EDLP
prereq: Prerequisites: EDLP 704.

title: Educational Policy Analysis
description: An introduction to policy making in education with emphasis on the local and state levels of policy formation.
subject: EDLP
prereq: Prerequisites: EDLP 705.

title: The Superintendency
description: A two-semester course on the district superintendency.
subject: EDLP
prereq: Prerequisites: EDLP 706 and admission to EdS or PhD program.

title: The Superintendent in Practice I
description: The first of two courses in a required two-semester internship in the district superintendency.
subject: EDLP

title: The Superintendent in Practice II
description: The second of two courses in a required two-semester internship in the district superintendency.
subject: EDLP

title: School Building Planning
description: Study of the problems involved and the procedures utilized in a comprehensive approach to planning and constructing school plants, the personnel involved and the roles they play, and the problems related to the long-term financing of such facilities.
subject: EDLP
prereq: Prerequisites: Admission to EdS or PhD program.

title: Administrative Evaluation and Decision-Making
description: A study of the requirements, practices, problems, and opportunities of administrative evaluation of programs and personnel as required by state and federal educational legislation.
subject: EDLP
prereq: Prerequisites: Admission to EdS or PhD program.

title: Advanced Educational Finance
description: A study of funding schemes, the economics of financing, and construction and defense of a school district budget. Microcomputers are utilized.
subject: EDLP
prereq: Prerequisites: EDLP 704 and EDLP 754 and PhD candidate.

title: Advanced Educational Policy Analysis
description: Advanced study of policy making at the federal level. The class will include a mandatory week-long stay in Washington, D.C.
subject: EDLP
prereq: Prerequisites: EDLP 705 and EDLP 755 and PhD candidate.

title: Theories of Educational Leadership
description: Organization, leadership, motivation, and change theories as they apply to educational agencies and institutions.
subject: EDLP

title: Seminar in Selected Topics in Educational Administration
description: Selected topics in educational administration in either finance, administration, supervision, evaluation, policy, and financial planning/management.
subject: EDLP
prereq: Prerequisites: Admission to doctoral program.

title: Field Problems in Educational Administration: The Literature
description: This course is designed to help students identify a research literature that provides the context for their own dissertation research. Students will conduct a systematic review of this literature in order to refine their questions and methods for their dissertation research, and to build towards their own dissertation proposal and literature review chapter.
subject: EDLP

title: Field Problems in Educational Administration
description: Opportunity for in-depth study of selected field problems in educational administration, utilizing research and other techniques.
subject: EDLP

title: Accelerated Dissertation in Practice Writing Course
description: A course designed to accelerate the dissertation writing process for Education System Improvement EdD students through course readings, engaging in seminar style discussions, and writing activities.
subject: EDLP

title: Independent Study
description: Independent study form required for authorization.
subject: EDLP

title: Dissertation Preparation
description: Dissertation Preparation
subject: EDLP

title: Middle Level Teaching and Management
description: Overview of national trends in the middle school with an analysis of the relationship of early adolescent developmental characteristics to organization, curriculum, instruction, and teaching in the middle school level.
subject: EDML

title: Foundations in Reading
description: Reading foundations, curriculum, and assessments for teaching young adolescents across academic disciplines.
subject: EDML

title: Middle Level Content Area Reading and Writing
description: Examination of planning, implementation, and assessment of reading strategy instruction across disciplines and content areas in middle level classrooms. Restricted to: teacher candidates enrolled in the middle level degree program.
subject: EDML

title: Internship A in the Middle School
description: Application of effective teaching techniques and organization of instructional settings for middle school students.
subject: EDML
prereq: Prerequisites: admission to internship in middle level program.

title: Internship B in the Middle School
description: Application of effective teaching techniques and organization of instructional settings for middle school students.
subject: EDML
prereq: Prerequisites: B or better in EDML 498.

title: Methods and Materials for Teaching Science in the Middle Grades
description: A study of methods, techniques, and materials of instruction appropriate to science teaching in the middle school.
subject: EDML

title: Methods and Materials for Teaching Social Studies in the Middle School
description: A study of goals, content, methods, and materials of instruction in middle school social studies.
subject: EDML

title: Middle Level Literacy Assessment
description: Introduces literacy assessment for individual and small groups or middle level students.
subject: EDML

title: Methods and Materials for Teaching English/Language Arts in the Middle Grades
description: Introduces goals, content, and methods of teaching language arts at the middle level.
subject: EDML

title: Methods and Materials for Teaching Mathematics in the Middle Grades
description: A study of methods, techniques, and materials of instruction appropriate to mathematics teaching in the middle school.
subject: EDML

title: Middle School Internship Seminar
description: Inquiry into the issues that arise during internship B experiences including classroom management, adolescent development, legal/professional responsibilities, multicultural perspectives, and needs of exceptional children.
subject: EDML

title: Introduction to Child Growth and Development
description: Basic course designed to familiarize the prospective teacher with the patterns of social, emotional, physical, and intellectual growth of the individual. Development of these growth patterns from the prenatal stage to the onset of adolescence.
subject: EDPY

title: Introduction to Adolescent Growth and Development
description: Basic course designed to familiarize the prospective junior and senior high school teacher with the pattern of social, emotional, physical, and intellectual growth of the individual during his adolescent years. Recommendation of the advisor(s) required.
subject: EDPY

title: Introduction to Educational Psychology
description: Applications of the psychology of learning and development. Special attention to basic statistics and the behavior of the school child.
subject: EDPY

title: Child and Adolescent Development
description: Foundational concepts and patterns in physical, cognitive, social, emotional, and behavioral development across childhood and adolescence. Application of this developmental knowledge to learning situations suitable to various developmental stages.
subject: EDPY

title: Independent Study
description: Contract approved by instructor, advisor, and department chair is required for undergraduate students.
subject: EDPY

title: Learners and the Diversity of Learning
description: Overview of psychological theories and research as it applies to education, including theories of learning, child and adolescent development, cognitive processes, classroom practices, individual differences/student diversity, and motivation.
subject: EDPY

title: Practicum: Learners and the Diversity of Learning
description: Field experience integrated with course on lifespan development and learning with an emphasis on individual and group diversity.
subject: EDPY

title: Free-Choice Learning and Informal Learning Environments
description: Examines free-choice (or informal) learning and the characteristics of settings and activities outside of formal schooling that effectively promote learning and development.
subject: EDPY

title: The Field of Educational Psychology
description: Introduction to current issues in educational psychology. Topics include, but are not limited to: learning and teaching, cognition, developmental theories, the brain, information processing, motivation, individual differences, and the social contexts of learning.
subject: EDPY

title: Human Growth and Development
description: Overview of the contributions of the biological and social sciences to an understanding of the mental, emotional, social, and physical development from infancy through adulthood. Study of behavior problems.
subject: EDPY

title: Growth and Development: Childhood
description: Presentation of theories and principles of human development that are particularly relevant to teaching. Application of such theories and principles to learning situations suitable to various age and grade levels.
subject: EDPY

title: Growth and Development: Middle Childhood and Adolescence
description: A review of the literature concerning adolescence; nine years through teens. Emphasis on application to the educational setting.
subject: EDPY

title: Growth and Development: Adulthood
description: Designed to further understanding of the adult and his/her endeavors in the learning process. Emphasis will be on the major contributing factors (physiological, psychological, and sociological) that affect the adult, on the relevant research findings, and on implications for educators.
subject: EDPY

title: Basic Processes: Cognition
description: A study of the cognitive processes involved in complex learning, conceptualization, problem-solving, abstract reasoning, and other aspects of higher intellectual functioning as developed and used in the educational setting.
subject: EDPY

title: Learning and Instruction
description: A systematic survey of major traditional and contemporary learning theories and principles relevant to the design and development of classroom teaching and instruction.
subject: EDPY

title: Research Methods in Educational Psychology
description: Analysis of concepts and methodological approaches to research in the Educational Psychology field. Focus on critical reading and evaluation of published literature across a broad spectrum of areas.
subject: EDPY

title: Motivation and School Learning
description: Motivation and School Learning.
subject: EDPY

title: Contemporary Research in Human Development and Education
description: Issues in research on human development with applications to educational settings.
subject: EDPY

title: Educational Psychology
description: Advanced study of educational psychology with special emphasis on learning.
subject: EDPY
prereq: Prerequisites: EDPY 752 and EDRM 711.

title: Advanced Problems in Educational Psychology
description: Advanced problems in educational psychology as they apply to the public schools at all levels. Designed to meet the needs of candidates for graduate degrees.
subject: EDPY

title: Independent Study
description: Contract approved by instructor, advisor, and department chair is required for undergraduate students.
subject: EDRD

title: Elementary Literacy Instruction I
description: Examination and implementation of the key concepts, content, goals, and strategies in teaching reading and the language arts in the elementary school. Students will work with elementary children in a school setting to learn literacy concepts and instructional strategies. Admission to the B.A. program in elementary education, children’s literature course. Designated for undergraduate students seeking initial certification.
subject: EDRD

title: Reading Assessment
description: Seminar and supervised one-on-one field experience focused on reading assessment of and instruction for individual children and groups of children. Restricted to: Undergraduate students pursuing teacher certification.
subject: EDRD
prereq: Prerequisites: admission to the professional program.

title: Content Area Literacy PK-12
description: A survey of research and practice which facilitates students' literacy skills in the content areas. For K-12 content area teachers of art, dance, physical education, foreign language, music and theatre.
subject: EDRD

title: Teaching Reading to Adults I
description: Diagnostic and prescriptive modes of teaching reading to adults, based on the physical, psychological, intellectual, and social characteristics of the adult learner.
subject: EDRD

title: Teaching Reading to Adults II
description: Preparation of materials for teaching basic reading skills to adults and practicum experiences in teaching adults to read.
subject: EDRD
prereq: Prerequisites: EDCO 511 or EDRD 511.

title: Teaching of Reading in the Elementary School
description: Study of the various phases of reading in their relation to a modern program of education and the place of reading in the curriculum. Emphasis on modern practices in the classroom teaching of reading.
subject: EDRD

title: Reading in the Secondary School
description: The place of reading instruction in high schools, the programming of special services in reading instruction, methods of teaching basic and developmental reading skills, and case studies of programs. Demonstrations of tests and devices.
subject: EDRD

title: Foundations of Reading Instruction
description: An overview of reading and its curriculum implications: grades K-12 and adults. Emphasis is placed on current trends and issues and related methodologies.
subject: EDRD

title: Teaching Reading Through A Literature Emphasis
description: Integrating appropriate literature into traditional and alternative reading programs. Identifying appropriate literature for classroom use and recreational reading. Use of literature as a means of developing and reinforcing reading skills.
subject: EDRD

title: Introduction to Teaching Media Literacy
description: A survey of analysis of electronic and non-print media themes and messages aimed at youth, with special emphasis on design and implementation of curricula for enhancing children’s media literacy.
subject: EDRD

title: Multimodal Multi-genre Writing
description: An active and reflective experience of process writing within multiple genres and multimodal literacies with classroom applications.
subject: EDRD

title: Psychological Foundations of Reading
description: Designed to familiarize students with research findings and theories in psychology as they relate specifically to the process of learning to read. Topics will range from perception, learning theory, and personality theory to the implications of cultural differences and language factors as they affect both the child’s learning to read and the teacher’s effective facilitation of this learning.
subject: EDRD
prereq: Prerequisites: EDRD 514, EDRD 716, EDRD 717.

title: Critical Foundations of Literacy
description: An overview of major theoretical, conceptual, and historical foundations in literacy and their curricular implications. Emphasis is placed on actively analyzing current trends and related methodologies.
subject: EDRD

title: Instructional Strategies for Reading
description: Demonstration and critical evaluation of teaching strategies and materials in reading.
subject: EDRD

title: Foundations of Reading Assessment
description: Seminar and supervised one-on-one field experience focusing on assessing and meeting the needs of individual children as readers with emphasis on at-risk children.
subject: EDRD

title: Seminar in Classroom Reading Assessment
description: Seminar and supervised field experience focusing on assessing and meeting the needs of small groups of children as readers.
subject: EDRD
prereq: Prerequisites: EDRD 600, EDRD 715, and EDRD 716.

title: Developing and Guiding the Reading Program
description: Design, management, and evaluation of reading programs at the classroom, school, or district levels.
subject: EDRD
prereq: Prerequisites: EDRD 600 and EDRD 715.

title: Capstone Seminar in Language and Literacy
description: Synthesis, critique, and evaluation of current research and educational practice in language and literacy.
subject: EDRD
prereq: Prerequisites: EDRD 600, EDRD 715, EDRD 716, EDRD 718, and EDRD 719.

title: Teaching Reading and Writing in the Content Areas
description: A survey of the strategies and materials which facilitate students’ reading and writing skill in the content areas. For P-12 reading education and content area teachers.
subject: EDRD

title: Assessment and the Foundations of Reading/Writing
description: Overview of assessment theory and practice; the reading/writing processes and the curricular implications across content areas.
subject: EDRD

title: Teaching Reading and Writing in the Content Areas
description: Survey of the strategies and materials which facilitate students’ reading and writing skill in the content areas.
subject: EDRD

title: Literacy Curriculum Development
description: Classroom, school, and district literacy curriculum will be explored through multicultural and global literature.
subject: EDRD

title: Literacy Research and Inquiry
description: Research and methods for literacy instruction with a focus on inquiry-based teaching and learning.
subject: EDRD

title: Coaching within Classrooms: Improving Teaching and Literacy Instruction
description: Coaching principles and strategies related to improvements and innovations in classrooms and in literacy instruction. Emphasizes working with teachers in classrooms to bring about educational reform and improvements in teaching and literacy instruction.
subject: EDRD

title: Literacy Leadership and Supervision
description: Developing as a literacy leader within school and district contexts through: engagement in effective collaboration; design, management, and evaluation of professional learning; design, management, and evaluation of family and community outreach.
subject: EDRD

title: Linguistics for Classroom Teachers PreK-12
description: An introduction to the concepts of linguistics specifically for preK-12 educators. Topics include syntax, morphology, semantics, pragmatics, and first and second language acquisition theories.
subject: EDRD

title: ESOL Principles and Strategies for PreK-12 Classrooms
description: A survey course focused on English for Speakers of Other Languages (ESOL), including a focus on different theoretical principles and approaches within various learning context as they concern preK-12 learners.
subject: EDRD

title: Teaching Reading and Writing to ESOL Learners: Theory and Practice
description: This course surveys research on the mental processes and linguistic contexts involved in reading and writing in a second language. Pedagogical implications for elementary, secondary, and postsecondary learners are discussed.
subject: EDRD

title: Assessment for English Language Learners
description: Seminar and supervised one-on-one field experience focusing on accessing and meeting the needs of English learners including approaches to classroom-based assessments in ESL, bilingual education, and preschool-grade 12 classrooms.
subject: EDRD

title: Curriculum Design and Materials Development for English Language Learners
description: This course will engage students in the examination and creation of research and theory that support curriculum design and materials development for the ESOL classroom.
subject: EDRD

title: Literacy Education P-12
description: Impact of theories of teaching, learning, and texts on literacy instruction; social, historical, political, and cultural influences on literacy.
subject: EDRD

title: Critical Perspective on English/Language Arts
description: Issues of literacy from a variety of critical stances such as democratic values, gender roles, and multiculturalism.
subject: EDRD

title: Internship in the Supervision of Reading
description: Internship in diagnosis and instruction of disabled, corrective, and developing readers to include supervision of graduate students enrolled in practica in reading, parent training, and program administration. Limited to advanced graduate students. May be repeated one additional time in a different supervisory setting for a maximum of six hours.
subject: EDRD

title: Pedagogical Applications of Reader Response Theory
description: Research and theory, emphasizing the role of the reader’s response in the reading process.
subject: EDRD

title: Teaching and Administrating the College Reading Program
description: Emphasis on the acquiring of background and skills necessary for instruction in and administration of college-level reading programs located in post-high school institutions (technical schools, two-year colleges, four-year colleges, and universities).
subject: EDRD
prereq: Prerequisites: EDRD 514, EDRD 518.

title: Practicum in Teaching and Administering the College Reading Program
description: Refining of counseling, evaluation, research, instructional, and administrative skills needed by college reading personnel. Practical application of the background and skills taught in EDRD 805 .
subject: EDRD
prereq: Prerequisites: EDRD 514, EDRD 805.

title: Cultural Perspective on Psychological and Social Foundations of Literacy Learning
description: Perspectives from psychology and sociocultural theory as they relate to literacy learning and research in literacy learning.
subject: EDRD

title: Critique of Qualitative Research in Language and Literacy Education
description: A review and critique of qualitative perspectives on language and literacy research.
subject: EDRD

title: Seminar in Language and Literacy Education
description: Intensive study of a designated topic influencing theory and/or practice in language and literacy education.
subject: EDRD
prereq: Prerequisites: Admission to a doctoral program in education or related field.

title: Semiotics, Reading, Literacy and Learning
description: Peircean semiotics and the implications of such for language and literacy education.
subject: EDRD

title: Windows into the Reading Process
description: An exploration of the reading process and how readers construct meaning by relating their sociopsycholinguistic backgrounds to discourse, including analysis of reading miscues at several linguistic levels, an examination of assessment and instructional tools, and developing a comprehension centered reading program.
subject: EDRD

title: Language Acquisition in Society
description: Historical and contemporary language issues in society, language diversity and marginalization, theoretical frameworks and findings.
subject: EDRD

subject: EDRD

title: Introduction to Classroom Assessment

subject: EDRM

subject: EDRM

title: Introduction to Research in Education
description: Concepts and methods of conducting research in education. Admission to graduate standing.
subject: EDRM

subject: EDRM

subject: EDRM
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in EDRM 705 or another introduction to applied statistics course.

subject: EDRM

subject: EDRM
prereq: Prerequisites: EDRM 711.

subject: EDRM
prereq: Prerequisites: An initial course in or experience with quantitative research (example - EDRM 705 or EDRM 710) and qualitative research (example - EDFI 731).

title: Research and the Statistical Packages
description: Advanced use of available statistical packages in educational research. Content varies; topics and credit announced in advance. May be repeated for up to six hours of credit. May be repeated for up to 6 hours of credit.
subject: EDRM
prereq: Prerequisites: EDRM 710 and EDRM 711.

title: Educational Measurement

subject: EDRM

title: Constructing Cognitive Instruments

prereq: Prerequisites: EDRM 710 and EDRM 720 or equivalent.

subject: EDRM

subject: EDRM

prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in EDRM 700 and EDRM 710.

subject: EDRM

subject: EDRM

subject: EDTE

title: Advanced Field Study of Teaching
description: Students will conduct and report results of a field study of a selected instructional innovation as specified by an implementation plan developed in EDTE 780 .
subject: EDTE
prereq: Prerequisites: EDTE 780.

title: Teaching Internship (Health)
description: Application of effective teaching techniques and organization of instructional settings in health for K-12.
subject: EDTE
prereq: Prerequisites: acceptance to the Professional Program in Education.

title: Teaching Internship (Health)
description: Application of effective teaching techniques and organization of instructional settings in health for K-12.
subject: EDTE
prereq: Prerequisites: EDTE 782A.

title: Global Education
description: An examination of global issues, including the concepts of interdependence and empowerment, as organizing principles in the design of instruction for internationalizing the curriculum.
subject: EDTE

title: International Perspectives of Instruction
description: A Comparative examination of the educational and instructional processes of select nations in a variety of instructional areas.
subject: EDTE

title: Developing Integrated Curricula
description: Theoretical foundation of an integrated curriculum and implications for current practice.
subject: EDTE

title: Research in STEM Education
description: Critical exploration of current research and research methodological approaches in STEM education.
subject: EDTE

title: Principles of STEM (Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics) Integration
description: Exploration of pedagogical practices and methodological approaches for integrating instruction across STEM disciplines.
subject: EDTE

title: Special Topics in Teaching and Learning
description: Intensive study of a designated topic influencing theory and/or practice in teaching and learning education.
subject: EDTE

title: Principles of Engineering in STEM Education
description: An exploration of integrated engineering instructional practices and research into science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM) pedagogical practices in order to effectively evaluate, create, and reflect on STEM education resources.
subject: EDTE

title: Adolescent Literacy Learning in a Multimodal and Digital World
description: A critical evaluation and reflection on issues of adolescent literacy learning in an increasingly multimodal and digitally-driven world through an examination of its epistemological and theoretical foundations, as well as the questions, methods, and analyses used in empirical research.
subject: EDTE

title: African and Diaspora Literacies for the Study of Teacher Education Pedagogy and Practice
description: The study of teacher education pedagogy and practice focused on developing doctoral students’ abilities as future teacher educators to support classroom teachers’ (pre-kindergarten through grade 12) knowledge of African and African Diaspora literacies and the ability to broaden curriculum in preK-12 classrooms.
subject: EDTE

title: Genre Study: Academic Writing in Education
description: An overview of academic writing in Education as a genre. Emphasis on defining and critiquing the genre, and producing papers, articles, proposals and/or dissertations.
subject: EDTE
prereq: Prerequisites: Admission to a doctoral program in education.

title: Internship in Teaching
description: Placement in an educational agency, clinical experience, or teacher education setting to gain supervised experience in teacher education. This course is repeatable for up to 9 credit hours. Six (6) hours of required courses in the Ph.D. in Teaching and Learning program and approval of doctoral advisor and field supervisor.
subject: EDTE

title: Internship in Research in Teaching and Learning
description: Placement in teaching and/or learning setting to gain supervised research experience. This course is repeatable for up to 9 credit hours. Six (6) hours of required courses in the Ph.D. in Teaching and Learning program and approval of doctoral advisor and field supervisor. Professional division contract approved by instructor, advisor and department chair is required for undergraduate students.
subject: EDTE

title: Advanced Critical Race Theory and Education
description: An advanced study of how Critical Race Theory is applied by researchers to investigate issues of racial justice. The study of race and racism is the primary lens of analysis in understanding disparities in educational, political, social and economic outcomes.
subject: EDTE
prereq: Prerequisites: EDFI 747.

title: Seminar in Instruction and Teacher Education
description: Major writers, issues, and research related to instruction and teacher education.
subject: EDTE
prereq: Prerequisites: advanced graduate standing.

title: USC Teaching Fellows Leadership Seminar
description: A resource for members of the Teaching Fellows Program to successfully complete yearly program requirements. Topics include: qualities of an effective teacher, how to give constructive classroom feedback, students' needs decision making, leadership styles, valuing diversity, and factors that influence/contribute to effective teacher leadership. Restricted to students enrolled in the USC Teaching Fellows Program. Instructor permission only.
subject: EDUC

title: Global and Multicultural Perspectives on Education in International Settings
description: Study abroad course in which students apply social science knowledge and analytical methods to understand the ways in which culture, society, politics, and global forces affect education and schooling in diverse international settings. May be repeated as content varies by destination.
subject: EDUC

title: Case Study in Classroom Management
description: Case study in the clinical application of pedagogy and methods related to classroom management, including relational, procedural and instructional aspects of a classroom management approach.
subject: EDUC

title: Field Problems in Education I
description: Selected educational problems will be identified and explored, and probable solutions developed. Emphasis will be on providing practicing school personnel an opportunity to work cooperatively, under supervision, toward solutions to those problems which are of immediate concern to them. Activities will include seminars, review of literature, observations, case studies, materials development, and other applicable approaches. Emphasis will be placed on the development of a comprehensive proposal designed to alleviate or solve the problems identified.
subject: EDUC

title: Field Problems in Education II
description: Selected educational problems will be identified and explored, and probable solutions developed. Emphasis will be on providing practicing school personnel an opportunity to work cooperatively, under supervision, toward solutions to those problems which are of immediate concern to them. Activities will include seminars, review of literature, observations, case studies, materials development, and other applicable approaches. Emphasis will be placed on the development of a comprehensive proposal designed to alleviate or solve the problems identified.
subject: EDUC

title: Field Problems in Education IV
description: Selected educational problems will be identified and explored, and probable solutions developed. Emphasis will be on providing practicing school personnel an opportunity to work cooperatively, under supervision, toward solutions to those problems which are of immediate concern to them. Activities will include seminars, review of literature, observations, case studies, materials development, and other applicable approaches. Emphasis will be placed on the development of a comprehensive proposal designed to alleviate or solve the problems identified.
subject: EDUC

title: Research Reflection on Educational Practices
description: Research and reflection on educational practices using evidence of skills and abilities documented through personalized learning experiences.
subject: EDUC

title: Responsive Inquiry into Teaching ‐ An Inquirer’s Stance
description: Professional inquiry focused on common problems of practice encountered in Pre‐K‐12 classrooms with an emphasis on effective pedagogy, relevant research, theoretical perspectives, current issues and dispositions regarding classroom environment, instruction, planning and professionalism.
subject: EDUC
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in EDTE 600.

title: Foundations of Teaching for Learning
description: Foundations of teaching for learning, including foundations of education, understanding learners and the diversity of contexts in which learning occurs, the development of assessments, and making instructional decisions based on assessment results.
subject: EDUC
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in EDTE 600.

title: Supervision of Student Teaching
description: The theoretical and functional foundations with which an early childhood, elementary, or secondary school teacher should be familiar when supervising a student teacher in a clinical situation.
subject: EDUC
prereq: Prerequisites: classroom experience.

title: Capstone Research Preparation
description: Preparation for developing and writing the capstone research project for the Ed.D. final degree requirement.
subject: EDUC

title: Electrical and Electronics Engineering
description: Introductions to: the profession of electrical engineering; the wide range of sub-disciplines that make electrical engineering so valuable in improving the human condition; the role of electrical engineers in society; and the role of electrical engineering students in the university.
subject: ELCT

title: Electrical Science
description: Fundamentals of electrical and electronic components. Basic network laws. Mathematical and computer tools for network analysis.
subject: ELCT

title: Introductory Electrical Engineering Laboratory
description: Laboratory procedures, instrumentation and measurements, report writing, computer use in system design, testing, and troubleshooting. Integrative project-based learning environment including passive, active, electronic and electromechanical systems.
subject: ELCT
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in ENGL 102 and C or better in CSCE 211.

title: Electrical Engineering for Non-Majors
description: Fundamentals of electrical engineering for mechanical, chemical, or other engineering disciplines, including electric circuits, measurements, data acquisition, sensors, motors, and controllers.
subject: ELCT
prereq: Prerequisites: MATH 142.

title: Circuits
description: Analysis of linear ac circuits using complex variables. Nodal and mesh analysis, Thevenin and Norton transformations, linearity, superposition, use of math solvers, circuit simulators, and computer-interfaced instrumentation.
subject: ELCT
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MATH 142; C or better in either ELCT 102 or AESP 265 or D or better in ELCT 220.

title: Signals and Systems
description: Analysis of continuous-time signals and systems in time and frequency domains, Fourier series and transforms, Laplace transforms; introduction to discrete-time signals.
subject: ELCT
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in ELCT 221 and in MATH 242.

title: Electronics Laboratory
description: Design and implementation of analog and digital electronic circuits, with emphasis on developing deep individual understanding of curriculum-spanning concepts.
subject: ELCT
prereq: Prerequisites: D or better in ELCT 201.

title: Real Time Systems Laboratory
description: Team-oriented application of sensing, measurement, and real time embedded digital control for autonomous vehicular systems. Requirements analysis, system modeling, software/hardware integration, report writing, development of teaming skills.
subject: ELCT
prereq: Prerequisites: D or better in ELCT 301 and ELCT 371.

title: Digital Signal Processing
description: An introduction to analysis, design and applications of discrete time systems; z- and discrete Fourier transforms; frequency and impulse responses, FIR and IIR filters.
subject: ELCT
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in ELCT 222.

title: Control Systems
description: Fundamentals of control systems. Analysis and design of control systems using physical system models. State variables, steady-state error, time- and frequency-responses, control system stability. Root locus analysis and controller design – PI, PD, PID, lead-lag compensator. Nyquist stability criterion.
subject: ELCT
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in ELCT 222.

title: Computer Modeling of Electrical Systems
description: Formulation of physics-based dynamic models of electrical or electromechanical systems. Solving dynamic equations of electrical systems in discrete time. Use of object oriented programming language (e.g., C++) and computer tools (e.g, MATLAB, virtual test bed) for solving dynamic equations of electrical systems.
subject: ELCT
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in ELCT 222, C or better in CSCE 145.

title: Electromagnetics
description: Basic concepts of electric and magnetic fields, including electrostatics, magnetostatics, and quasi-statics with computer applications.
subject: ELCT
prereq: Prerequisites: PHYS 212 and MATH 241.

title: Introduction to Microelectronics
description: Properties and characteristics of semiconductor materials, p-n and semiconductor-metal junctions. Basic properties, characteristics and operation of diodes and transistors.
subject: ELCT
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in CHEM 111 and PHYS 212.

title: Electronics
description: Introduction to design and analysis of electronic circuits and systems. Applications of amplifiers, op-amps, diodes, bipolar and field-effect transistors in analog and digital circuits.
subject: ELCT
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in ELCT 222.

title: Biometric Measurements in Healthcare and Wearables
description: Fundamentals of non-invasive probes for biometrics. Principles of ultrasound for echocardiography, optoelectronics for photoplethysmography, and electrochemistry for electrocardiography and electroencephalography. Introduction to amplification methods for biometric probe readout electronics. Studies of engineering tradeoffs between signal level versus adverse effects. Measurement basics of signals.
subject: ELCT
prereq: Prerequisites: D or better in MATH 141 or MATH 122; D or better in PHYS 201 or PHYS 211.

title: Capstone Design Project I
description: Planning, preliminary design, and prototyping. Analysis and specification of system and subsystem requirements, measures of performance, analysis of alternatives, effective team work. Project management and scheduling. Prototype implementation and characterization. This course should be taken during student’s penultimate semester.
subject: ELCT
prereq: Prerequisites: D or better in ELCT 302.

title: Capstone Design Project II
description: Continuation of Capstone Design Project I. Final design and implementation including design iteration, design for reliability, system integration and characterization, business case development.
subject: ELCT
prereq: Prerequisites: D or better in ELCT 403.

title: Fundamentals of Communication Systems
description: Introduction to communication systems, sampling theorem, modulation theory, multiplexing, phase-lock loops, and related topics.
subject: ELCT

title: Special Problems
description: Individual investigation or studies of special topics. A maximum of 3 credits total may be applied toward a degree. Advanced approval of project proposal by instructor and department advisor.
subject: ELCT

title: Photovoltaic Materials and Devices
description: Fundamentals of photovoltaic solar cell technologies. Design and operation of solar cells, including efficiency analysis and cost benefit. Applications to green and sustainable energy systems.
subject: ELCT
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in ELCT 363.

title: Introduction to Microwave Engineering
description: Introduction to plane electromagnetic wave propagation, reflection, transmission, antennas and antenna arrays, wireless link margin; noise, noise figure, noise temperature; switches and phase shifters; microwave modeling.
subject: ELCT
prereq: Prerequisites: D or better in ELCT 361 or PHYS 504.

title: Industrial Controls
description: The embedded electronics and software used in data acquisition, and process and instrument control in an industrial or manufacturing environment.
subject: ELCT
prereq: Prerequisites: D or better in ELCT 331, or departmental approval.

title: Digital Control Systems
description: Analysis and design of discrete-time control systems, implementation of control systems using digital electronic systems. Applications to electrical systems.
subject: ELCT
prereq: Prerequisites: ELCT 331.

title: System Health Management
description: Sensing, data acquisition, and data processing for evaluation of performance and system health. Integration and implementation of health management systems.
subject: ELCT
