description: Survey of states in East-central and Southeastern Europe. Problems of national identity, modernization, and small state politics. Impact of WWII, the Cold war, the fall of communism, and the return to pluralism.
subject: HIST

title: The Middle East in Modern Times
description: The impact of modern civilization upon the Middle East, including the history of the Arab, Turkish, Iranian, and Israeli segments of the Middle East during the 19th and 20th centuries.
subject: HIST

title: North Africa from Colonialism to Revolution: 1830-1962
description: A survey of French North Africa (Morocco, Algeria, and Tunisia) and Libya under colonial rule. The creation, development, and triumph of the nationalist movements, with particular attention to Algeria and its revolution.
subject: HIST

title: The Contemporary Middle East and North Africa
description: Political, social, and economic history of the Middle East and North Africa in the years since World War II.
subject: HIST

title: Saving Africa: Development and Humanitarianism in Historical Perspective
description: Exploration of how and why Africa is often represented as helpless, the colonial origins of common patterns of development and humanitarianism, and other possible models for these processes.
subject: HIST

title: Africa to 1800
description: Social, cultural, economic, and political developments, focusing on internally and externally generated changes.
subject: HIST

title: Africa since 1800
description: Commercial and religious revolutions of the 19th century, imposition and ending of formal colonial rule, and post-colonial issues.
subject: HIST

title: Problems in the History of Africa South of the Sahara
description: Independent readings and written papers on appropriate topics.
subject: HIST

title: Late Imperial China
description: Political, economic, social, and intellectual transformations of late imperial China from the Ming dynasty (1368-1644) through the last empire of China, the Qing dynasty (1644-1911).
subject: HIST

title: China Since 1949
description: Introduction to the major social, economic, and political changes in China from the Communist Revolution in 1949 to the present.
subject: HIST

title: Japan to 1800: Aristocrats and Warriors
description: The growth of the ancient state and the evolution of the samurai class and its political authority.
subject: HIST

title: Japan since 1800
description: The development of modern Japan: political evolution, industrial growth, social change, war, defeat, and occupation.
subject: HIST

title: Cold War Koreas
description: Survey of the history of the Korean peninsula since 1945 in the context of the Cold War.
subject: HIST

title: Into the Wild: Global Conservation since 1800
description: Global and comparative environmental-historical investigation of the ecological, socioeconomic, and cultural significance of wilderness protection, nature conservation, national parks, and nature tourism; field excursions required.
subject: HIST

title: The Age of Alexander the Great
description: In-depth study of the history, literature and culture of the Greek world era of Alexander the Great.
subject: HIST

title: The Fall of the Roman Empire
description: Historical, literary, and cultural study of the Roman Empire during its final years of dominance in the West.
subject: HIST

title: Science, Technology and Society
description: Historical, ethical and epistemic development of science and technology in its relationship to society.
subject: HIST

title: East Africa and the Indian Ocean World
description: East Africans’ contributions to an Indian ocean World that transcends single nation-states (stretching from Mozambique and Somalia to the Middle east, India and China) from the deep past to the present, including sections on “piracy,” Islam, slavery, race, and gender.
subject: HIST

description: Causes, consequences, and legacies of the largest mass displacement in modern history: the division of British India into independent states of India and Pakistan. Focuses upon British imperialism, South Asian nationalisms, religious identity, mass violence between Hindus, Muslims, and Sikhs, and how these events are remembered.
subject: HIST

title: The Caesars: Rome's First Family
description: Study of Rome’s imperial family from Julius Caesar until the death of Nero.
subject: HIST

title: History of Capitalism 1: Ancient and Medieval World
description: History of capitalist economic behavior and culture in various premodern societies: the Ancient Middle East, Classical Greece, the Roman Empire, early Islamic society, medieval Christian and Islamic states, the Mongol period and the era of global expansionism; evaluation of competing theories about premodern economic life and the meaning of capitalism
subject: HIST

title: History of Capitalism 2: From the Industrial Revolution to the Global Economy
description: A history of capitalism and its evolving definitions in Europe from the Middle Ages to the 20th Century, including its role in agriculture, mechanical industry, international trade, and colonialism and domination.
subject: HIST

title: History of Airpower

The emphasis is on the development of various theories about the application of aerial force, and how operations in time of war have confirmed or challenged these theories from a multinational perspective.
subject: HIST

title: History of Modern Sea Power
description: The evolution of sea power through the development of steam navies around the globe, 1860 CE - 2020 CE.
subject: HIST

title: History of Freedom
description: An examination of the concept of freedom from its ancient origins to modern debates over free speech.
subject: HIST

title: Nationalism: Myth and Reality

subject: HIST

title: Nazis and Fascists in European History, 1919-1945

subject: HIST

title: War and European Society, 1914-1945
description: Thematic examination of the nature and impact of total war on European society; emphasis on socio-economic, cultural, and military aspects.
subject: HIST

title: Business in Historical Perspective
description: Capitalism in the Western world; the rise of modern corporate enterprise in Europe and America since 1850.
subject: HIST

title: Urban Experience in Modern Europe
description: Social and cultural impact of urbanization in Europe since 1789 through a comparison of major cities such as London, Paris, Vienna, and Berlin.
subject: HIST

title: Women in Modern Europe
description: Survey of women in European history from the eighteenth to the twenty-first century. Focus on women’s citizenship beginning with Enlightenment idea of rights through developments in modern feminism.
subject: HIST

title: History of the Holocaust
description: Introduction to Nazi Germany’s systematic mass-murder of Europe’s Jews and other minorities during war. Examination of forces that led to the Holocaust, including scientific racism, Nazi policy implementation, and dynamics of annihilation during war.
subject: HIST

title: The Nobel Peace Prize: Peace, War, and Politics
description: Modern history through the lens of the Nobel Peace Prize. Limitations of the Nobel as encouragement to peace.
subject: HIST

title: History of Medicine: Antiquity to the Scientific Revolution

How Western cultures of the past approached health and illness; anatomy; nutrition; sexuality; disease and plague; mental and emotional health; and more. From ancient Greece, through medieval and early modern Islamic, Jewish, and Christian approaches to medicine and the body.
subject: HIST

title: Jewish History I: Late Antiquity to 1500

subject: HIST

title: Jewish History II: 1500 to the Present

subject: HIST

Consideration of the great crises that shaped the structure and form of Christianity during the last 20 centuries: the Hellenistic world; the medieval syntheses; the breakup of Western Christian unity; the transition to worldwide mission activity in the industrial age.
subject: HIST

title: Islamic Institutions and Traditions

subject: HIST

title: Messiahs, Mystics and Rebels in the Islamic World

subject: HIST

title: Engineering in History

subject: HIST

title: Information Technology: Past and Present

subject: HIST

title: Making Modern Science: The Physical Sciences

subject: HIST

title: Making Modern Science: The Life Sciences

subject: HIST

title: History of the Automobile

subject: HIST

title: Plagues and Societies in World History

subject: HIST

title: Evolution of Warfare I
description: A history of tactics, strategy, weapons, and logistics from 500 B.C. to A.D. 1400.
subject: HIST

title: Evolution of Warfare II
description: A history of tactics, strategy, weapons, and logistics from A.D. 1400 to the present.
subject: HIST

title: Sustainability in World History from Early Times to the Anthropocene
description: An interdisciplinary examination of sustainability around the world from social, environmental, technological, and economic perspectives from early times to the present.
subject: HIST

title: Independent Study
description: Contract approved by instructor, advisor, and department chair is required for undergraduate students.
subject: HIST

title: Urban America in the Modern Age, 19th Century-present
description: Survey of the urban history of the United States from the 19th Century through today.
subject: HIST

title: The Development of the American People to 1789

subject: HIST

title: The New Nation, 1789-1828

subject: HIST

title: The Sections and the Nation, 1828-1860

subject: HIST

title: Civil War and Reconstruction, 1860-1877

subject: HIST

title: The Rise of Industrial America, 1877-1917
description: A survey of recent United States history with emphasis on the economic, social, and literary developments from 1877 to 1917.
subject: HIST

title: The United States and a World at War, 1917-1945
description: The United States and a World at War, 1917-1945.
subject: HIST

title: United States History from World War II to the 1960's
description: A survey of the political, economic, social, and cultural developments in the period from World War II until the end of the 1960s era.
subject: HIST

title: The Making of Contemporary America: United States History, the 1960s to the Present
subject: HIST

title: History of South Carolina Since 1865
description: A survey of recent South Carolina history with emphasis on social and institutional development.
subject: HIST

title: History of Canada
description: A survey of Canadian development from colony to modern nation.
subject: HIST

title: Voices of America: U.S. History Through Biography
description: Historical exploration of the daily lives and personal stories of Americans through biographies and ethnographies.

subject: HIST

subject: HIST

subject: HIST

subject: HIST

title: European Intellectual History, 1815-1900
description: A reading course in art history, literature, and changing social thought in the 19th century.
subject: HIST

title: European Intellectual History, 1900-1960
description: A reading course in art, architecture, the cinema, literature, and social thought in the 20th century.
subject: HIST

title: France since 1815
description: Readings in the political, social, economic, and cultural history of modern France.
subject: HIST

title: Russia from Peter the Great to Nicholas I
description: The history of Russia from 1675-1855.
subject: HIST

title: Russia, 1855-1930
description: A reading course dealing with specific problems of modern Russian history.
subject: HIST

title: Empire and Nation in Modern Europe
subject: HIST

title: State and Society in Eastern Europe
description: Selected topics in the development of the area in the 19th and 20th centuries.
subject: HIST

title: Readings in Pre-Modern Chinese History
description: Selected topics in the history of China from the founding of the Han Dynasty in 202 B.C. to the end of the Ming Dynasty in A.D. 1644.
subject: HIST

title: China and the West, 1840-1949
description: A reading course on political, intellectual, and social changes in China resulting from the increased contacts with the West.
subject: HIST

title: Readings in the Social History of Sport
description: Reading and discussion of the critical and analytical literature on sport history.
subject: HIST

title: French Revolution and Napoleonic Era
description: Reading course in the historical literature of the revolutionary era, including the 18th-century background.
subject: HIST

title: Readings in Modern Japanese History
description: Topics include the Meiji Restoration, industrialization, nationhood and nationalism, World War II, and postwar changes.
subject: HIST

title: The Middle East and North Africa, 1798-1962
description: A reading course emphasizing political, intellectual, social, and religious movements in the Ottoman Empire and its successor states. Special attention to the growth of contacts between the Middle East and the West.
subject: HIST

title: The Coming of the Civil War, 1815-1860
description: A study of the various factors which produced a breakdown of the democratic process in the United States and produced a domestic war.
subject: HIST

title: African American Women in Nineteenth and Twentieth Centuries
description: This course will acquaint students with some of the secondary literature in African American women’s history from the late nineteenth century through the twentieth century. The course examines the impact of race, gender, and class on the lives of black women and explores the historical relationship between African American women, work, family, community, and politics.
subject: HIST

title: Capital City Field School: Theory and Practice of Historic Preservation
description: Introduction to theory and practice of historic preservation, taught in Columbia through on-campus classes, off-campus meetings with working professionals, and site visits around the Midlands.
subject: HIST

title: Southern Intellectual and Cultural History
description: A study of the Southern mind together with an investigation of such other aspects of Southern civilization as are clearly related to the mental life of the region.
subject: HIST

title: The New South
description: A survey of the economic, social, and political development of the Southern region since 1876.
subject: HIST

title: Victorian America
description: Readings in the social and political history of the United States in the period from Reconstruction to the First World War.
subject: HIST

title: History of American Women
description: Selected research topics on the cultural, social, economic, and political roles and contributions of American women.
subject: HIST

title: Latin American History
description: Readings in selected topics in Latin American history.
subject: HIST

title: Exploring Ethnohistory
description: Cross-cultural study of history. Includes theoretical perspectives and cases from the Americas, Europe, Africa, and Asia.
subject: HIST

title: History of Mexico
description: Readings in the political, economic and social history of Mexico.
subject: HIST

title: Atlantic World History, 15th to 19th Century
description: Analysis of the methodological, conceptual, and historiographical debates dealing with the social, political, and cultural process that linked the continents bordering the Atlantic Ocean from the 15th to the 19th century.
subject: HIST

title: Comparative History of Slavery in the Americas from the 15th to the 19th Century
description: Comparative approaches to the methodological, conceptual, and historiographical debates of slavery and the African Diaspora in the Americas, 15th to the 19th century.
subject: HIST

title: History of Brazil
description: Readings in the political, economic and social history of Brazil.
subject: HIST

title: Theory and Methodology of Religious History
description: Examines theoretical and methodological approaches to religious history.
subject: HIST

title: Global History of Capitalism: Approaches and Issues
description: Examination of how scholars conceptualize capitalism as a historical economic system, in comparative and connective global context; scrutinizing of explanations for the rise of global capitalism and development of its key features, from pre-modern to modern times.
subject: HIST

title: Readings in Modern Military Thought
description: Major military thought from the French Revolution to the present.
subject: HIST

title: History and Theory of Museums
description: Museums as central places for the creation, presentation, and representation of human knowledge and enhancement of civic ritual in modern states. U.S. museums considered in international context.
subject: HIST

title: Business History
description: Readings in the modern history of business in Europe and America.
subject: HIST

title: History and Theory
description: Examination of theory and case studies highlighting current themes in cultural history. Topics may include memory, ethnicity and race, gender and sexuality, popular culture, and truth and objectivity.
subject: HIST

title: Modern British Material Culture
description: Use of material culture by historians of modern Britain including the country house, food and drink, slums and suburbs, the seaside resort, and the public school.
subject: HIST

title: Comparative History of Time
description: Historical study of time-consciousness; how different modes of production have stimulated different forms of time-consciousness in American and other cultures.
subject: HIST

title: Comparative Applied History, U.S. and U.K.
description: Summer field school in the U.K. to provide comparisons with U.S. theory and practice in archives administration, museum management, and historic preservation.
subject: HIST

title: Material Culture Studies
description: Seminar in historical study of material culture; principal disciplinary and theoretical perspectives; emphasis on material culture of North America.
subject: HIST

title: Memory, History, and Space
description: A seminar in the historical study of buildings, the built environment, and cultural landscape.
subject: HIST

title: Historic Site Interpretation
description: An examination of the issues and problems in the interpretation of historic house museums and historic sites, with special emphasis on the development of an interpretive exhibit related to state and local history. Field trips.
subject: HIST

title: Archival Administration and Techniques
description: The nature, value, and use of public and private archives; the principles and techniques for preservation, arrangement, description, and reference service for archives, personal papers, and historical manuscripts.
subject: HIST

title: Historical Editing
description: An introduction to and a synopsis of the editorial process, including canons of selection and textual criticism; the editorial commitment; annotation; preparing manuscript for the printer; and the one-person editorial project.
subject: HIST

title: Historic Preservation
description: An examination of the preservation process, including the history of historic preservation, the development of preservation administrative systems, and preservation research methods and strategies. Field trips.
subject: HIST

title: State and Local History
description: An intensive inquiry into the source materials of South Carolina and the unique problems associated with state and local history.
subject: HIST

title: Research for Teaching
description: Course to familiarize M.A.T. students with the basic bibliographic aids and printed sources useful for the preparation of lectures.
subject: HIST

title: Special Topics: Study Travel in History
subject: HIST

title: European Historiography
description: A course whose purpose is to acquaint students with the development of European historiography, schools of historical thought and interpretation. This course or HIST 797 is required of all history graduate students.
subject: HIST

title: American Historiography
description: A course whose purpose is to acquaint students with the development of American historiography, schools of historical thought and interpretation. This course or HIST 796 is required of all history graduate students.
subject: HIST

title: Internship in History
description: The application of historical skills in a sponsoring historical or public agency.
subject: HIST

title: Thesis Preparation
description: For master's candidates.
subject: HIST

title: Topics in History Research
description: Writing seminar on selected historical subjects. May be repeated for credit as topics change.
subject: HIST

title: Research Seminar in Colonial American History
description: Restricted to M.A. and Ph.D. students in history.
subject: HIST

title: Research Seminar in American History, 1789-1876
description: Restricted to M.A. and Ph.D. students in history.
subject: HIST

title: Research Seminar in American History, 1876-Present
description: Restricted to M.A. and Ph.D. students in history.
subject: HIST

description: Restricted to M.A. and Ph.D. students in history.
subject: HIST
