description: Restricted to M.A. and Ph.D. students in history.
subject: HIST

title: Research Seminar in Early Modern European History
description: Restricted to M.A. and Ph.D. students in history.
subject: HIST

title: Research Seminar in Modern European History
description: Restricted to M.A. and Ph.D. students in history.
subject: HIST

title: Research Seminar in British History
description: Restricted to M.A. and Ph.D. students in history.
subject: HIST

title: Research Seminar in Latin American History
description: Restricted to M.A. and Ph.D. students in history.
subject: HIST

title: Research Seminar in East Asian History
description: Restricted to M.A. and Ph.D. students in history.
subject: HIST

title: Research Seminar in African History
description: Restricted to M.A. and Ph.D. students in history.
subject: HIST

title: Research Seminar in Middle Eastern and Islamic History
description: Restricted to M.A. and Ph.D. students in history.
subject: HIST

title: Dissertation Prospectus Seminar
description: Restricted to Ph.D. students in history.
subject: HIST

title: Planning Health Promotion Programs
description: Advanced skills in planning, implementing, and evaluating community health promotion programs to foster behavior changes in high-risk groups and enhance health outcomes. Examination and application of the PRECEDE-PROCEED Model, an evidence-based conceptual framework, to strategically plan targeted and focused public health programs.
subject: HPEB

title: Practicum in Health Promotion
description: Practical experience in applying health promotion principles in the community or organization.
subject: HPEB
prereq: Prerequisites: HPEB 300.

title: Personal and Community Health
description: Psychosocial health, stress management, leading infectious and noninfectious diseases, nutrition, physical fitness, sexuality, consumer health and health care access, environmental health, aging, and death.
subject: HPEB

title: Health Education for the Elementary School
description: Methods and materials for elementary schools. Integration and correlation of materials with school subjects. Sample content developed for primary, intermediate, and upper grades.
subject: HPEB

title: First Aid and Emergency Preparedness
description: Emphasis upon preparing school personnel to act responsibly in emergency situations. Includes the American National Red Cross standard and advanced first aid instruction.
subject: HPEB

title: Independent Study
description: Enrollment and topic to be approved in advance by advisor and instructor. Open to sophomores and above.
subject: HPEB

title: Health Education
description: Methods of teaching health in school and community settings; techniques and strategies.
subject: HPEB
prereq: Prerequisites: HPEB 221, HPEB 223, and HPEB 224.

title: Safety and Safety Education
description: Place of safety in modern life. Contributing factors to accidents, developing an awareness of the potential accident situation with special emphasis upon school setting, planning for and conduct of a safety education program.
subject: HPEB

title: Principles of Global Health
description: Examination of major global health topics and approaches used by governmental, non-governmental, international institutions and donor agencies to improve health in low and middle income countries. Critical analysis and generation of intervention strategies to combat health issues in various country settings.
subject: HPEB

title: Social Determinants of Health
description: The social, cultural, economic, and political factors that influence health and its distribution within and between populations. How society helps shape our health beliefs, behaviors, and status.
subject: HPEB

title: Food Systems
description: Multiple dimensions of food systems along multiple dimensions of theory and practice. Restricted to Junior or Senior level standing.
subject: HPEB

title: Food Systems Capstone Seminar
description: Synthesis and application of content and competencies of the minor in nutrition and food systems in a practical setting with emphasis on student identified areas for professional growth. Restricted to Senior level standing.
subject: HPEB

title: Special Topics in Health Promotion, Education, and Behavior
description: A study of special topics in health promotion, education, and behavior. Individual topics to be announced in master schedule by title.
subject: HPEB

title: Human Sexuality Education
description: Planning, implementation and evaluation of effective sexuality education programs. Includes strategies for educating about a variety of sexuality topics (e.g., reproductive biology, relationships, HIV/AIDS, sexual orientation, pregnancy, childbirth, and parenting).
subject: HPEB

title: Applied Aspects of Human Nutrition
description: Application of nutrition principles including functions of food and nutrients in health and disease prevention throughout the life cycle. Applied topics include weight management, food safety, and other contemporary issues.
subject: HPEB

title: Health Promotion in a Changing Society
subject: HPEB

title: Southern Discomfort: Public Health in the American South
description: Investigation of the unique health and disease profile of the American South, including regional disparities that remain unresolved despite a public health revolution. Topics range from endemic diseases of the antebellum period to the current HIV/AIDS crisis, and ethics of research.
subject: HPEB

title: Race, Ethnicity, and Health: Examining Health Inequalities
description: A comprehensive overview of race/ethnicity and health. Class discussions will focus on comparing health status and health outcomes of different racial/ethnic groups in the U.S. and discussing possible explanations for inequalities from a behavioral science perspective.
subject: HPEB

title: The Total School Health Program
description: A course designed to acquaint the student with the various facets of the modern school health program. Includes school responsibilities for health and safety instruction, school health services, school environmental health problems, school and community relationships, resources for health, and evaluation of programs.
subject: HPEB

title: Drug Prevention
description: Nature of drug actions, motivational factors that influence the use and abuse of drugs, and examination and evaluation of procedures to provide effective drug prevention efforts.
subject: HPEB

title: Tobacco Prevention and Control in Public Health
description: Examines policies and practices for tobacco prevention and control in public health.
subject: HPEB

title: Consumer Health in Contemporary Society
description: An analysis and appraisal of issues related to the production and distribution of products and services as these activities affect consumer health.
subject: HPEB

title: Behavioral Concepts and Processes for the Health Professional
description: The development of interpersonal skills in dealing with health clients in various settings.
subject: HPEB

title: Medical Anthropology: Fieldwork
description: Application of observation techniques, field notes, informant interviewing, and secondary data analysis to interpreting differential perceptions of health problem solving in the community and clinic.
subject: HPEB

title: Medical Anthropology
description: Socio-cultural factors in health, illness, healing, and in medical systems. Cross-cultural and ethnographic evidence for public health research and program applications.
subject: HPEB

title: Community Health
description: Identification and analysis of community health issues, their causes, and the roles of individuals, community agencies, and government in affecting their solutions. Emphasis upon personal involvement and responsibility for community health.
subject: HPEB

title: Managing Stress
description: Conceptualizing the nature of the stress; psychological, emotional, and spiritual aspects of stress; competency in the active management of stress and mobilizing support.
subject: HPEB

title: Cooking Up a Storm: Food, Globalization, Localization, and Health in the South
description: The role of food in defining our relationships to our family, community, nation, and world. How food underlies much of the political, economic, and social struggles throughout the world.
subject: HPEB

title: Implementation Science of Health Promotion Interventions
description: A study of health program and policy implementation processes and activities, illustrating how implementation can be the object of study in both general and intervention-specific research.
subject: HPEB

title: Nutrition Through the Life Cycle
description: Examination of nutritional concerns, requirements, and metabolism from pre-conception through the aging process; analysis of cultural, environmental, psychosocial, physical, and economic factors affecting nutritional status through the life cycle; and methods for assuring adequate nutrition through dietary selection, promotion of healthy eating throughout the life cycle and nutritional assessment for each state of the life cycle.
subject: HPEB

title: Maternal and Child Health
description: Public health issues, social and behavioral science, policies, programs, and services related to maternal and child health in the United States and other countries.
subject: HPEB

title: Lesbian, Gay, Bisexual and Transgender (LGBT) Health
description: Health status and concerns of lesbian, gay, bisexual, and transgender communities. Includes an examination of measurement issues and methodological considerations in research, as well as intervention efforts targeting LGBT populations.
subject: HPEB

title: Health Promotion for Elementary and Middle School Teachers
description: A multimedia course emphasizing health education strategies for major S.C. health problems, risk factors, and concepts of positive health behavior.
subject: HPEB

title: Behavioral Economics in Public Health
description: The ways behavioral economics can help achieve goals in public health and health care. How behavioral insights can be applied to reach promotion aims ranging from achieving weight loss to medication adherence to appointment attendance.
subject: HPEB

title: Nutrition Assessment and Counseling
description: Assessment of nutritional outcomes and work with research participants/patients. Methods for collection of dietary data, anthropometry, and body composition, including the use of new technologies. Nutrition counseling and interviewing techniques useful in gathering nutrition information.
subject: HPEB

title: Maternal and Child Nutrition
description: A survey of current concepts in clinical and public health nutrition which are unique to infants, children, and pregnant and lactating women.
subject: HPEB

title: Social Networks, Social Capital, and Health
description: Examination of health sciences and sociological research on social networks, social capital, and health. Theoretical and methodological foundations for network analysis of social relationships and health, design of public health interventions, and use of online social networks to promote health. Key constructs include social support, social capital, and social diffusion.
subject: HPEB

title: Addressing Childhood Obesity through Community Approaches
description: Approaches for prevention of childhood obesity, using perspectives from public health, social work, exercise science, pharmacy, medicine, and behavioral nutrition. Training to teach diet/physical activity lessons in elementary school settings.
subject: HPEB

title: Laboratory Techniques in Physiological Measurement
description: Practical laboratory skills and theoretical bases of measurements in human physiology; bioelectrical potentials, respiratory physiology, energy expenditure, body composition, temperature regulation, and biochemical assays.
subject: HPEB

title: Sexual Health
description: Overview of sexual health. Topics include sexual health across the life span, critical issues in sexual health, and sexual health interventions.
subject: HPEB

title: HIV/STI Prevention
description: The role of effective behavioral interventions in preventing the spread of the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) and other sexually transmitted infections (STI) among diverse populations.
subject: HPEB

title: Independent Study
description: Topics to be assigned and approved by advisor and department head.
subject: HPEB

title: Theoretical Foundations of Health Promotion
description: Role of theory in shaping research and practice in health promotion and education; historical and ongoing interaction between health education and the applied social sciences.
subject: HPEB

title: Planning Health Promotion Programs
description: Health promotion planning frameworks; issues in planning, implementing, and evaluating health promotion programs in a variety of settings.
subject: HPEB

title: Public Health Education Seminar
description: An examination of controversial and critical issues confronting the health educator, including ethical and professional concerns relating to the practice of health education in a variety of settings.
subject: HPEB

title: Health Education Research Seminar
description: Presentation and discussion of research topics in health education. May be repeated for up to a total of 3 hours.
subject: HPEB

title: Contemporary Concepts of Health and Health Education
description: This course will explore and analyze various concepts of health, disease, and illness as applicable to health education. Consideration will be given to alternative concepts of health and their implications for directions in health education.
subject: HPEB

title: Consultation in Health Systems
description: Consultation for role, program, and organization development in local and state health agencies, and in communities. Advanced practice in consulting roles aimed at system change.
subject: HPEB

title: Health Promotion Research Methods
description: Research methods applicable to the study of individual and group health behavior. Interfaces behavioral theory, research design and methods, and data analysis/interpretation. Introduction to evaluating health promotion research and evaluation.
subject: HPEB

title: Health Education Methods
description: Curricular planning and instructional strategies utilized in public health education settings.
subject: HPEB

title: Stress and Support Concepts and Management
description: Identifies environmental, organizational, interpersonal, and individual patterns of stress with particular reference to health professionals; competency in the active management of stress and mobilizing support in health settings and organizations is evaluated.
subject: HPEB

title: Evaluation of Health Promotion Programs
description: Planning and implementation of health education program evaluations. Emphasis on political, practical, and theoretical aspects of evaluation.
subject: HPEB

title: Applied Health Communication
description: Application of communication, media, and health behavior theory to the development of health communication messages and campaigns.
subject: HPEB

title: Changing Health Practices
description: Students plan and implement a class on changing a health practice such as exercise, diet, or smoking. Lectures and reading assignments on theoretical foundations of how to conduct classes in changing health practices.
subject: HPEB

title: Behavioral Aspects of Physical Activity
description: Psychosocial and behavioral factors in physical activity. Topics include mental health effects of exercise, behavior change theories applied to mental health effects of exercise, behavior change theories applied to physical activity, and physical activity determinants and interventions.
subject: HPEB

title: Qualitative Research Methods in Public Health
description: An overview of qualitative data collection and analysis methods commonly used in public health research with an emphasis on practical applications and hands-on experience.
subject: HPEB

title: Coordinating the School Health Program
description: For persons administratively responsible for the school health program and for individuals involved in discharging the various responsibilities. Characteristics of the Health Education Coordinator; areas of responsibility, policies and procedures relative to health education needs; curricular patterns; criteria for an extensive evaluation of selected school health programs.
subject: HPEB
prereq: Prerequisites: HPEB 521.

title: Health Education Curriculum Development
description: The basis and nature of the health curriculum, its development, and appraisal of state and national health education curricula.
subject: HPEB

title: Stigma and Public Health
description: Drivers, manifestations, measurement, and impacts of stigma on various disease conditions among diverse populations. Includes application of knowledge and skills to critically review interventions aiming to reduce stigma or mitigate the negative impacts of stigma.
subject: HPEB

title: Prevention of Teen Pregnancy
description: Design and implementation of community-based educational practices and services to prevent teen pregnancy.
subject: HPEB

title: Programs for Patient Education
description: Knowledge and skills for identifying needs, obtaining support, designing curricula, organizing resources, training personnel, implementing activities, and evaluating patient education programs are presented.
subject: HPEB

title: Health Promotion for Older Adults
description: Research and practice issues in health promotion with older adults, including the impact of ageism, ethnicity, gender, normal aging changes, self-management skills, and social networks on healthy aging.
subject: HPEB

title: Alcohol, Drugs, and Public Health Policy
description: Public health policy issues related to treatment, prevention, research, and education in the field of alcohol and drug abuse.
subject: HPEB

title: Community Health Development
description: Organizational development, policy influence, capacity building, empowerment, community diagnosis and coalition development for enhancing health.
subject: HPEB

title: Health Implications of Stress and Disease
description: Causative agents of chronic disease, with particular emphasis placed on those illnesses which have been termed psychosomatic and related to or caused by stress; physiological response of the individual to contemporary psychological stressors as well as methods of adaptation and prophylaxis.
subject: HPEB

title: Physical Activity and Health
description: An examination of physical activity/exercise habit patterns as they relate to health status. Emphasis on the chronic effects of exercise.
subject: HPEB

title: Nutrition and Public Health
description: A study of the relationship of human nutrition to public health and the potential for risk reduction through health education.
subject: HPEB
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in HPEB 502 or equivalent.

title: Obesity and Eating Disorders
description: The public health implications of obesity and eating disorders, considerations of causes, and intervention strategies.
subject: HPEB

title: Community-Based Physical Activity Intervention
description: Role of the physical activity specialist within the community health department. Development, initiation, and evaluation of campaigns, resources, community capacity building, and coalitions to promote physical activity.
subject: HPEB
prereq: Prerequisites: EXSC 700 or HPEB 700.

title: Health Education in Occupational Worksites
description: An overview of health education program models and strategies designed for workers in industry and business.
subject: HPEB

title: Interdisciplinary Perspectives on Child Abuse and Neglect
description: Current knowledge about child abuse and neglect, including typologies, etiology, effects, and current practice interventions.
subject: HPEB

title: Social Determinants of Health
description: Theories, measurement, and empirical evidence regarding structural factors and health. How social, economic, and environmental conditions can constrain and promote population and individual health.
subject: HPEB

title: Responding to Global Health Challenges
description: Selected global health challenges are used to examine sociopolitical, advocacy, policy, organizational, and programmatic actions by governmental, non-governmental, and international institutions aiming to improve health in resource-limited settings.
subject: HPEB

title: International Public Health Seminar
description: Various lecturers address the state of the art of public health strategies for third world countries aimed at the reduction of death and disease. Repeatable up to a maximum of 3 credit hours.
subject: HPEB

title: Health of Populations Affected by Humanitarian Crises
description: Current health issues and needs of populations affected by humanitarian crises, and the role of humanitarian interventions in responding to those needs.
subject: HPEB

title: Injury Prevention and Control
description: Etiology of injuries and strategies for their prevention are examined within an interdisciplinary framework.
subject: HPEB

title: Teaching Internship (Health)
description: Application of effective teaching techniques and organization of instructional settings in health for K-12.
subject: HPEB
prereq: Prerequisites: acceptance to the Professional Program in Education.

title: Teaching Internship (Health)
description: Application of effective teaching techniques and organization of instructional settings in health for K-12.
subject: HPEB
prereq: Prerequisites: HPEB 782A.

title: Selected Topics in Health Education
description: A study of selected issues confronted in health education programs.
subject: HPEB

title: Health Education Project
description: Performance of a predetermined work or service project in a health education setting.
subject: HPEB

title: Applied Practice Experience
description: The focus of this course is the synthesis and application of selected program learning objectives in the performance of an applied work or service project in a public need setting.
subject: HPEB

title: Public Health Practicum Seminar
description: Synthesis and application of MPH competencies for professional development, culminating in a practicum fieldwork contract.
subject: HPEB
prereq: Prerequisites: HPEB 700, HPEB 701, HPEB 702, HPEB 707, HPEB 710, HPEB 748.

title: Public Health Practicum Fieldwork
description: Performance of a limited work or service project in a public need setting, pursuit of planned, learning objectives related to previously identified aspects of the student's chosen role.
subject: HPEB

title: Implementation Science of Health Promotion Interventions
description: Implementation science aims to improve intervention effectiveness by studying the processes and activities around health program and policy implementation, incorporating knowledge and experiences of intervention implementers as well as of experts. Implementation science encompasses both general and intervention-specific research, centering implementation as the object of study.
subject: HPEB

title: Applied Measurement in Health Education Research
description: This doctoral seminar will examine conceptual and measurement issues regarding psychosocial constructs typically used in health education and health survey research--specifically health beliefs, health attitudes, health behaviors, health status, wellness, and perceptions of health care contexts.
subject: HPEB

title: Advanced Public Health Communication: Theory and Methods
description: Advanced conceptualization, development, and evaluation of communication strategies that aim to promote public health.
subject: HPEB
prereq: Prerequisites: HPEB 711 or equivalent course.

title: Theory-Driven Analysis
description: Intermediate-level overview of methods for answering theoretically-informed research questions using quantitative data. Designed for applied researchers who seek a better understanding of statistical methods from describing a population to testing a conceptual model. Practical guidance for organizing, writing about, and presenting results from basic and multivariate statistical analyses.
subject: HPEB
prereq: Prerequisites: BIOS 757 or an equivalent.

title: Advanced Evaluation of Health Promotion Programs
description: Evaluation methods including formative, retrospective and monitoring techniques; survey and trend analysis; application of experimental and quasi-experimental designs, triangulation, and cost-accounting.
subject: HPEB

title: Public Health Advocacy and Policy
description: Overview of the key concepts and practices in advocacy efforts to promote the health of human populations through policy, including theories and conceptual frameworks for understanding how social and political change happens. Emphasis on strategies for promoting public health policies, and methods for evaluating advocacy activities and policy impacts.
subject: HPEB

title: Social and Physical Environment Interventions in Health Promotion
description: This advanced course examines issues related to planning, implementing or evaluating health promotion interventions that target change in physical and social including policy environments.
subject: HPEB

title: Doctor of Public Health Practicum
description: Applied comprehensive fieldwork experience which facilitates the synthesis of knowledge, skills and application of DrPH core competencies and core content of the discipline. Course may be taken a maximum of 3 times. Enrollment restricted to DrPH students.
subject: HPEB

title: Dissertation Preparation
description: Prerequisite: one full year, 18 hrs, of graduate study beyond the master's level.
subject: HPEB

title: HRSM Professional Development Career Exploration Seminar
description: Exploration of career pathway planning essentials in the in the hospitality, tourism, retail, sport, and entertainment management industries.
subject: HRSM

title: HRSM Professional Development Seminar
description: Strategies, tactics, and requisite skills for career success in the hospitality, retail, sport, entertainment, and services management industries.
subject: HRSM
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in HRSM 101, passing grade in SPTE 195, or enrollment in the BAIS-Services Management major.

title: HRSM Advanced Professional Development Seminar
description: Advanced strategies, tactics, and requisite skills for career success in the hospitality, tourism, retail, sport and entertainment, and services management industries. Course must be taken prior to HRSM internship.
subject: HRSM
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in HRSM 201.

title: Services Management in Hospitality, Retail, Sport and Entertainment
description: Introduction to service management principles and practices across hospitality, retail, sport, and entertainment industries, emphasizing service design, quality management, customer experience optimization, and operational excellence in experience-based businesses.
subject: HRSM
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in HRSM 101.

title: Global Learning in Hospitality, Retail, and Sport Management
description: Instructor-Led Education Abroad Program involving two or more HRSM-related industries (Hospitality, Tourism, Sport, Live Entertainment, Retail, and Fashion Merchandising, Event Management). Topic and location varies. May be repeated once under a different topic.
subject: HRSM

title: Interdisciplinary Special Topics in HRSM
description: Current topics involving two or more HRSM-related industries (Hospitality, Tourism, Sport, Live Entertainment, Retail, and Fashion Merchandising, Event Management). Content varies by title. May be repeated once under a different topic.
subject: HRSM

title: Leadership Development in HRSM
description: Examination of effective techniques and practices of leadership development in the hospitality/tourism, retail and sport/entertainment industries.
subject: HRSM

title: Event Production, Evaluation, and Analysis
description: This course examines and analyzes the effectiveness and efficiency of events in Hospitality, Tourism, Sport, Entertainment and Fashion and Retail Management industries. Event production, evaluation, and analysis will be conducted. Detailed activities involved in event production, evaluation, and analysis will be discussed and implemented.
subject: HRSM
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in HRTM 364, and SPTE 203, and RETL 362.

title: Internship
description: The internship is a supervised work experience in the student’s major area of study.
subject: HRSM

title: Senior Seminar
description: Integration of prior academic work through the examination of problem inquiry, conduct of research, application of a solution development and the completion of a research paper.
subject: HRSM
prereq: Prerequisites: Senior status, and HRSM student (SPTE, HTMT, RETL, or BAIS).

title: Quantitative Methods in HRSM
description: This course introduces the basic knowledge of quantitative concepts, principles, and methods necessary for scientific investigation of research problems related to hospitality, retail, and sport management.
subject: HRSM

title: Global Seminar in Hospitality, Retail, Sport Technology Management
description: This is an interdisciplinary learning experience where students apply major specific constructs and current trends in hospitality, retail, sport and technology management in a study abroad context. Students will visit destinations and venues where they will interact with on site management. This course is for HRSM graduate students only and requires permission of the departmental graduate director. This course may be repeated twice for credit.
subject: HRSM

title: Business Research and Analytics in Hospitality, Retail and Sports Management
description: The course is structured to help students apply business research methods in the field of hospitality/tourism, retail, and sport management. This course will provide the student with an understanding of the process and the tools to support business problem identification, research design, information/data collection, data analytics, result visualization, and managerial decision-making of business cases.
subject: HRSM

title: Field Project in Hospitality, Retail, and Sport Management
description: Work experience and participation in management decision-making in a hospitality/tourism, retail, or sport/entertainment business environment. Positions assigned on an individual basis with emphasis on oral and written communication skills, planning, and problem solving.
subject: HRSM

title: Research Design in Hospitality, Retail, and Sport Management
description: The principles of research design, focusing on the application of these principles as they apply to sport and entertainment, hospitality, and/or tourism management.
subject: HRSM

title: Independent Study
description: Enrollment and topic to be approved in advance by advisor and instructor.
subject: HSPM

title: Health Economics
description: Designed to serve as an introduction to economic principles and applications used in the health sector. The role economics plays in various aspects of health care, demand-side and supply-side factors and issues, how various health care systems are impacted.
subject: HSPM

title: Special Topics in Health Services Policy and Management
description: Emerging issues in Health Services Policy and Management. May be repeated for a total of 6 credit hours as content varies by title.
subject: HSPM

title: Introduction to Health Care Management and Organization
description: Provide students with overview of health services management, management techniques and the different roles and functions of the different health care services. Use of field trips and guest speakers from different health care providers.
subject: HSPM

title: Fundamentals of Rural Health
description: Overview of the delivery and financing of health care in the rural U.S., with emphasis on vulnerable rural populations and access to care.
subject: HSPM

title: Comparative Health Systems and Health System Efficiency, Effectiveness, Sustainability and Equity
description: Comparative health systems of the world including health system organization, management, financing, resource use and health outcomes.
subject: HSPM

title: Issues in Health Care Information Management
description: An introduction to data management in healthcare institutions for undergraduate students and non-HSPM major graduate students. Topics include the nature of medical data, legal protections surrounding such information, and basis strategies for managing information technology resources.
subject: HSPM
prereq: Prerequisites: HSPM 500.

title: Introduction to Health Services Delivery and Policy
description: Overview of health services delivery in the United States, including organization and financing of health care, health insurance practices, primary and long-term care among other topics.
subject: HSPM

title: Finance in Health Administration
description: Introduction to health care finance. Course will teach reimbursement structures, regulatory mechanisms, cost control, and related factors unique to healthcare organizations.
subject: HSPM
prereq: Prerequisites: BADM 225.

title: Approaches and Concepts for Health Administration
description: An interdisciplinary perspective on the field of health administration. Philosophy concepts, and skills of implementation, management, and evaluation are presented and discussed. Principles in the practice of health administration are applied to identified problems and situations.
subject: HSPM

title: Maternal and Child Health Programs and Policies: Past, Present, and Future
description: Introduction to historical and contemporary health care programs and policy initiatives in the public and/or private sectors that help improve equity in health and health care appropriate to women, childhood, and adolescence.
subject: HSPM

title: Health and Economic Development: Interrelationships among health, poverty and economic progress
description: Linkages among economic development, poverty, inequality and health. Direct and indirect effects of health in economic development.
subject: HSPM

title: Ethical Issues in Health Care and Research
description: The ethical dimensions of decision-making in health care delivery, administration and epidemiologic research. Provides ethical foundations for discussion of topics in health-related research and practice.
subject: HSPM

title: Methods of Economic Evaluation of Health Projects, Policies and Programs
description: Concepts and principles of economic evaluation and applications of evaluation techniques (e.g., cost-effectiveness, cost-utility, cost-benefits, decision modeling) to the real-world issues and problems.
subject: HSPM

title: Perspectives in Rural Health
description: Analysis of issues and demographic, economic, and political forces affecting health care delivery systems in rural America. Examines structure of federal and state public health programs that impact rural health.
subject: HSPM

title: Introduction to the US Health Care System
description: Overview of the organization, financing, and delivery of health care services in the United States. Major topics covered include factors that have shaped the existing health care system, health care organizations and providers, public and private health insurance, access, cost, and quality of health care, health policy, and health reform efforts.
subject: HSPM

title: Health Policy
description: Introduction to the public policy process with an emphasis on policy formation, implementation, and impacts, as well as the role of various public and private stakeholders in the context of health care policies and programs at both the state and federal level; Overview of major models of policymaking; Examination of current health legislation.
subject: HSPM
prereq: Prerequisites: B or better in HSPM 710.

title: Health Economics
description: Introduction to the application of tools from theoretical and empirical economics to issues related to health, health care, and health policy. Major topics covered include demand for health and health care, supply of health care services, health insurance, externalities, technological change in the health care sector, and health policy.
subject: HSPM

title: Information Systems in Health Administration
description: Understanding and optimizing the use of health information systems and allied technologies including electronic medical records to improve healthcare organizations' performance in the areas of care delivery, operations management, quality, safety, and accessibility of healthcare services.
subject: HSPM

title: Introduction to Health Services Research
description: Overview of health services research as a discipline with an emphasis on foundational work in the field, as well as contemporary issues in health policy and practice. Introduces students to conducting systematic literature reviews and using conceptual models to develop hypothesis-driven research questions designed to address a knowledge gap.
subject: HSPM

title: Quantitative Methods for Health Administration
description: An introduction to quantitative methods and analytical techniques with application to health administration. The course includes the use of models and simulation for decision making and control in health administration.
subject: HSPM
prereq: Prerequisites: HSPM 775 or BIOS 700.

title: Health Planning
description: Strategy and tactics of state, regional, institutional health services planning. Special attention to the role of marketing.
subject: HSPM
prereq: Prerequisites: HSPM 733 or equivalent.

title: Sociology of Health for Health Services Managers I
description: Sociological approaches to the study of health and the decision to seek health care.
subject: HSPM

title: Sociology of Health for Health Services Managers II
description: Sociological approaches to the study of health care institutions.
subject: HSPM

title: Health Law
description: Legal basis for health care activities; health care provider laws, regulations, antitrust and organizational governance.
subject: HSPM

title: Human Resources Issues in Health Care Sector
description: A study of the current problems, theories, models, and strategics associated with managing human resources in the health care sector.
subject: HSPM

title: Applied Public Health Law for Administrators
description: Course addresses the significant legal and ethical issues in Public Health practice; basis for Public Health actions, authorities and limitations; role of the three branches of government in Public Health protection.
subject: HSPM

title: Advancing Public Health Policy
description: Advancing public health policy through design and implementation of a policy change campaign.
subject: HSPM
prereq: Prerequisites: PUBH 730.

title: Financing of Health Care
description: Application of the principles of financial management to the systems involved in the delivery of health care.
subject: HSPM
prereq: Prerequisites: BADM 660 or equivalent.

title: Health Care Finance I
description: Provides working knowledge of financial management techniques for managers in the health care sector.
subject: HSPM
prereq: Prerequisites: HSPM 733.

title: Health Care Finance II
description: Provides knowledge base and decision-making tools for financial management in health care organizations using financial management tools and principles.
subject: HSPM
prereq: Prerequisites: HSPM 731.

title: Health Care Management Accounting
description: Financial accounting and internal accounting for management decision-making, including cost determination, cost control, performance evaluation and financial planning.
subject: HSPM
prereq: Prerequisites: Undergraduate course in accounting or competency in accounting through personal study of an undergraduate accounting text.

title: Long-Term Care Administration
description: An overview of management and policy concepts and issues pertaining to long-term care facilities and programs.
subject: HSPM

title: Leadership in Health Care Organizations
description: Seminar on theory of and practice of leadership as a manager in the health care industry.
subject: HSPM

title: Health Services Administration I
description: Human Resources Management in health care and allied topics.
subject: HSPM
prereq: Prerequisites: HSPM 769.

title: Health Services Administration II
description: Operations management in health care, supply chain management and logistics processes, health services process improvement.
subject: HSPM
prereq: Prerequisites: HSPM 716.

title: Organizational Behavior
description: To explore organizational behavior at the micro level (individuals, motivation, leadership, conflict management) and macro level (social systems, inter-organizational relationships, change and innovation, performance and strategy, organizational design), with particular focus on health care environments.
subject: HSPM

title: Decision Making For Health Care Executives
description: Case study format where students identify problems, evaluate alternatives and make decisions using health care leaders in the community to prepare cases. Integration of principles learned in other health care theory and management courses.
subject: HSPM
prereq: Prerequisites: HSPM 732, HSPM 718, and HSPM 769.

title: International Health
description: Overview of international health status, demographics; communicable/noncommunicable diseases; health care needs, financing, and infrastructure delivery; and maternal and child health, family planning, and public health programs.
subject: HSPM

title: Quality Management in Healthcare
description: Systems approach to quality management focusing on Lean and other quality management methods that can be directly applied in a healthcare setting. Group projects in local acutecare settings allow students to practice quality management skills.
subject: HSPM
prereq: Prerequisites: HSPM 716, HSPM 731 or equivalent.

title: Managerial Epidemiology and Statistics in Healthcare
description: Principles and tools of epidemiology applied to decision-making in a health care environment. Knowledge and skills useful to health services managers related to statistics, population health management and assessment of medical care processes/outcomes are taught.
subject: HSPM

title: Physician Practice Management
description: Builds on prior MHA coursework on health care management to focus on specific knowledge and skills applicable to the management of physician practices.
subject: HSPM
prereq: Prerequisites: HSPM 731 and HSPM 769.

title: Healthcare Policy and Principles of Health Insurance
description: Demand, supply, employment-based coverage, government-sponsored programs and managed care.
subject: HSPM

title: Health Care Marketing
description: The principles of marketing applied to the health care setting.
subject: HSPM

title: Applied Practice Experience
description: Applied public health practice experience addressing management or policy need in public health setting.
subject: HSPM
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in all of the following courses: PUBH 725, PUBH 726, PUBH 730, PUBH 735, and 6 additional hours HSPM courses.

title: Selected Topics
description: Content varies by title. Course my be repeated for a total of 6 credit hours.
subject: HSPM

title: Integrative Learning Experience
description: Synthesis of foundational and HSPM MPH competencies in preparing a high quality grant proposal to address a public health problem.
subject: HSPM
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in all of the following courses: PUBH 725, PUBH 726, PUBH 730, PUBH 735, HSPM 712, HSPM 730, and HSPM 768.

title: Management Residency
description: On-site management project in a health care setting.
subject: HSPM

title: Public Health Residency
description: Practicum in approved public health, community health, or health care setting emphasizing evaluation and service delivery planning or a project such as resolution of a management problem or evaluation of a program component.
subject: HSPM
prereq: Prerequisites: EPID 700, BIOS 700 and three additional hours in the major.

title: Doctoral Seminar
description: This course uses a mixture of didactic presentations and seminar-style discussions to expose doctoral students to contemporary research in the field of health services policy and management and introduce various topics related to professional development. For advanced doctoral students, presenting at least once in this course will count towards satisfaction of the teaching requirement and is strongly encouraged.
subject: HSPM

title: Health Services Research Design
description: Introduction to the research process and the fundamental research designs commonly used in health services research, including experimental, quasi-experimental, and non-experimental approaches. Other topics include ethical issues in conducting research, the historical development of human subject protections, and the Institutional Review Board.
subject: HSPM

title: Fundamentals of Data Analysis
description: This course prepares doctoral students for more advanced analytic methods courses by providing them with a foundation in statistical programming software and a variety of mathematical, statistical, and epidemiological concepts including matrix algebra, differential calculus, statistical inference, correlation, univariate and bivariate analysis, sensitivity, specificity, direct and indirect standardization, etc.
subject: HSPM

title: Linear Regression Models
description: Introduction to ordinary least squares regression and related concepts including endogeneity, multicollinearity, heteroskedasticity, autocorrelation, and hypothesis testing. Application of concepts using statistical programming software.
subject: HSPM
prereq: Prerequisites: B or better in HSPM 810.

title: Panel Data Analysis
description: Overview of econometric methods for analyzing panel data using quasi-experimental designs including difference-in-differences, event studies, regression discontinuity designs, interrupted time series, and two-stage least squares/instrumental variables. Other topics include complex survey design, approaches to missing data, factor analysis, and specification tests. Application of concepts using statistical programming software.
subject: HSPM
prereq: Prerequisites: B or better in HSPM 811.

title: Categorical Data Analysis
description: Overview of regression models using maximum likelihood estimation and other non-linear approaches to analyze non-continuous dependent variables. Topics include logit, probit, ordered and multinomial models, count models, censored data, two-part models, and specification tests. Application of concepts using statistical programming software.
subject: HSPM
prereq: Prerequisites: B or better in HSPM 812.

title: Qualitative and Mixed Methods in Health Services Research
description: Overview of qualitative and mixed methods approaches to answering health services research questions. Topics include grounded theory, phenomenology, semi-structured interviews, focus groups, ethnography, observation, case studies, content analysis, transcription and coding. Strengths and weaknesses of different approaches are discussed.
subject: HSPM

title: Economic Evaluation and Policy Analysis of Health Services
description: The course foci is on theories and techniques used in conducting economic evaluations and policy analyses to ascertain the efficacy and effectiveness of public health and health care programs, services, and policies. Enrollment is restricted to DrPH or PhD students.
subject: HSPM
prereq: Prerequisites: HSPM 711, HSPM 712, HSPM 845 and HSPM 846.

title: Secondary Data Sources for Health Services Research
description: Overview of secondary data sources commonly applied in health services research, such as population and provider-based surveys, electronic health records, claims data, and administrative data sources. Application of data management concepts using statistical programming software.
subject: HSPM

title: Advanced Study in Health Policy Management I
description: Readings and discussion of topics relevant to research in health administration/health sciences.
subject: HSPM

title: Advanced Topics in Health Policy and Management II
description: Readings and discussion of’ major topics, including institutionally based issues, in research in health administration/health sciences.
subject: HSPM
prereq: Prerequisites: HSPM 845.

title: Doctor of Public Health Practicum
description: Students are required to conduct applied public health methods and strategies as a part of their practicum experience. Examples of practicum include, but not limited to development, implementation, and evaluation of public health or healthcare services, policies, organizational development, and regulatory activities.
subject: HSPM

title: Dissertation Preparation
description: One full year ,18 hrs, of graduate study beyond the master’s level.
subject: HSPM

title: Introduction to Hospitality Industry
description: History, growth, developments, and future opportunities in the hospitality industry.
subject: HTMT

title: Breads, Pastas and Sauces
description: Hands-on training in the basic foundations of breads, pastas, and sauces. Basic bread types, pasta shapes, pasta sauces, food safety, and new techniques to create personalized dishes.
subject: HTMT

title: Breakfast
description: Hands-on training in the basic foundations of meal preparation, simple breakfast staples, egg cookery, alternative breakfasts, basic food safety, and new techniques to create personalized dishes.
subject: HTMT

title: Cooking for Two
description: Hands-on training in the basic foundations of cooking complete dinners for two people. Includes effective purchasing, appetizers, complete meals, proper table set-up, desserts, and special meal ideas.
subject: HTMT

title: Desserts
description: Hands-on training in the basic foundations of meal preparation, staple dessert items, basic food safety, and new techniques to create personalized dishes.
subject: HTMT

title: Introduction to Healthy Mediterranean Cooking
description: Basic elements of the traditional Mediterranean diet, cooking techniques, and how to cook and eat to stay fit and healthy.
subject: HTMT

title: Introduction to Cooking
description: Hands-on training in the basic foundations of meal preparation, cooking basics, simple sauces, complete meals, staple dessert items, basic food safety, and new techniques to create personalized dishes.
subject: HTMT

title: Simply French
description: Hands-on training in the basic foundations of meal preparation, French cooking basics, simple sauces, complete meals, staple dessert items, basic food safety, and new techniques to create personalized dishes.
subject: HTMT

title: Simply Italian
description: Hands-on training in the basic foundations of classical Italian dishes, including sauteing, frying, and braising, basic food safety, and new techniques to create personalized dishes.
subject: HTMT

title: Tailgating 101
description: Hands-on training in the basic foundations of classic tailgating dishes, including grilling, frying, and braising, basic food safety, and new techniques to create personalized dishes.
subject: HTMT

title: ServSafe Sanitation
description: Food safety and sanitation in a commercial kitchen operation.
subject: HTMT

title: Special Topics in Culinary Arts
description: Special topics within the culinary discipline designed to give students a hands-on approach to learning special techniques, cooking styles and preparation, and practical application used in the foodservice industry. Content varies by title. May be repeated.
subject: HTMT

title: Cost Controls in the Hospitality Industry
description: This course is designed to give students a thorough understanding of the numerous ways hospitality managers can control costs. This does not mean just cutting costs but includes operational and budgetary policies which lead to intended results. Some of these planned procedures include forecasting, financial analysis, budgeting, storage, and purchasing.
subject: HTMT

title: Hospitality Management
description: Tools available to management and their utilization in the hospitality industry.
subject: HTMT

title: Hotel Management
description: Students will gain an overview of the hotel industry by exploring each of the major sectors of this industry including lodging, food beverage, and housekeeping.
subject: HTMT

title: Spa Management
description: Basic introduction to the spa segment with an overview on establishing a spa business, operations and management, business skills, and knowledge of future trends in the industry.
subject: HTMT

title: Introduction to Food Production Management
description: The basics of food production from storeroom to consumer. Various techniques of storage, preparation, merchandising, and menu-planning, as well as the many aspects of service. One lecture and three laboratory hours per week.
subject: HTMT

title: Introduction to Beverage Management
description: Introduction to Beverage Management, is an introductory course designed to give students a broad understanding of the beverage industry.
subject: HTMT

title: Foundations of Tourism
description: Basic introduction to the social science of tourism in the US and the world, including definitional issues, motivations for travel, factors influencing demand-side and supply-side growth, the tourism product, market segmentation and marketing, socioeconomic, and ecological impacts, and destination life cycle dynamics. May not be used to satisfy Carolina Core requirements for HTMT majors.
subject: HTMT

title: Club Management
description: Unique problems and issues associated with private club management.
subject: HTMT

title: Hospitality and Tourism Practicum
description: Supervised full-time work experience in an area of the hospitality and tourism industry, selected by the student and approved by the practicum coordinator. 400 hours required for 6 credit hours, 200 hours required for 3 credit hours.
subject: HTMT

title: Nutrition
description: The utilization of food by the body; menu planning and food production for institutions in relation to regular and modified diets.
subject: HTMT

title: Human Resource Management in Hospitality, Retail, and Sport Management
description: Recruitment, selection, utilization, and development of human resources; role of supervisors in management and personnel administration.
subject: HTMT

title: Beverage Marketing and Promotion
description: Study of marketing and sales practices within the overall beverage industry and impacts on consumer behaviors and consumption patterns.
subject: HTMT

title: Hospitality Loyalty Strategy
description: Overview of the background, principles, and design functions associated with loyalty strategy within the hospitality and tourism industry from both a theoretical and practical perspective.
subject: HTMT
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in HTMT 110 or HTMT 280.

title: Physical Plant Design
description: Design, equipment, and maintenance of hospitality facilities.
subject: HTMT

title: Hotel and Restaurant Law
description: A comprehensive overview of laws and regulatory agencies governing the lodging and food services industries.
subject: HTMT
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in SPTE 240.

title: Wedding Planning and Management
description: Sociocultural, political, economic, religious, and legal influences on wedding planning and business strategies will be explored as background to practices relevant to successful wedding planning and consultancy for diverse clients.
subject: HTMT

title: Conference and Meeting Planning
description: Planning and managing conferences and meetings in the tourism industry.
subject: HTMT

title: Restaurant Operations Management
description: Management techniques and operating problems in food service operations. One lecture and five laboratory hours per week.
subject: HTMT
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in HTMT 270.

title: Coffee, Tea, and Non-Alcoholic Beverages
description: Examination of coffee, tea, and other non-alcoholic beverages and how to build a successful non-alcoholic beverage program in a restaurant, bar or other hospitality operation.
subject: HTMT

title: Wine, Beverage, and Culture
description: This course provides a broad base of knowledge, covering all commercially relevant beverages including origins, tradition and culture.
subject: HTMT

title: Venue Food and Beverage Operations
description: Introduction to the principles of food and beverage in venue management and their operational applications.
subject: HTMT

title: Travel and Destination Management
description: Describes role of travel agencies, tour operators, tour guides, transportation providers, and attractions as critical sectors within the travel industry.
subject: HTMT

title: Travel and Tourism Law
description: This course focuses on legal issues affecting the tourism industry, including international travel law, travel litigation, liability, and topics specific to travel agencies, carriers, attractions, and destinations.
subject: HTMT

title: Ecotourism
description: Focuses on tourism that is nature-based and entails a learning component while being managed for environmental, economic, and sociocultural sustainability.
subject: HTMT

title: Tourism Festival Planning and Management
description: Planning, marketing, sponsorship, budgeting, management, impacts, and evaluation of successful and sustainable special tourism festivals are discussed from both a theoretical and practical perspective.
subject: HTMT

title: Cruise Ship Industry
description: Organization, market segmentation, marketing, design, anatomy of experience, environmental and social impacts, health and safety, and trends within cruising.
subject: HTMT

title: Resort Development and Management
description: Examines effective practices in the sustainable planning, development, and management of resorts and spas, including host community relations, social effects, design, marketing, operations, finance, and recreation programming.
subject: HTMT

title: International Tourism Field Experience
description: An experiential field trip where students evaluate selected tourism issues and products in an international destination.
subject: HTMT

title: Special Topics in HTMT
description: Concepts, issues, and trends in the hospitality and tourism industry.
subject: HTMT

title: Independent Study
description: Contract approved by instructor, advisor, and department head is required for undergraduate students.
subject: HTMT

title: HTMT Current Issues
description: Issues and problems concerning the hospitality industry.
subject: HTMT

title: Hospitality Financial Management
description: Financial decision making including ratio analysis, asset management, leverage, short, intermediate, and long-term financing in the hospitality industry.
subject: HTMT
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in RETL 262.

title: Sustainable Foodservice Systems
description: Factors affecting the food supply in the United States and world. The class will explore the economic, political, legal, and societal forces affecting the distribution system of our food supply.
subject: HTMT
prereq: Prerequisites: HTMT 228.

title: Services Management for Hospitality and Tourism
description: Management issues pertinent to quality service delivery in hospitality and tourism organizations.
subject: HTMT

title: Hospitality and Tourism Marketing
description: Application of marketing principles and promotional techniques to the hospitality and travel industry.
subject: HTMT

title: Hospitality Sales Management
description: Basic sales management policies and procedures within the hospitality industry with emphasis on sales planning, preparation, presentations and client contact within hospitality organizations.
subject: HTMT

title: Club Cuisine and Service
description: Advanced topics in the management of production and service techniques for private clubs.
subject: HTMT
prereq: Prerequisites: HTMT 270, HTMT 285.

title: Distilled Spirits for the Hospitality Industry
description: Study of production methods, styles, regions of distillation, standards of identity, sensory evaluation, and proper procedures for serving distilled spirits.
subject: HTMT
prereq: Prerequisites: Students must be 21 years or older to enroll in this course.

title: Wines and Spirits in Food Service Establishments
description: Management overview and operating problems of beverages in the hospitality industry.
subject: HTMT
prereq: Prerequisites: Students must be 21 years or older to enroll in this course.

title: Craft Beer
description: Study of craft beer through exploration of current trends, countries of origin, beer styles, flavor profiles, food flavor pairings and best business practices.
subject: HTMT
prereq: Prerequisites: Students must be 21 years or older to enroll in this course.

title: Commercial Real Estate Development for the HRSM Industries
description: Introduction to the specialized field of commercial real estate development for hospitality, tourism, retail, sport, and entertainment industries, covering the entire development process from site selection and market analysis to financing, design, construction, and asset management.
subject: HTMT
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in FINA 366.

title: Analytical Techniques in Tourism and Hospitality
description: Examination and application of analytical and research methods to tourism and hospitality problems.
subject: HTMT
prereq: Prerequisites: STAT 201 or equivalent.

title: Tourism Economics
description: Macro- and microeconomic dimensions of tourism are considered in relation to the demand and supply of tourism products at the national, state, regional, and local levels.
subject: HTMT
prereq: Prerequisites: ECON 224.

title: Sustainable Tourism
description: Principles and practices of environmental, economic, and sociocultural sustainability in tourism are described and analyzed.
subject: HTMT

title: Strategic Hospitality Management and Leadership
description: Students utilize knowledge gained from previous coursework combined with contemporary strategic management and leadership practices to identify, analyze, and solve problems in the highly competitive and dynamic hospitality business environment.
subject: HTMT
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MGMT 371.

title: Hospitality and Tourism Internship
description: Structured industry practical experience in a hospitality or tourism company. 400 hours required.
subject: HTMT
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in HTMT 344; C or better in HTMT 110 or HTMT 280.

title: Hospitality Human Capital and Talent Management
description: This course will help students learn and apply concepts comprising talent management, including the role that talent management plays in the strategic management of hospitality and tourism operations, diversity, recruitment, selection, training and development of talent within the organization, as well as performance management for hospitality supervisors and management.
subject: HTMT

title: Revenue Management in the Hospitality Industry
description: Examination of revenue management in the hospitality industry with an emphasis on the theory and dynamics of revenue management, the implementation of capacity management, forecasting and discounting.
subject: HTMT
prereq: Prerequisites: HTMT 450.

title: Social and Cultural Issues in the Hospitality and Tourism Industry
description: Topics relating to the various social structures and social issues facing the hospitality industry today and into the future.
subject: HTMT

title: Hospitality and Tourism Innovation Entrepreneurship
description: Fundamental innovation and entrepreneurship concepts in hospitality and tourism. Emphasizes the key phases of entrepreneurship: creating and finding opportunities, evaluating and acting on opportunities, and resourcing new opportunities.
subject: HTMT

title: Theme Park and Attractions Management
description: This course will give students an overview of the theme park and attractions industry. We will explore each of the areas of this industry including: history, venues, resources, ride operations, merchandising, food service and design.
subject: HTMT

title: Security Management of Hotels and Restaurants
description: Individualized security programs, procedures, legal issues, and review of local, state, and federal laws that apply to the lodging and restaurant industry.
subject: HTMT
prereq: Prerequisites: HTMT 357 or equivalent.

title: Advanced Lodging Management
description: Advanced principles of the management of hotels and resorts.
subject: HTMT
prereq: Prerequisites: HTMT 260.

title: Advanced Meeting Management
description: Analysis of current issues and problems in the meetings industry with emphasis on planning, organizing, managing, and enhancing meetings.
subject: HTMT
prereq: Prerequisites: HTMT 364.

title: International Lodging Management
description: Analysis of the structure of international lodging companies, challenges of marketing U.S. lodging companies abroad, and cultural differences in international management.
subject: HTMT
prereq: Prerequisites: HTMT 260.

title: Timeshare and Vacation Ownership Management
description: Management of the timeshare and vacation ownership industry.
subject: HTMT

title: Managing Food Service Operations
description: An advanced study of the food-service industry and its operations both internally and externally to the physical plant.
subject: HTMT
prereq: Prerequisites: HTMT 270.

title: The Global Business of Beverage Distribution
description: The Global Business of Beverage Distribution, exploring the global beverage industry from product management perspective, from raw materials to end user.

title: Geopolitics and International Business
description: This class introduces graduate students to the modern global business environment in its broad economic, political, social, and geopolitical context. On the one hand, the institutional and economic progress achieved by many societies through the last four decades has enriched consumers, improved economic policymaking, and turbocharged innovation, hence offering unprecedented opportunities to global firms. On the other hand, the emergence of geopolitical conflict between major powers (such as the U.S., the EU, China, and Russia), and the political polarization within some Western societies, have threatened globalization and the institutional structures supporting multinational corporations. We will strive to understand these contradictions by systematically examining diverse risks and opportunities arising from the global and national business settings. Among others, we will explore three big-picture questions: (1) What are the distinct economic functions of the state in various geographical settings, and how do these functions impact corporate performance? (2) How does the sociopolitical environment affect the firms, and what can the firms do to shape this environment? (3) What does modern geopolitical conflict mean for corporate strategy?
subject: IBUS
