title: Compensation and Retention
description: An examination of how organizations use pay and other types of rewards (both intrinsic and extrinsic) to affect employee motivation and behavior.
subject: MGMT
prereq: Prerequisites: MGMT 374.

title: Talent Management
description: An examination of how organizations can improve workforce quality by making effective use of recruiting and selection processes. Attention is also given to the training and development of new hires and to legal issues that surround the staffing process.
subject: MGMT
prereq: Prerequisites: MGMT 374.

title: International Human Resource Management
description: This course examines how human resources are managed within a global context. It examines how human resources are managed within global firms as well as across different cultural settings.
subject: MGMT
prereq: Prerequisites: MGMT 374.

title: Corporate Social Responsibility and Stakeholder Management
description: This course examines the role of corporate social responsibility in managing organizations. Attention is given to the role of stakeholder management and ethics in organizational decision-making.
subject: MGMT
prereq: Prerequisites: MGMT 371.

title: Managing Today's Global Workforce
description: Principles and practices of managing a dynamic and complex workforce in today’s global business environment. Topics to include: leadership, team dynamics, communication, motivation, conflict resolution, and organizational culture.
subject: MGMT
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MGMT 371.

title: Analytics for the Human Resources Professional
description: Metrics and measurements of HR concepts; processes through which knowledge is gained; tools and techniques for gathering and analyzing data; and critical thinking centered on whether findings are likely to be reproducible and trustworthy.
subject: MGMT
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in both MGMT 371 and MGSC 291.

title: Intercultural Competencies for Working in International Teams
description: How to recruit, lead, and collaborate more effectively in international teams by examining differences related to culture, work-values, economic climate, and career progression.
subject: MGMT
prereq: Prerequisites: IBUS 310.

title: Developing and Launching New Ventures
description: Explore business models and paths to commercialization for new ventures and concepts through experimentation; investigate strategies to develop and validate the business model; identify the resources needed to launch the venture and methods for acquiring those resources.
subject: MGMT
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MGMT 373.

title: Executing Strategy in New Ventures
description: Influencing the performance of new ventures through the strategies, metrics, and resources needed for their development, monitoring, and funding.
subject: MGMT
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MGMT 473.

title: Collective Bargaining
description: Practice and structure of collective bargaining, and administration of collective agreements, law of union-management relations, labor history, and bargaining theory and practice.
subject: MGMT
prereq: Prerequisites: MGMT 371 and junior standing.

title: Strategic Management
description: A study of the formulation and application of functionally integrated business policy by top management. Emphasis is on decision making in the face of changing conditions.
subject: MGMT
prereq: Prerequisites: MKTG 350, FINA 363, MGMT 371.

title: Applications in Entrepreneurship and New Ventures
description: Through immersive projects, students apply entrepreneurial concepts, tools, and frameworks to advance a new or established venture.
subject: MGMT
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MGMT 473.

title: Special Topics in Management
description: Current topics, issues and practices in various areas of Management. Course may be repeated up to three (3) times as content varies by title.
subject: MGMT

title: Business Internship in Management
description: Supervised work experience as approved by department. Generally three hours of academic credit, but upon special request of supervising professor and approval of appropriate area director, an internship may carry a maximum of six hours credit, however, only three hours may apply towards major credit. Pass-Fail only. Internship contract required.
subject: MGMT
prereq: Prerequisites: Completion of DMSB core curriculum except MGMT 478, plus at least one additional course in the student’s major field of study. Cumulative GPA of 2.75 or consent of instructor.

title: Special Topics in Management
description: Current topics, issues and practices in various areas of Management. Course may be repeated up to four (4) times as content varies by title.
subject: MGMT

title: Human Resources and the Global Firm
description: An analysis of human resources practices viewed from a comparative, cross-national perspective and the perspective of a multinational enterprise.
subject: MGMT

title: Management of Human Resources
description: The processes inherent in effective management of the organization's human resources. Topics include: employee selection, training, and development; design of compensation and reward systems; applied motivation models; and current issues in the management of human resources.
subject: MGMT

title: Management of Compensation
description: Examines the techniques, policies, processes, strategies, and practices used by companies, managers, and individuals to effectively and efficiently motivate behavior via rewards.
subject: MGMT
prereq: Prerequisites: MGMT 701.

title: Staffing
description: Topics in staffing on a rotating basis among job analysis, recruitment, test validation, selection systems, and other subjects.
subject: MGMT
prereq: Prerequisites: MGMT 701.

title: Employment Relations Law
description: The law of employment relations. Policy and practice in areas such as equal employment, wages and hours, employee health and safety, pensions, and labor relations.
subject: MGMT

title: Labor Relations
description: An analysis of some of the major problems faced by managers in their dealings with organizations representing employees. Primary emphasis is on the negotiation of labor agreements and the handling of problems arising under them. The public policy aspect of these problems is also considered.
subject: MGMT

title: Employee Responsibilities and Rights
description: Rights of employees in relation to their employers that arise from individual and collective agreements. Employee discipline and justice. Grievance procedures and their administration, including labor arbitration.
subject: MGMT

title: American Labor Unions
description: The nature, structure, and development of the labor union in the United States. Topics covered include labor history, structure of the labor movement, union governance, the law of unions, and problems of the labor movement.
subject: MGMT

title: Human Resource Metrics and Research
description: Applied data analysis and research methods in human resources. Emphasis on research design, analytic strategies, measurement of human resource variables and outcomes, and presentation of written and oral information.
subject: MGMT

title: Human Resource and Business Strategy
description: Capstone course for master’s students in human resources. integration of course work through analysis of current management issues and the use of case analysis and business simulations.
subject: MGMT
prereq: Prerequisites: MGMT 701.

title: Internship or Practicum in Human Resources
description: Field experience in human resources. Includes a report analyzing the experience.
subject: MGMT

title: Teams and Teamwork Management
description: When to use teams; how to design and implement team structures and motivate and lead team members; team decision making, conflict resolution, and other team processes.
subject: MGMT

title: Managing Cross-border Teams
description: How the presence of members from multiple national cultures and multiple national locations affect the functioning of teams within the multinational corporation.
subject: MGMT
prereq: Prerequisites: DMSB 714 or IBUS 700 or MGMT 770 or MGMT 799.

title: Consulting and Organizational Development in MNCs
description: Restructuring and transformation initiatives within multinational organizations as internal consultants and/or change agents.
subject: MGMT
prereq: Prerequisites: DMSB 711 or IBUS 700 or MGMT 779.

title: Negotiations
description: Negotiations analysis, thinking, and communication; alignment between negotiation objectives and strategies; negotiation skills.
subject: MGMT

title: Learning with Leaders
description: Experiential learning with successful business leaders; diagnosis of specific contexts and issues of leadership within these contexts; integration and comparison of leadership across contexts.
subject: MGMT

title: Strategic Management of Technology and Innovation
description: Understanding and managing innovation, both sustaining and disruptive; creating new capabilities in new entrepreneurial firms or large corporations.
subject: MGMT

title: Mergers and Acquisitions
description: Mergers and acquisitions in international and domestic settings: effects of strategic, financial, legal, accounting, and human resource factors.
subject: MGMT

title: Human Resources Experiential Project
description: Provides an applied Human Resources experiential project where students work under close faculty supervision to solve real-world business challenges using applied research and analytical skills.
subject: MGMT
prereq: Prerequisites: MGMT 725.

title: Strategic Management
description: Accounting, economics, finance, marketing, production, and international environment integrated for the analysis of management problems. Oral and written communications using the case method.
subject: MGMT

title: Finance for Human Resource Professionals
description: Fundamentals of analysis and decision-making in financial management for human resource professionals.
subject: MGMT

title: Organizational Behavior
description: Development of an understanding of behavioral concepts necessary for effective production management of organizations. Current literature, case studies, and other simulations to demonstrate applicability of concepts. Concepts studies include perception, motivation, leadership, and intergroup conflict.
subject: MGMT

title: Organization Theory and Design
description: A study of the nature of organizations, their design, their structure, their processes, as well as problems inherent in organizations (e.g., coordination, conflict, communications, power usage, politics).
subject: MGMT

title: Employee and Leadership Development
description: Examination of methods of employee development and leadership development with an emphasis given to program design, management, and evaluation.
subject: MGMT

title: Business Policy
description: Policy formulation and decision-making in organizations. The interrelationships of functional areas within the organization, the application of management skills and processes to integrate these areas, and the impact of factors external to the organization are examined with a view toward the attainment of organizational goals.
subject: MGMT
prereq: Prerequisites: MKTG 751, FINA 760, MGMT 770, and MGSC 791.

title: The Firm and Its Environment
description: The forces which influence and constrain decision and actions within the individual firm. Consideration of these forces as features of the existing legal, social, and ethical environments. Specific topics include the legal system and public policy, social organization, moral and ethical standards, public opinion, the social responsibility of the firm, and conception within the firm of its role in society, and the interaction of these forces with economic forces.
subject: MGMT
prereq: Prerequisites: ECON 720.

title: Strategic Analysis and Application of Innovation
description: Analyzing emerging technologies and applying strategic innovation frameworks for stakeholders in competitive environments with a multi-disciplinary team.
subject: MGMT
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MGMT 733 or MGMT 776.

title: Strategic Innovation Planning and Processes
description: An evaluation of strategic planning and processes in converging industries. Emphasis on underlying scientific, innovation, and technological concepts.
subject: MGMT

title: Innovation and New Venture Analysis
description: Examines the principles, tools, and techniques necessary to conceptualize and initiate a new business entity.
subject: MGMT
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in ACCT 725, DMSB 710, or ACCT 728.

title: Small Business Management
description: An examination of problems involved in the organization and management of a small business, including an analysis of legal forms, location, product market determination, production, and other operating conditions.
subject: MGMT

title: Personnel and Employment Relations
description: Orientation to the field of personnel and employment relations. Also provides training in communications and computer skills needed in managing human resources.
subject: MGMT

title: Entrepreneurial Finance and the Dynamics of Emerging Ventures
description: Exploration of the funding and financial management of emerging ventures, including sources and structure of capital, financial levers to drive performance and metrics to monitor performance, and the study of how to impact, capture, quantify and realize value.
subject: MGMT

title: Managing Careers in Organizations
description: Chronological view of career development issues, from initial vocational and job choice decisions through retirement. Short-run and long-term consequences of individual and organizational career development strategies.
subject: MGMT

title: Business Research and Reports
description: Research tools, techniques, and sources are utilized in the development of both analytical ability and facility in communication.
subject: MGMT

title: Foundations of Management Theory
description: Emphasis on development of understanding the history of management theory and examination of current interpretations of these concepts.
subject: MGMT

title: Doctoral Seminar in the Behavioral Sciences I
description: Study of major theoretical and methodological issues in organizational behavior with emphasis on developing conceptual models and implementing research designs.
subject: MGMT

title: Doctoral Seminar in the Behavioral Sciences II
description: Exploration of current specialized topics in organizational behavior with emphasis on synthesizing research, developing conceptual models, and implementing research designs.
subject: MGMT

title: Current Topics in the Organization Sciences
description: An advanced seminar focusing upon reading, synthesis, and critical evaluation of current research in business and management.
subject: MGMT
prereq: Prerequisites: MGMT 821 or MGMT 822.

title: Doctoral Seminar in Human Resource Management I
description: Theories and research in human resource management.
subject: MGMT

title: Doctoral Seminar in Human Resource Management II
description: Advanced theories and research in human resource management.
subject: MGMT

title: Seminar in Entrepreneurship
description: Theories and research in entrepreneurship, emphasizing strategic management framework in integrating diverse treatments of the entrepreneur, new ventures, and corporate innovation.
subject: MGMT
prereq: Prerequisites: MGMT 878.

title: Organization Theory
description: An evaluation of theories of organization, with particular emphasis on business applications. Approaches to a conceptual framework include decision theory, sociological and behavioral theories. Various models are evaluated in an attempt to build a framework for analysis of organizations.
subject: MGMT

title: Seminar in Management Research Methodology
description: Research methods and techniques for translation of management theory and practical problems into testable propositions.
subject: MGMT
prereq: Prerequisites: MGSC 882.

title: Seminar in Strategy Formulation
description: Investigates the theoretical and empirical evidence regarding strategies aimed at creating and sustaining competitive superiority in business firms and the strategy formation process.
subject: MGMT

title: Seminar in Strategy Implementation
description: Continuation of MGMT 878 , with emphasis on strategy implementation.
subject: MGMT
prereq: Prerequisites: MGMT 878.

title: Advanced Statistics for Business I
description: The development and application of advanced statistical methods to problems in business and economics. Topics include application of estimation and hypothesis testing in both univariate and multivariate cases.
subject: MGMT

title: Advanced Statistics for Business II
description: The structure and analysis of experimental and research designs with applications to business problems.
subject: MGMT
prereq: Prerequisites: MGMT 882 or equivalent.

title: Computer Information Systems in Business
description: An introduction to the effective use of information systems tools in day-to-day business communications, analysis, and decision making.
subject: MGSC

title: Applied Statistics for Business
description: Descriptive statistics, topics in probability, statistical inference and modeling. Emphasis on the collection, summarization, analysis, and reporting of numerical findings relevant to business decisions and economic analysis.
subject: MGSC
prereq: Prerequisites: STAT 206.

title: Program Design and Development
description: Fundamental algorithms and processes used in business information systems. Development and representation of programming logic. Introduction to implementation using a high-level programming language.
subject: MGSC

title: Business Information Systems
description: Introduction to the planning, development, and management of Business Information Systems (BIS) and related information technologies in organizations. Topics include hardware, software, database, telecommunications, and the Internet.
subject: MGSC
prereq: Prerequisites: MGSC 290 or equivalent.

title: Applied Statistical Modeling
description: Multiple regression, ANOVA, forecasting, quality control, and nonparametric techniques. Emphasis is on the application of these statistical models in business decision making.
subject: MGSC
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MGSC 291.

title: Quantitative Analysis for Business Decision Making
description: Quantitative approaches for managerial problem solving and decision making. Formulation, solution, analysis, and interpretation of spreadsheet-based models for common business decision-making situations. Optimization, simulation, decision analysis.
subject: MGSC
prereq: Prerequisites: MGSC 291.

title: Advanced Management Science
description: Advanced topics in management science; stochastic models, nonlinear programming, dynamic programming, Markov processes, and multiple criteria decision models.
subject: MGSC
prereq: Prerequisites: MGSC 392.

title: Data Analytics for Business
description: Extracting, transforming, visualizing, and analyzing data to support business intelligence and decision making. Business analytics, relational database management, principles and applications of data visualization.
subject: MGSC
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MGSC 290 and MGSC 291.

title: Operations Management
description: Management of activities/resources that result in the production of competitive goods and services in the global market place. Integrates cross-functional concepts from marketing, finance, and management.
subject: MGSC
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in STAT 206.

title: Applied Problem Solving and Programming
description: Systematic problem definition, solution formulation, and computer implementation for business and related areas. Internet and database applications. Programming exercises in a high-level programming language.
subject: MGSC
prereq: Prerequisites: CSCE 104 or MGSC 298.

title: International Information Systems
description: An examination of the challenges and opportunities associated with the development, management, and use of global information systems.
subject: MGSC

title: Special Topics in Management Science
description: Current topics, issues and practices in various areas of Management Science. Course may be repeated up to three (3) times as content varies by title.
subject: MGSC
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MGSC 395.

title: Business Process Management
description: Course entails learning and applying cutting-edge concepts and techniques of business process analyses to process improvement initiatives such as reengineering, lean, and six-sigma.
subject: MGSC
prereq: Prerequisites: MGSC 395.

title: Service Operations Management
description: Course focuses on the management of service operations, with considerable attention paid to cutting-edge quantitative techniques. Topics covered include facility location, waiting line management, and revenue management, among others.
subject: MGSC
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MGSC 291 and MGSC 395.

title: Global Sourcing Strategies and Application
description: Course covers skills necessary to pursue sourcing-related careers in manufacturing, services, retailing, and government agencies, including discussion of cutting-edge negotiation and global sourcing strategies pursued in exemplar firms.
subject: MGSC
prereq: Prerequisites: MGSC 395.

title: Innovation and Product Development
description: Creation and launch of viable businesses, products, and services using innovation tournaments. Development of problem solving and design skills. Application to real hurdles in innovation and entrepreneurship.
subject: MGSC
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MGSC 395.

title: Sustainable Operations Supply Chain
description: This course explores how operations managers can analyze and make decisions regarding the interaction of operations activities on the environment. Students will learn various tools to enable better decision making within the sustainable operations realm.
subject: MGSC
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MGSC 395.

title: Information Systems Analysis and Design
description: Analysis and design of business information systems using modern tools, techniques, and methodologies.
subject: MGSC
prereq: Prerequisites: MGSC 394 or CSCE 145, and MGSC 390.

title: Supply Chain Management
description: This course examines principles governing an integrated supply chain that includes the cross-functional processes of sourcing, inventory management, and logistics. Key tools for managing supply chains will also be discussed.
subject: MGSC
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MGSC 395.

title: Logistics, Transportation and Distribution
description: This course is designed to provide a body of knowledge, technical know-how, and standards for those in the logistics, transportation and distribution industries.
subject: MGSC
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MGSC 395.

title: Business Telecommunications
description: Identifies and explains the principal technologies, concepts, and techniques currently applied in the business data communications field. Current and future hardware and software technologies, networking, protocols, and the OSI Model are covered.
subject: MGSC
prereq: Prerequisites: MGSC 390.

title: Supply Chain Planning and Execution
description: Managing the flow of materials from suppliers to customers; supply chain concepts, terminology, and relationships; processes used to develop operations plans.
subject: MGSC
prereq: Prerequisites: MGSC 395.

title: OSC Capstone Project
description: Course entails executing projects that apply OSC concepts to solve significant operational or supply-chain problems at simulated or actual firms. Teams write recommendation reports and oversee sourcing strategies pursued in exemplar firms.
subject: MGSC
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MGSC 485 and permission of instructor based on competitive application.

title: Project Management for Business
description: An introduction to the qualitative and quantitative approaches to the management of projects as well as a review MS Project software.
subject: MGSC

title: Statistical Quality Control
description: Statistical procedures for process control including CUSUM and Shewhart Control Charts, and lot-acceptance sampling.
subject: MGSC
prereq: Prerequisites: STAT 509 or STAT 515 or MGSC 391.

title: E-Commerce Concepts and Research Topics
description: Social, technological, commercial, marketing, and political implications of current and impending tends in E-Commerce.
subject: MGSC
prereq: Prerequisites: MGSC 390.

title: Simulation of Business Systems
description: Theory and design of business simulation experiments, development and use of computer simulation models, and analysis of data generated by computer simulation experiments.
subject: MGSC
prereq: Prerequisites: MGSC 291.

title: Analysis of Decisions Under Uncertainty
description: Theory and practice of making decisions in an environment of uncertainty; development of skill in the assessment of preferences and probability distributions.
subject: MGSC
prereq: Prerequisites: MGSC 291.

title: Strategic Management of Information Systems
description: Strategic management and use of information systems in organizations.
subject: MGSC

title: Database Management for Business
description: Technology overview and principles of database design for business applications. Enterprise database administration and planning. Design exercises and projects using the latest database management systems software.
subject: MGSC

title: Quantitative Foundations for Business and Economics I
description: Calculus and classical optimization methods applied to problems in business and economic analysis; matrices, derivatives, and integrals in the analysis of both univariate and multivariate business and economic models.
subject: MGSC

title: Quantitative Foundations for Business and Economics II
description: Statistics and probability theory applied to problems of business and economic analysis.
subject: MGSC
prereq: Prerequisites: MGSC 690 or ECON 690.

title: Quantitative Methods I
description: Probability and statistics necessary for graduate study in economics and business administration; estimation, hypothesis testing, regression, analysis of variance, and nonparametric methods.
subject: MGSC

title: Quantitative Methods II
description: Decision models useful in business and economics; linear programming, sensitivity analysis and duality, network models, integer programming, dynamic programming, inventory and queuing, and simulation.
subject: MGSC
prereq: Prerequisites: MGSC 692 or equivalent.

title: Computers in Business Administration and Economics
description: Introduction to data processing concepts suitable for research interests in business and economics. Topics include research databases, statistical packages, and communication networks.
subject: MGSC

title: Quantitative Methods in Business
description: Quantitative procedures used to make informed business decisions. The course focuses on the application of descriptive statistics, probability, interval estimates, hypothesis testing, and regression to management problems. Restricted to business students.
subject: MGSC

title: Healthcare Operations Management
description: This course will emphasize on understanding and improving healthcare (care delivery and administrative) processes and the tools required for the same. We will apply operations management concepts – like capacity management, technology management, and process improvement, to develop a better understanding of healthcare processes and a framework for analyzing and improving such processes. We will also explore a variety of current and relevant issues facing the healthcare sector and attempt to come up with frameworks for analyzing such issues.
subject: MGSC

title: Predictive Analytics using No-Code AI
description: The graduate-level course on Predictive Analytics using No-Code AI is designed to equip students with the necessary skills to apply advanced predictive analytics without the need for traditional programming.
subject: MGSC
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MGSC 711 or DMSB 712, or by instructor permission.

title: Global Sourcing: Strategies and Applications
description: This course covers skills necessary to pursue sourcing and related careers in manufacturing, services, retailing, and government agencies, including discussion of cutting-edge negotiation and sourcing strategies pursued by exemplar firms.
subject: MGSC
prereq: Prerequisites: DMSB 718 or MGSC 791.

title: Project Management
description: Management of projects including justification, planning, scheduling, monitoring, controlling, and auditing. Makes extensive use of project management information systems.
subject: MGSC

title: Sustainable Operations and Supply Chain
description: This course explores how operations managers can analyze and make decisions regarding the interaction of operations activities on the environment. Students will learn various tools to enable better decision making within the sustainable operations realm.
subject: MGSC
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MGSC 791 or DMSB 718.

title: Supply Chain Modeling
description: Study of the issues, principles, tools and decision processes involved in designing and effectively managing a supply chain. The course entails analyzing and developing models on various topics such as inventory management, transportation, network design and planning, and supply chain coordination.
subject: MGSC
prereq: Prerequisites: DMSB 718 or MGSC 791.

title: Advanced Quantitative Methods in Business
description: Practical applications of statistical, optimization, heuristic, simulation, and other quantitative techniques for analyzing problems of contemporary business interest in the areas of manufacturing, services, and supply chain management.
subject: MGSC
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MGSC 711, DMSB 712 or equivalent.

title: Revenue Management
description: Concepts of forecasting demand, segmenting customers and allocating capacity or customizing proce offers to distinct customer segments to maximize profits.
subject: MGSC

title: Innovation and Product Development
description: Creation and launch of viable businesses, products, and services using innovation tournaments. Development of problem solving and design skills. Application to real hurdles in innovation and entrepreneurship.
subject: MGSC

title: Business Process Analysis, Design, and Implementation
description: Study of the concepts, tools and issues associated with analyzing and improving modern business processes including quality management, process control, systems and technologies for process support, and case studies.
subject: MGSC

title: Data Resource Management
description: Overview of data resource management, including database technology and design, information architecture planning, and database administration. A design project is required.
subject: MGSC

title: Operations Management
description: Survey of production or operations functions of organizations. Development of concepts and decision processes relevant to major problem areas. Emphasizes relevance of operations management in both manufacturing and service organizations.
subject: MGSC
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in ACCT 726 and MGSC 711.

title: Simulation Methods in Business Systems
description: Advanced theory and design of business simulation programs and validation and statistical analysis of model output.
subject: MGSC
prereq: Prerequisites: MGSC 692, MGSC 694.

title: Programming Methods
description: Mathematical programming techniques which are useful in business and economics. Topics include: solution techniques and applications of linear programming, duality theory, parametric programming, the decomposition problem, integer programming, dynamic programming, Lagrange multipliers, Kuhn-Tucker theory, and an introduction to control theory.
subject: MGSC
prereq: Prerequisites: MGSC 694.

title: Econometrics and Regression I
description: A treatment of single equation estimating techniques for the simple linear model, various nonlinear models, and the general linear model.
subject: MGSC
prereq: Prerequisites: MGSC 882.

title: Information Systems
description: The study of the integration of functional area and company-wide information components considering both internal and external information flows.
subject: MGSC

title: Sampling Techniques
description: Statistical designs and techniques for survey investigations. Mathematical development of sampling systems; sampling units; sample size; estimation; costs; non-sampling problems. Methods of obtaining and reporting information.
subject: MGSC
prereq: Prerequisites: MGSC 692 or equivalent.

title: Introduction to Cybersecurity Management
description: Analysis of concepts, conventions, and assumptions needed to manage cybersecurity policy and strategy development. Covers nomenclature, primary threat vectors, various cybersecurity frameworks, and ethical dimensions of cybersecurity management.
subject: MGSC

title: Service Operations Management
description: Analysis of service operations, integrated about strategy, design, and delivery issues. Comparison of manufacturing and service operations, emphasizing consumer participation in the service process.
subject: MGSC
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in DMSB 718 or MGSC 791.

title: Operations Strategy and Productive Systems Design
description: Development and implementation of productive operations strategy and its relation to the overall organizational strategy; particular attention is given to the interface between product and process technology.
subject: MGSC
prereq: Prerequisites: MGSC 791.

title: Supply Chain Coordination and Control
description: Design and management of systems that coordinate information and material flows within and between firms in a supply chain. Addresses planning basics, system alternatives, and advanced value stream synchronization.
subject: MGSC
prereq: Prerequisites: DMSB 718 or MGSC 791.

title: Advanced Statistics for Business I
description: The development and application of advanced statistical methods to problems in business and economics. Topics include application of estimation and hypothesis testing in both univariate and multivariate cases.
subject: MGSC

title: Design of Advanced Business Information Structures
description: A study of data structures and file management with specific attention to applied business problems. Special emphasis is placed on the structure and management of information for management decision-making systems.
subject: MGSC
prereq: Prerequisites: CSCE 205 and CSCE 500.

title: Telecommunications
description: Technological concepts and techniques applied in video, data, and voice communications. Topics include local area networks, wide area networks, standards, management, and cost issues involved in telecommunications.
subject: MGSC
prereq: Prerequisites: MGSC 796.

title: Advanced Statistics for Business II
description: The structure and analysis of experimental and research designs with applications to business and economic problems.
subject: MGSC
prereq: Prerequisites: MGSC 882/MGMT 882 or equivalent.

title: Advanced Topics in Management Science
description: Topics will be selected from: nonlinear programming, stochastic programming, integer programming, spectral analysis, decision theory, Markov processes, Box-Jenkins methods, management information systems.
subject: MGSC
prereq: Prerequisites: MGSC 694.

title: Econometrics and Regression II
description: Topics in generalized least squares, autocorrelation, distributed lag models, principle components, identification, and simultaneous estimating techniques.
subject: MGSC
prereq: Prerequisites: MGSC 795.

title: Business Intelligence Systems with AI Applications
description: Explore AI tools and techniques to build several machine learning applications for supporting effective managerial decision making.
subject: MGSC

title: Global Supply Chain Operations Management: Graduate Capstone Consulting Project
description: This course entails a live project under faculty supervision in which student teams apply GSCOM concepts and techniques to solve practical significant problems and opportunities in real-world service and manufacturing firms.
subject: MGSC

title: Management of Technology and Innovation
description: The role of product and process innovation in competitiveness, intellectual property rights, organizing the technical effort, and techniques to enhance and pace technology development.
subject: MGSC
prereq: Prerequisites: MKTG 751 and MGSC 791.

title: Principles of Marketing
description: Principles and concepts underlying marketing functions, including the conception, pricing, promotion, and distribution of products and services and the role of marketing in society.
subject: MKTG

title: Consumer Behavior
description: The consumer decision process, with emphasis on consumer decision making, satisfaction/dissatisfaction factors, perception, learning, group influences, and marketing strategy implications.
subject: MKTG
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MKTG 350.

title: Principles of Marketing Research
description: Investigates the theory and practice of marketing research with emphasis on the problem-oriented nature of marketing research and how research activities are implemented by marketing researchers and used by managers.
subject: MKTG
prereq: Prerequisites: MKTG 350, MGSC 291.

title: Sales Strategy
description: Understand the fundamentals of the sales process and how to ensure client engagement. Learn follow-up skills that will help you in every walk of life. Learn the fundamentals of effective business communication (voicemail, email, and hand-written).
subject: MKTG

title: Sales Automation and Customer Management
description: The context, tools and conceptual understanding of the technology-related and managerial expectations of an entry level sales representative.
subject: MKTG

title: Pricing Strategy and Analytics
description: An in-depth understanding of the fundamentals of pricing strategy formulation and implementation.
subject: MKTG
prereq: Prerequisites: MKTG 350.

title: Data Science for Business Decision-Making
description: The conceptual, applied, and managerial elements of advanced data science methods for business decision-making.
subject: MKTG
prereq: Prerequisites: B+ or better in MGSC 291.

title: Topics in Marketing
description: Reading and research on selected topics in marketing. Course content varies and will be announced in the schedule of classes by title.
subject: MKTG
prereq: Prerequisites: MKTG 350.

title: Sales Practicum
description: In this course students will learn about how to sell various products/services. The concepts will be brought to life with real tools and training from several companies. This “Experiential Learning” class involves learning by participating in real professional sales in action.
subject: MKTG
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MKTG 350, MKTG 352, MKTG 457.

title: Marketing Practicum
description: Students form a working marketing agency and complete a real-world marketing campaign for a client. Working as a team, they apply marketing theory and concepts to a substantively important marketing problem.
subject: MKTG
prereq: Prerequisites: MKTG 350, MKTG 351, MKTG 352.

title: Business-to-Business Marketing
description: Marketing strategy in marketing to other business and nonprofit organizations, emphasizing relationship building, alliances and partnerships, data interchange, power shifts in the channel and the impact of changing technology.
subject: MKTG
prereq: Prerequisites: MKTG 350.

title: Marketing Communications and Strategy
description: Integration of advertising, sales promotion, point-of-purchase communications toward the goal of enhancing brand equity.
subject: MKTG
prereq: Prerequisites: MKTG 350.

title: Sales Management
description: The Sales Management course is the study of building and leading high-performance sales teams to grow revenue for organizations.
subject: MKTG

title: Introduction To Sales
description: Examines fundamentals of personal selling and sales management and the development of communication and selling skills that yield desired sales results.
subject: MKTG

title: Marketing Channels and Distribution
description: Marketing functions and channel flows used to develop distribution strategies that provide effective, efficient, and reliable delivery of products and services to end-user markets.
subject: MKTG
prereq: Prerequisites: MKTG 350.

title: Product and Brand Management
description: The primary tasks involved in developing marketing strategies from a product and/or brand management perspective, including product policy, competitive analysis, pricing, brand equity management, marketing information systems management, sales forecasting and monitoring customer satisfaction.
subject: MKTG
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MKTG 350.

title: Retailing Management
description: Studies retail institutions, relationships with other channel members, factors influencing patronage patterns, adapting to change in dynamic environments with emphasis on strategy implications for managers in retail organizations.
subject: MKTG
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MKTG 350.

title: Marketing Strategy and Planning
description: Management of marketing organizations and integration of functions, with emphasis on planning and designing strategies and applying tools and techniques for problem solving and decision making. For marketing majors only.
subject: MKTG
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MKTG 350 and MKTG 352.

title: New Product Development
description: The development of new products (products or services) is a cross-functional process inside any organization. This course explores this process and how new product development managers are responsible for leading an organization from identifying marketing opportunities or gaps through product commercialization. This course is focused on how companies take ideas, prioritizes them and ultimately makes them a reality.
subject: MKTG
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MKTG 350.

title: Digital Marketing Social Media Analytics
description: This course aims to prepare students to be effective leaders in marketing analytics, especially in the dynamic context of digital platforms and social media.
subject: MKTG
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MKTG 350.

title: Business, Markets and Sustainability
description: Sustainability (environmental, social, and economic performance) is a top priority for CEOs and is increasingly embedded into business strategy, resulting in changing business models, product strategies and innovation, etc. This course explores the challenges and opportunities facing businesses through a sustainability lens. Through readings, speakers, discussions and critical thinking exercises, students will gain exposure to the key environmental and social issues facing businesses today and how they are reacting. Emphasis will be placed on understanding the tradeoffs between economic, environmental and social performance. Throughout the semester, students will analyze the complex tradeoffs and leverage various frameworks to understand how businesses determine their strategic priorities, measure performance, and communicate that to key stakeholders.
subject: MKTG

title: Consultative Selling with Certification
description: Understand and demonstrate benefit and solution-based sales processes. Create daily discipline to drive sales performance.
subject: MKTG

title: Social Media Marketing
description: An introduction to Marketing on the predominant social media platforms (e.g. Facebook, Instagram, Twitter, etc).
subject: MKTG
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MKTG 350.

title: Marketing for Nonprofit Organizations
description: Overview of the unique challenges associated with marketing the offerings of a nonprofit organization. Organizing, planning, implementing, and controlling marketing efforts of nonprofit organizations. Focus on fundraising, market research, positioning, and communications.
subject: MKTG

title: Sales Internship
description: Student will participate in a Sales Internship for the duration of the current semester. The scope of the work for the internship must be agreed upon by the student, a representative of the DMBS undergrad advisement office, the professor and the internship coordinator for the company before the semester starts.
subject: MKTG
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MKTG 350.

title: Marketing Internship
description: Student will participate in a Marketing Internship for the duration of the current semester. The scope of the work for the internship must be agreed upon by the student, a representative of the DMBS undergrad advisement office, the professor and the internship coordinator for the company before the semester starts.
subject: MKTG
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MKTG 350.

title: Marketing Management
description: Marketing function with emphasis on the procedures and techniques for analyzing, planning, and implementing marketing strategies and tactics related to product, pricing, communication, and distribution decisions.
subject: MKTG

title: Marketing Research
description: Research methodology as applied to marketing problems; includes research problem definition, sample design, data collection procedures, valid and reliable measurement, data analysis techniques, and sales forecasting fundamentals.
subject: MKTG
prereq: Prerequisites: MKTG 701 or DMSB 716.

title: Marketing Planning
description: Tools and techniques for creating marketing plans.
subject: MKTG
prereq: Prerequisites: MKTG 701 or DMSB 716.

title: Consumer Behavior
description: Concepts, theories, and techniques applicable to obtaining a sophisticated understanding of consumer motives, attitudes, decision-making processes, and satisfaction determinants.
subject: MKTG
prereq: Prerequisites: MKTG 701 or DMSB 716.

title: Marketing Communications
description: Advertising, sales promotions, marketing-oriented public relations, event and sponsorship marketing, point-of-purchase communications, and other aspects of integrated marketing communications.
subject: MKTG
prereq: Prerequisites: MKTG 701 or DMSB 716.

title: Sales and Sales Management
description: The role and activities of sales in marketing, including concepts, practices, and procedures of sales force management.
subject: MKTG
prereq: Prerequisites: MKTG 701 or DMSB 716.

title: Product and Branding Policies
description: Product line and portfolio planning, stage-gate approach to new product development, product launch and product life cycle management, and branding strategies and procedures.
subject: MKTG
prereq: Prerequisites: MKTG 701 or DMSB 716.

title: Customer Relationship Management and Data Mining
description: Techniques, procedures, and software applications for database marketing, managing customer relations, and mining large databases.
subject: MKTG
prereq: Prerequisites: MKTG 701 or DMSB 716.

title: Internet Marketing
description: The Internet as both a marketing channel and communication medium, including E-commerce from a marketing perspective.
subject: MKTG
prereq: Prerequisites: MKTG 701 or DMSB 716.

title: Strategic Marketing
description: An integrative treatment of the role of marketing in strategic problem solving.
subject: MKTG
prereq: Prerequisites: MKTG 701 or DMSB 716.

title: Business, Markets, and Sustainability
description: This course explores sustainability through a marketing lens. Emphasis is placed on understanding the tradeoffs between economic, environmental and social performance while making product, price, distribution and promotional decisions.
subject: MKTG
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MKTG 701 or DMSB 716.

title: Topics in Marketing Thought and Practice
description: Readings and research on selected topics in marketing. Course content varies and will be announced in the schedule of classes by title.
subject: MKTG
prereq: Prerequisites: MKTG 701 or DMSB 716.

title: Managing Customer Satisfaction
description: This course explores the importance of customer satisfaction in today's marketplace and the challenges of designing and implementing an actual customer satisfaction measurement process.
subject: MKTG
prereq: Prerequisites: MKTG 710 or DMSB 716.

title: Pricing Strategy and Analysis
description: Economics and behavioral foundations of pricing strategy formulation and implementation.
subject: MKTG
prereq: Prerequisites: MKTG 701 or DMSB 701 or equivalent.

title: Listening to the Voice of the Market
description: Building competencies that help managers acquire knowledge from clients and apply it in ways that enhance openness in building strategic responses.
subject: MKTG
prereq: Prerequisites: DMSB 716 or MKTG 701.

title: Fundamentals of Marketing Analytics
description: The critical thinking, statistical tools, and marketing application of business analytics.
subject: MKTG
prereq: Prerequisites: DMSB 716 or MKTG 701 and DMSB 712 or MGSC 711.

title: Social and Digital Media Strategies for Businesses
description: Develop a social and digital media plan/solution to present to a client. Business enterprises and even non-business organizations are demanding new marketing hires and their marketing agencies have an acute understanding of how to develop and utilize online and social media as part of their integrated marketing plans. Both business-to-consumer and business-to-business enterprises are migrating to social media as a primary form of communication with customers and partners.
subject: MKTG
prereq: Prerequisites: DMSB 716 and MKTG 701.

title: Business to Business Marketing
description: Identifying, developing, communicating and commercializing a value proposition between businesses in the form of a product or service.
subject: MKTG
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in DMSB 716 or MKTG 701.

title: Sales: Process, People and Performance
description: Examines and explores the Sales function within the modern organization. In addition to addressing the practical and classic sales issues of alignment, structure, performance and development, the course will also review the technologies and trends that are changing the commercial landscape and causing the fast evolution of the sales function.
subject: MKTG
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in DMSB 716 or MKTG 701.

title: Machine Learning for Business Decision Making
description: The conceptual, applied, and managerial elements of machine learning for business decision-making.
subject: MKTG

title: Marketing Analytics
description: Analytical techniques for generating insights in marketing areas such as customer analytics, product analytics, marketing-mix analytics and social media analytics.
subject: MKTG
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MKTG 701 or DMSB 716; C or better in MGSC 711.

title: AI in Marketing
description: This course explores the transformative impact of AI on marketing, equipping students with the knowledge and skills to leverage AI tools in real-world marketing scenarios. Through a combination of theoretical insights, practical applications, and critical analysis, students will learn how to use AI to enhance market analysis, target audience definition, brand development, and go-to-market strategies.
subject: MKTG
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MKTG 701 or DMSB 716.

title: Marketing for Nonprofit Organizations
description: Non-profit organizations are important constituents in today’s business world, and their marketing efforts face unique issues and challenges that are different from for-profit organizations. Through lectures, cases, discussions, and a project, this course will examine what is unique about doing marketing in certain nonprofit sectors (e.g., arts, religious, social welfare, associations, charities, universities, hospitals, and government agencies). Students will come away from the course with an improved ability to organize, plan, implement, and control the marketing efforts of nonprofit organizations. It will also focus on certain marketing tasks that are crucial for nonprofit managers (e.g., fundraising, doing marketing research, positioning, and communications).
subject: MKTG

title: Digital Marketing Social Media Analytics
description: This course aims to prepare students to be effective leaders in marketing analytics, especially in the dynamic context of digital platforms and social media.
subject: MKTG
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MKTG 701 or DMSB 716 or equivalent.

title: Managing Artificial Intelligence and Data Science
description: Management concepts, methods, and frameworks to conceive, support, deploy, evaluate, and safeguard data science and AI initiatives.
subject: MKTG

title: Marketing Consulting Project
description: Develop successful marketing strategies using data, research, and analysis to create impactful strategies for real business clients. Businesses and recruiters are increasingly demanding experience handling practical problems faced by a marketing manager from our MBA students. In this elective course, the students get that experience.
subject: MKTG
prereq: Prerequisites: MKTG 701.

title: Research Methods and Philosophies in Marketing
description: Doctoral seminar covering research methods and philosophies that underpin knowledge generation in marketing.
subject: MKTG

title: Concepts and Theories in Consumer Research
description: Doctoral seminar exploring concepts, theories, and research methods relevant to understanding consumer behavior.
subject: MKTG

title: Current Topics in Consumer Research
description: Doctoral seminar involving intensive study and criticism of the current consumer research literature.
subject: MKTG
prereq: Prerequisites: MKTG 851.

title: Analytic Techniques for Marketing Decision Making
description: Doctoral seminar investigating contemporary analytic techniques for testing marketing theories.
subject: MKTG

title: Latent Variable Estimation Techniques
description: Doctoral seminar examining covariance structure methods for developing measures of unobservable constructs and testing structural models.
subject: MKTG

title: Conceptualization in Marketing
description: A directed project for marketing Ph.D. students requiring literature synthesis of a selected topical area and formulation of original concepts and theoretical propositions.
subject: MKTG

title: Empirical Testing of Theoretical Propositions in Marketing
description: A directed project for marketing Ph.D. students requiring an empirical investigation and testing of theoretical propositions.
subject: MKTG

title: Marketing Models
description: Doctoral seminar covering advances in marketing science models, including brand choice, product development, media choice, and other models.
subject: MKTG
prereq: Prerequisites: MGSC 882.

title: Seminar in Marketing Strategy I
description: Doctoral seminar investigating emerging paradigms and theory regarding the role of marketing within the firm and the effects of marketing mix variables on consumer behavior and firm performance.
subject: MKTG

title: Seminar in Marketing Strategy II
description: Theoretical foundations of marketing strategy.
subject: MKTG

title: Empirical Causal Analyses for Marketing Strategy Research
description: The course aims to provide good understanding of endogeneity issues in marketing strategy research and the empirical strategies that one can adopt to credibly estimate the effects of interest. The students should have a graduate level understanding of econometrics to benefit from this course.
subject: MKTG

title: The Ocean Environment
description: Origin and evolution of the oceans, plate tectonics, ocean circulation, waves and tides, seawater and sediment composition, and influences on biology. Three lecture and three laboratory hours per week. Scheduled field trips required.
subject: MSCI

title: The Living Ocean
description: Origin, evolution, and diversity of marine life, biological production, trophic dynamics, nutrient cycles, marine resources, and environmental concerns. Three lecture and three laboratory hours per week. Scheduled field trips required.
subject: MSCI

title: Oceans and Society
description: A nontechnical introduction to human interactions with the marine environment: marine organisms, marine systems, and the physical and chemical characteristics of oceans and estuaries. Not available for marine science major credit.
subject: MSCI

title: Oceans and Society Laboratory
description: Experiments and exercises which illustrate how specific components of marine environments are structured, function, and can be measured. Two laboratory hours per week. Not available for marine science major credit. Attendance on designated field trips may be required.
subject: MSCI

title: Coastal Environments of the Southeastern U.S.
description: Coastal zones of South Carolina and neighboring states, including geologic history, geomorphology, stratigraphy, hydrogeology, shoreline processes, environmental issues, and effects of man. Not available for geological science or marine science major credit. Three lecture hours each week plus optional field trips.
subject: MSCI

title: Coastal Environments of the Southeastern U.S. (Laboratory)
description: Exercises examining coastal ecology, geomorphology, hydrogeology, shoreline processes, environmental issues, and human impact. Not available for marine science major credit. Two laboratory hours per week. Scheduled field trips required.
subject: MSCI

title: Ocean Data Analysis
description: Instrumentation, oceanographic time series, spatial and directional data sets, and basic parametric modeling.
subject: MSCI
prereq: Prerequisites: MSCI 101 and MATH 141.

title: Biology of Marine Organisms
description: Biological concepts emphasizing adaptation to marine environments. Laboratory experiments emphasize principles and techniques of marine biological study. Three lecture and three laboratory hours per week. Scheduled field trips are required.
subject: MSCI
prereq: Prerequisites: MSCI 102 or BIOL 101.

title: The Chemistry of the Sea
description: Biogeochemical cycling, carbonate chemistry, climate change, hydrothermal vents, stable isotopes, trace metals, radioactive tracers, mass balance, and properties of sea water. Three lecture and three laboratory hours per week.
subject: MSCI
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MSCI 101, CHEM 111, CHEM 112, and MATH 141.

title: Physical Oceanography
description: Properties of seawater, mass and momentum balances, circulation, mixing, waves and other processes in the marine environment.
subject: MSCI
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MSCI 101 and MATH 141; C or better in PHYS 201 or PHYS 211.

title: Marine Organic Pollution
description: An exploration of the science behind oil spills, toxic chemicals, microplastics, and other organic pollutants impacting our oceans, from coastlines to the deep-sea. Emphasis on how these contaminants move, transform, and affect marine life and human health, and how cutting-edge science drives legal action and policy change.
subject: MSCI

title: Processes of Global Environmental Change
description: The science of global change, its relation to the hydrosphere, atmosphere, lithosphere, and biosphere. Global system science, biogeochemical cycles, paleoclimatology, glaciation, and eustacy.
subject: MSCI

title: SEOE Peer Mentorship, Leadership, Professional Development
description: Exploration of post-graduation opportunities in academia and industry. Interactive discussion of topics including helping skills, active listening, oral communication, goal setting, interviewing, and building a strong professional portfolio.
subject: MSCI

title: Data Science in Earth, Ocean and Environmental Science
description: Computational analysis of earth, marine, and environmental datasets applying time series analysis, regression, filtering, and statistical analysis.
subject: MSCI
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in CSCE 106 or higher; C or better in MATH 122 or higher.

title: The Deep Sea
description: The Deep Sea is an interdisciplinary, scientific survey of the geology, biology, chemistry, and physical setting of the deep-sea (more than 1000 m depth).
subject: MSCI

title: Policy and Marine Science
description: Analysis of past and current issues in global and national marine policy. Relationship between science and policymakers.
subject: MSCI

title: Independent Study
description: Contract approved by instructor, advisor, and department chair is required for undergraduate students.
subject: MSCI

title: Principles of Biological Oceanography
description: Principles and methods of measuring production in the sea. Emphasis on the ocean’s role in the global carbon budget. Three lecture hours per week. Scheduled field trips are required.
subject: MSCI
prereq: Prerequisites: MSCI 311, BIOL 301.

title: Field and Laboratory Investigations in Marine Science
description: Intensive inquiry-based investigations combining oceanographic field sampling with laboratory measurements of collected samples using modern analytical instrumentation, and with analysis and integration of data into a final research report. Most of the course is conducted in residence at a marine field site.
subject: MSCI
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MSCI 101 and MSCI 102 or C or better in BIOL 101 and 102.

title: Internship in Marine Science
description: Internship experience that offers practical field or laboratory experience in oceanography and/or related marine sciences. Course content varies and will be announced by title in schedule of courses. Usually conducted off campus and student must be able to access internship on their own.
subject: MSCI
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MSCI 311, MSCI 313 and MSCI 314.

title: Undergraduate Research
description: Student research on problems of fundamental significance in collaboration with faculty mentors. Emphasis on critical thinking, problem solving, proposal development, scientific writing, and professional presentation. Nine hours of laboratory, field, or library work per week.
subject: MSCI

title: Undergraduate Research
description: Student research on problems of fundamental significance in collaboration with faculty mentors. Emphasis on critical thinking, problem solving, proposal development, scientific writing, and professional presentation. Nine hours of laboratory, field, or library work per week.
subject: MSCI

title: Undergraduate Research
description: Student research on problems of fundamental significance in collaboration with faculty mentors. Emphasis on critical thinking, problem solving, proposal development, scientific writing, and professional presentation. Nine hours of laboratory, field, or library work per week.
subject: MSCI

title: Undergraduate Research
description: Student research on problems of fundamental significance in collaboration with faculty mentors. Emphasis on critical thinking, problem solving, proposal development, scientific writing, and professional presentation. Nine hours of laboratory, field, or library work per week.
subject: MSCI

title: Principles of Geomorphology
description: The process of earth denudation with emphasis on chemistry of weathering, stream and erosion hydraulics, quantitative analysis of land form evolution.
subject: MSCI
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in GEOL 101.

title: Principles of Coastal Geomorphology
description: Geological and physical controls on the morphology, development, and stability of coastlines. Analysis of waves and erosional processes, and coastal zone morphodynamics. Several required field trips.
subject: MSCI

title: Environmental Microbiology
description: An overview of the microbial world including a survey of the distribution, functioning, and diversity of microorganisms in natural systems. Discusses the crucial roles that microorganisms play in ecosystem function, biogeochemical cycles, and environmental quality.
subject: MSCI
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MSCI 102 or BIOL 102; C or better in CHEM 112.

title: Climate Geoengineering
description: This course will discuss the urgent need for deploying solar radiation and carbon dioxide removal approaches at scale, including potential benefits and risks of these options. It will also discuss regulatory and governance considerations at both the national and international level and strategizes to incentivize large-scale adoption of these approaches.
subject: MSCI

title: Coastal Nature-Based Solutions
description: Application of the principles of ecology with the methods of engineering to design sustainable ecosystems to better integrate human society with the coastal environment.
subject: MSCI

title: MATLAB-Based Data Analysis in Ocean Sciences
description: MATLAB-based course in processing, analysis, and visualization of large oceanographic data sets. Includes scalar and vector time series measured at fixed locations as well as shipboard surveys of oceanographic characteristics varying both in 3-D and in time. Methods and techniques are relevant to other geoscience disciplines.
subject: MSCI
prereq: Prerequisites: MATH 141.

title: Invertebrate Zoology
description: Phylogenetic and comparative aspects of anatomy, physiology, reproduction, and embryology of the invertebrates. Three lecture and one three-hour laboratory period per week.
subject: MSCI
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in BIOL 301 or MSCI 311.

title: Advanced Paleontology
description: Systematic, ecologic, biogeographic, and evolutionary aspects of paleontology; lectures, practical exercises, field trips.
subject: MSCI
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in GEOL 305.

title: Marine Micropaleontology
description: Marine microfossils; distribution, ecology, paleoecology, and biostratigraphy; use of microfossils in marine sediments to study oceanographic history. Three lectures and two laboratory hours per week.
subject: MSCI

title: Introduction to Geochemistry
description: Investigation of low temperature chemical reactions controlling the geochemistry of the earth’s surface. Emphasis on CO2, carbonates, oxidation-reduction, thermodynamics, isotopes, biogeochemistry.
subject: MSCI

title: Environmental Radioisotope Geochemistry
description: Introduction to radioactivity and the use of radionuclides to study environmental processes, including age-dating and biogeochemical cycling in aquatic systems.
subject: MSCI
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in CHEM 111, CHEM 112 and MATH 141.

title: Marine Plants
description: Diversity, distribution, physiology, ecology, evolution, and economic importance of marine algal, seagrass, and mangrove communities. Three lecture and three laboratory hours per week. Scheduled field trips are required.
subject: MSCI
prereq: Prerequisites: BIOL 301 or MSCI 311.

title: Fishery Management
description: Management and conservation of aquatic and marine resources, with emphasis on fisheries. Data procurement and analysis; commercial and recreational fisheries; sociological, political, legal, and environmental factors that affect fishery management; and fish biodiversity.
subject: MSCI
prereq: Prerequisites: BIOL 301.

title: Ichthyology
description: Phylogeny, morphology, behavior, and ecology of fishes. Three lecture and 3 laboratory hours plus three field trips to be arranged.
subject: MSCI
prereq: Prerequisites: BIOL 301 or MSCI 311.

title: Aquaculture
description: Introduction to the practical and scientific aspects of the commercial culture of freshwater and marine organisms. Three lecture hours per week. One all-day field trip required.
subject: MSCI
prereq: Prerequisites: BIOL 301 or MSCI 311.

title: Behavior of Marine Organisms
description: The identification of behavioral adaptations of estuarine and marine organisms: their ecology, physiology, development, and evolutionary history; field observations.
subject: MSCI
prereq: Prerequisites: BIOL 101 and BIOL 102 or MSCI 311.

title: Geological Oceanography
description: A comprehensive study of the origin and development of the major structural features of the ocean basins and the continental margins. Discussion of the techniques used in obtaining geologic data and the interpretation of sedimentary processes, vulcanism, and the stratigraphy of the ocean basins.
subject: MSCI

title: Sedimentary Simulations and Sequence Stratigraphy
description: Problems of sequence stratigraphy resolved with graphic computer simulations. Sedimentary fill of basins by carbonates and/or clastics tracked as a function of rate of sediment accumulation, tectonic behavior, and sea level. Includes laboratory.
subject: MSCI
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in GEOL 325.

title: Population Genetics
description: An introduction to the principles of population genetics, with emphasis on the origin, maintenance, and significance of genetic variation in natural populations.
subject: MSCI
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in BIOL 301 or MSCI 311.

title: Marine Sediments
description: Marine sedimentary environments; physical/biological factors which control the formation and distribution of modern marine sediments.
subject: MSCI
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in GEOL 516.

title: Conservation and Health in Marine Systems
description: Introduces the field of conservation and explores the intersection between conservation and environmental health with a particular focus on coastal and marine case studies.
subject: MSCI

title: Coastal Processes
description: Physical and geological processes controlling the formation and evolution of beach, barrier, and nearshore environments, including discussion of coastal management issues.
subject: MSCI

title: Ecosystem Analysis
description: The formulation and simulation of compartment models of marine and terrestrial ecosystems with complex nutrient cycling, food chains, and energy flow. Analog and digital simulation techniques. Ecosystem stability and sensitivity. Organization, structure, and diversity of an ecosystem.
subject: MSCI

title: Introduction to Micrometeorology
description: Small-scale processes in the atmospheric boundary layers, including energy budget, radiation, soil heat transfer, humidity, viscous flows, turbulence, momentum and heat exchanges, evaporation, and marine atmospheric boundary layer.
subject: MSCI
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in PHYS 201 and MATH 141.

title: Marine Conservation Biology
description: Exploration of how human activities affect marine natural populations, species, communities and ecosystems, including threats to biodiversity; approaches to marine conservation; and ecological and evolutionary responses to anthropogenic disturbance.
subject: MSCI
prereq: Prerequisites: BIOL 301.

title: Marine Ecology
description: Structure, dynamics, and interactions between populations and communities in marine ecosystems. Attendance at designated departmental seminars is required. Three lecture hours per week.
subject: MSCI
prereq: Prerequisites: CHEM 111 and BIOL 301 or MSCI 311.

title: Marine Ecology Laboratory
description: Laboratory and field exercises in coastal environments. Three hours per week plus field trips.
subject: MSCI

title: Marine Fisheries Ecology
description: Interdisciplinary examination of the distribution, reproduction, survival, and historical variation of the principal commercial marine fisheries.
subject: MSCI
prereq: Prerequisites: BIOL 301.

title: Ecology of Coral Reefs
description: Structure, productivity, and biodiversity of coral reefs, emphasizing their sensitivity, stability, and sustainability. Taught as an extended field experience with daily lectures and guided research activities.
subject: MSCI
prereq: Prerequisites: BIOL 301 or MSCI 311.

title: Physiological and Pollution Ecology of Marine Organisms
description: Functional adaptation of marine plants and animals to ecological stresses including pollution. Three lecture hours per week.
subject: MSCI
prereq: Prerequisites: MSCI 311 or equivalent.

title: Air-Sea Interaction
description: The physical mechanism responsible for interaction between the ocean and the atmosphere and the influence of air-sea interaction on atmospheric and oceanic dynamics and thermodynamics on a wide variety of spatial/temporal scales.
subject: MSCI

title: Satellite Oceanography
description: This course provides knowledge of various techniques used in satellite remote sensing of the oceans. Key skills will be developed in satellite data processing, image analysis, and hands-on research.
subject: MSCI

title: Estuarine Oceanography
description: Estuarine kinematics and dynamics; classification of estuaries; estuarine circulation and mixing. Scheduled field trips are required.
subject: MSCI
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MSCI 314.

title: Marine Hydrodynamics
description: Basic principles of fluid statics and dynamics. Conservation of mass, momentum, and energy; viscosity, vorticity, and boundary layers with examples from the marine environment. Applications to and analysis of ocean currents and waves.
subject: MSCI
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MATH 241 and C or better in either PHYS 201 or PHYS 211.

title: Geology and Geochemistry of Salt Marshes
description: Geological and geochemical processes in salt marshes. Methods of geological research in marshes including instrumental techniques, sampling design, and data analysis. Two lectures per week plus four weekends of project oriented fieldwork and/or equivalent lab work. Scheduled field trips are required.
subject: MSCI

title: Coastal Tropical Oceanography
description: Descriptive oceanography of mangrove and coral reef coasts with emphasis on physical processes. Taught as an extended field experience with daily lectures and guided research activities.
subject: MSCI
prereq: Prerequisites: MSCI 312.

title: Beach-Dune Interactions
description: Influence of wind on coastal systems, with emphasis on nearshore currents, sediment transport and bedforms, aeolian transport, and dunes. Minimum Junior standing required.
subject: MSCI

title: Topics in Marine Science
description: Current developments in marine science selected to meet faculty and student interests. Course content varies and will be announced by title in schedule of courses.
subject: MSCI

title: Marine Phytoplankton
description: Examines the physiology and ecology of phytoplankton, including environmental controls on community composition, primary productivity, and detection and characterization of water quality (eutrophication) and harmful algal blooms.
subject: MSCI
prereq: Prerequisites: MSCI 102 or MSCI 450 or BIOL 450.

title: Coastal Nature-Based Solutions
description: Application of the principles of ecology with the methods of engineering to design sustainable ecosystems to better integrate human society with the coastal environment.
subject: MSCI

title: Marine Data Science with R
description: This course provides a hands-on, project-oriented investigation of current approaches for research in marine science, ecology and environmental science. Components of the course will include exploratory data analyses, statistics, graphics and the R programming language. Prior programming experience is beneficial, but not required.
subject: MSCI

title: Paleoclimatology
description: An overview of Earth’s climate history during Cenozoic. Emphasis will be placed on Pleistocene glacial-interglacial climate variability and understanding the proxies used to reconstruct past climate changes.
subject: MSCI

title: Eustasy and Global Variations in Sequence Stratigraphy
description: Relationship of sequence stratigraphy to sea level variations, tectonics and sedimentation. Construction and analyses of paleogeographic maps, regional cross-sections, and chronostratigraphic charts.
subject: MSCI

title: Organic Geochemistry
description: Sources, transport, and fate of organic matter in natural environments including soils, riverine, estuarine, coastal and open ocean sediments and waters.
subject: MSCI
prereq: Prerequisites: GEO 521L/MSCI 521.

title: Geological Oceanography
description: A comprehensive study of the origin and development of the major structural features of the ocean basins and the continental margins. Discussion of the techniques used in obtaining geologic data and the interpretation of sedimentary processes, vulcanism, and the stratigraphy of the ocean basins.
subject: MSCI

title: Advanced Biological Oceanography
description: Three lecture hours per week.
subject: MSCI
prereq: Prerequisites: BIOL 450/MSCI 450.

title: Marine Biogeochemistry
description: Biological, geological, and physical processes that influence the cyling of major bioactive elements (C, O, N, P, S) in marine waters and sediments.
subject: MSCI

title: Marine Conservation and Environmental Health
description: Explores the intersection between conservation and environmental health with a particular focus on coastal and marine case studies.
subject: MSCI

title: Ecological Modeling and Environmental Planning
description: Concepts in systems ecology and ecological modeling. Emphasis on the use of models and computer simulations in examining environmental interactions, predicting environmental impact, and facilitating the process of environmental planning. Lab practice in model development and computer simulation analysis.
subject: MSCI
prereq: Prerequisites: MATH 111 or equivalent, ecology, ENHS 660.

title: Reproductive Ecology
description: Theoretical aspects and examples of the variety of reproductive and life history patterns found in animals and plants as adaptations to various environmental constraints. Three lecture hours per week.
subject: MSCI
prereq: Prerequisites: BIOL 570.

title: Current Topics in Marine Ecology for Teachers
description: Primarily for teachers. Marine science materials with emphasis on coastal environments. Field exercises.
subject: MSCI

title: Current Topics in Marine Ecology for Teachers
description: Primarily for teachers. Marine science materials with emphasis on coastal environments. Field exercises.
subject: MSCI

title: Physical Oceanography
description: Geographic and hydrodynamic aspects of oceanography, with emphasis on estuaries. Physical properties of sea water and theories and methods involved in ocean currents, air-sea interaction, waves, and tides.
subject: MSCI

title: Chemical Oceanography
description: Chemical characteristics of sea water, distribution of properties, and chemical processes in the oceans, with emphasis on estuaries.
subject: MSCI

title: Oceanographic Time Series Analysis
description: Techniques in the analysis of oceanographic data sequences, including filtering techniques, fast Fourier transformers, and empirical orthogonal functions.
subject: MSCI

title: Geophysical Fluid Dynamics
description: Equations governing the large-scale dynamics of the atmosphere and ocean, rotational influence, shallow water equations, vorticity, quasi-geostrophic dynamics, Rossby waves, energy and enstrophy, and geostrophic turbulence.
subject: MSCI
prereq: Prerequisites: MATH 241 or ECIV 360 or GEOL 582/MSCI 582 or GEOL 781/MSCI 781.

title: Atmospheric Dynamics
description: Elementary applications of the basic equations, scale analysis, planetary boundary layer, atmospheric oscillations, synoptic and mesoscale systems, hydrodynamic instability, cyclogenesis, frontogenesis, energy cycle, momentum budget, and tropical motion systems.
subject: MSCI

title: Directed Individual Studies in Marine Sciences
