description: Directed research topics to be individually assigned and supervised by graduate faculty.
subject: MSCI

title: Issues in Coastal Environmental Health
description: Problems associated with coastal population growth and development. Emphasis is on the working group approach to ameliorating impacts on ecosystem and human health.
subject: MSCI

title: Research in Marine Science
description: In depth research methods and techniques in preparation of thesis or dissertation.
subject: MSCI

title: Marine Science Seminar
description: Advanced topics in geoscience research presented in departmental seminar format. Contains a mix of marine science, geological science, and environmental science content.
subject: MSCI

description: MUED 104A -violin; MUED 104B -viola; MUED 104C -cello; MUED 104D -string bass; MUED 104P -guitar. Fundamentals of playing and teaching string instruments in a homogeneous class. Special study of problems unique to the instrument relating to care and repair, tone production, bowing, articulation, fingering, intonation, and teaching materials. One class meeting per week. May be repeated for credit.

description: Secondary Strings-Violin. Fundamentals of playing and teaching string instruments in a homogeneous class. title: Classroom Instruments
description: Class instruction in guitar, recorder, non-pitched and pitched percussion, with emphasis on the development of performance skills, the understanding of pedagogical techniques and materials, and the ability to play and teach these instruments in a group setting.
subject: MUED

description: A course for beginning piano students meeting in groups. Emphasis on music reading and elementary techniques. Two meetings and two laboratory periods a week. Repeatable once for credit.
subject: MUED

title: Group Piano
description: A course for beginning piano students meeting in groups. Emphasis on music reading and elementary techniques. Two meetings and two laboratory periods a week. Repeatable once for credit.
subject: MUED
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MUED 155 or advanced placement via exam.

title: Class Voice (Basic)
description: Elementary courses in singing in which both group and individual techniques are employed. Study of voice production and principles of singing.
subject: MUED

title: Class Voice (Basic)
description: Elementary courses in singing in which both group and individual techniques are employed. Study of voice production and principles of singing.
subject: MUED

description: Practicum experiences in various types of public school music settings. Seminars and group discussions included. Pass-Fail credit.
subject: MUED

title: Class Voice (Intermediate)
description: Continued study of the principles of singing and song repertoire.
subject: MUED
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MUED 165, MUED 166, or preparation satisfactory to the instructor.

title: Class Voice (Intermediate)
description: Continued study of the principles of singing and song repertoire.
subject: MUED
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MUED 165, MUED 166, or preparation satisfactory to the instructor.

description: Practical application of choral conducting skills and rehearsal techniques with choral literature.
subject: MUED

description: Continued application of choral conducting skills and rehearsal techniques with choral literature.
subject: MUED

description: Group piano for music education students emphasizing choral music. Opportunities for accompanying instrumentalists, vocalists, and choral groups. Repeatable once for credit.
subject: MUED
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MUED 156 or advanced placement via exam.

title: Advanced Group Piano
description: Group piano for music education students emphasizing choral music. Opportunities for accompanying instrumentalists, vocalists, and choral groups. Repeatable once for credit.
subject: MUED
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MUED 355 or advanced placement via exam.

title: Wind Pedagogy I
description: Fundamentals of playing and teaching wind instruments in a heterogeneous class. Study of basic principles regarding embouchure, tone production, articulation, fingering, and teaching materials.
subject: MUED

title: Strings
description: Fundamentals of playing and teaching string instruments in a heterogeneous class. Problems unique to each instrument relating to care and repair, tone production, bowing, fingering, and teaching materials.
subject: MUED
prereq: Prerequisites: Passing grade in MUED 200.

title: Instrumental Techniques for Choral Majors
description: Techniques for leading an instrumental or mixed choral/instrumental ensemble. Instrumental tone, maintenance, score-reading skills, transposition, and rehearsal methods. Not auditable.
subject: MUED

title: Percussion Techniques
description: Fundamentals of playing and teaching percussion instruments. Special study of problems unique to each percussion instrument and related teaching materials.
subject: MUED
prereq: Prerequisites: Passing grade in MUED 200.

title: Percussion Practicum
description: Practicum experiences teaching percussion in various types of school and community music settings.
subject: MUED
prereq: Prerequisites: Satisfactory in MUED 200.

title: Music for Young Children
description: Examination and practical application of methods, techniques, and materials for teaching music, Pre-K to Grade 5.
subject: MUED

title: Band Literature and Materials
description: A study of teaching materials, solo- and small-ensemble literature, and full band ensemble literature appropriate for grades 5-12.
subject: MUED

title: Technology for Music Teachers
description: Familiarization with computer software and hardware for the teaching of music and administration of music programs.
subject: MUED

title: General Music in Elementary Schools
description: Discussion of sequential music development; implementation of methods, techniques, and materials for teaching music in grades K-5.
subject: MUED
prereq: Prerequisites: Passing grade in MUED 200.

title: Practicum in Elementary Music
description: Practical application of elementary methods and techniques studied in school settings.
subject: MUED

title: General Music in Secondary Schools
description: Middle-school and high-school general music education philosophy, teaching techniques, music objectives, curriculum development, administration, facilities, and equipment.
subject: MUED
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in EDUC 300.

title: Choral Methods and Materials
description: Procedures and materials for choral instruction applicable to elementary, middle-school, and high-school choruses; basic concepts of choral tone and vocal development.
subject: MUED
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MUSC 218 and MUED 356; a passing grade in MUED 200.

title: Practicum in Choral Music
description: Practical application of choral methods and techniques in school settings. Not auditable.
subject: MUED

title: Marching Band Techniques
description: Fundamentals of marching, maneuvering, and formations; special features such as drum majoring and auxiliary units; discussion and planning of halftime shows.
subject: MUED

title: Directed Teaching (Music)
description: A clinical field experience in the public school setting. Note: Prior to enrolling in MUED 477, students must complete the following: 1) Admission to the Professional Program, and 2) All MUED courses, and conducting courses are required for the student’s specific degree track. Courses must be passed with Grade of “C” or better. Students are strongly encouraged to complete all MUSC courses prior to the student teaching semester for optimum preparation for the pre-service internship. No additional course work may be taken during the Directed Teaching semester without permission of the School of Music Undergraduate Director.
subject: MUED

title: Orchestra Methods I
description: Fundamentals of teaching orchestral stringed instruments in school string and orchestra classes. Emphasis on sequential instruction, materials, and classroom management.
subject: MUED
prereq: Prerequisites: Passing grade in MUED 200 and C or better in three semesters selected from MUED 104A - MUED 104D.

title: Practicum in Orchestra Methods I
description: Practical application of string methods and materials in public and community school settings. Not auditable.
subject: MUED

title: Orchestra Methods II
description: Continued study of methods, materials, and concepts of teaching orchestral stringed instruments in school string and orchestra classes. Emphasis on rehearsal techniques and curricula.
subject: MUED

title: Practicum in Orchestra Methods II
description: Practical application of string methods and materials in public and community school settings. Not auditable
subject: MUED

description: Study of teaching materials, methods, literature, and educational practices used in middle school band settings.
subject: MUED
prereq: Prerequisites: Passing grade in MUED 200 and C or better in two semesters selected from MUSC 101, MUED 104A-MUED 104D, MUED 105A-MUED 105P or MUED 106A-MUED 106P, or graduate standing.

description: Study of teaching materials, methods, literature, and educational practices used in high school band settings.
subject: MUED

subject: MUED

subject: MUED

title: Wind Pedagogy II
description: Continued study of the issues in playing and teaching wind instruments in a heterogeneous class. Special study of problems unique to each woodwind and brass instrument regarding fingering and intonation.
subject: MUED

title: Arraning for the Marching Band
description: Instruction and practice in arranging music for the marching band.
subject: MUED

title: String Instrument Pedagogy
description: Principles and practices in teaching string instruments, including Suzuki and Rolland. Emphasis on teaching in the private studio.
subject: MUED

title: Specialized Elementary Music Methods
description: Advanced study of Orff, Kodaly, Dalcroze, and Gordon music learning theories as applied in elementary schools.
subject: MUED
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MUED 465.

title: Organization and Administration of Music Programs
description: Topics include materials and techniques of class teaching, equipment purchase, budgeting, recruiting, public relations, and the music library.
subject: MUED
prereq: Prerequisites: passing grade in MUED 200, or equivalent.

title: Practicum in Instrumental Music
description: Practical application of instrumental methods and techniques in school settings. Not auditable.
subject: MUED

title: Independent Study in Music Education
description: This course is designed for graduate students who wish to pursue a study of various areas of music education according to their particular needs.
subject: MUED

title: Teaching Internship in Music
description: This course is designed to provide a full semester of field experience in the public school for Master of Arts in Teaching (music ) degree participants. Not Auditable.
subject: MUED
prereq: Prerequisites: Acceptance into Professional Program in Education.

title: Music Teaching Internship Seminar
description: A series of seminars designed to enhance the student teaching experience. Topics include classroom management, lesson planning, assessment, and curriculum development.
subject: MUED
prereq: Prerequisites: MUED 731 and Acceptance into Professional Program in Education.

title: Psychology of Music
description: Study of the physical factors involved with the production and perception of musical sound; the tests for measuring music aptitude and achievement; the psychology of learning applied to music.
subject: MUED

title: Instrumental Development and Related Materials
description: Detailed study and analysis of techniques used for the development of the instrumental program in the public schools; diagnosis of problems relating to strings, woodwinds, brass, and percussion; study and evaluation of literature and materials for orchestras suitable for middle and high schools.
subject: MUED

title: Music Administration and Curriculum
description: Concepts of and trends and practices in supervision; the place of music in the total program of education; consideration of current types of administrative organizations; study of administrative problems including scheduling and budgeting; consideration of effective music programs in city and county school systems; in-service education and workshop techniques; school and community relationships.
subject: MUED

title: Choral Develoment and Related Materials

subject: MUED

title: Seminar in Elementary School Music
description: A survey and evaluation of music book series for the elementary and middle school; an investigation of contemporary developments in pedagogy and in the application of research findings; and the formulation of a topical bibliography.
subject: MUED

title: Advanced Brass Pedagogy
description: Pedagogical methods and techniques of brass instruction, and a survey of music literature appropriate for intermediate through advanced level.
subject: MUED

title: Advanced Woodwind Pedagogy
description: An investigation of pedagogical methods and techniques of woodwind instruction through performance, and a survey of music literature appropriate for intermediate through advanced levels.
subject: MUED

subject: MUED

title: Band Literature
description: History and literature of the wind band/ensemble.
subject: MUED

title: Measurement and Evaluation of Music Learning

subject: MUED

subject: MUED

subject: MUED

title: Topics in Music Education
description: Students will immerse themselves in the various societal issues that impact music teaching and learning.
subject: MUED

title: New Directions in Music Education
description: Current trends in music education and an examination of how these issues affect music teaching and learning.
subject: MUED

title: Research in Music Education and Pedagogy

subject: MUED

description: Survey and critical analysis of research in music education, culminating in the presentation and defense of individual research proposals.
subject: MUED

subject: MUED

Pass/Fail grading only.
subject: MUSC

description: An introduction to advocacy methods, techniques and experiences to understand the positive effects of music in American society. Pass/Fail Grading.
subject: MUSC

title: Recital Class Laboratory
description: Introduction to the literature and styles of western music. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC

subject: MUSC

title: Introduction to Beat Making and Digital Audio Production
description: Practice in basic audio production (signal-flow, digital audio workstations, plug-ins, virtual instruments, effects) and beat making (audio production in a digital workstation using samples, loops, and virtual instruments). Concepts of music production and prominent styles in beat-driven music.
subject: MUSC
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MUSC 114 or a passing score on the Music Theory Diagnostic Exam.

title: Introduction to Digital Music Creation
description: Research, analysis, and creation of digital music using techniques in commercial and production music including DAW, MIDI, samples, effects, and more. In each learning module, students will read about, listen to, and analyze model recordings in specific subgenres of commercial, production, and art music before creating their own short music track. At the end of the semester students will research, plan, and create a final project based on their interests. Topics include Introduction to DAW, MIDI signal flow; Virtual instruments samples; The studio as instrument, Notation; Rhythm, groove, micro-timing, beats; Building full textures Orchestration; Music for advertising; Underscoring for TV film; Video game music, vamps, cycles; Sound design; and Electro-acoustic mixture.
subject: MUSC
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MUSC 115.

title: Keyboard Harmony and Sight Reading Laboratory
description: Functional keyboard skills in sight reading, harmonization, transposition, and improvisation.
subject: MUSC

title: Beginning Piano Accompanying
description: Approaches to specific problems in vocal and instrumental accompanying; supervised accompanying in class. Repeatable for credit.
subject: MUSC
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MUSC 268.

title: Introduction to Singer's Diction

title: Introduction to Music Industry Studies
description: Overview of the music industry, including exploration of a variety of music industry career paths in areas such as arts management, music products and merchandising, public relations, music production and recording, publishing, online music distribution, and live music event organization.
subject: MUSC

title: Music Composition II
description: Intermediate level composition class.
subject: MUSC
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MUSC 316.

title: Special Topics in Musicology and Ethnomusicology
description: An exploration of methodologies in music research, investigating any combination of historical inquiries, including archival research and score studies; ethnographic approaches; and modern techniques such as sound studies, field recordings, critical theory, and digital humanities.
subject: MUSC
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MUSC 354.

title: Musical Theatre Workshop
description: Intensive musical theatre training in areas of song interpretation, musical theatre, dance, voice and acting.
subject: MUSC

title: History of Western Music III
description: Western music from ca. 1860 until the present time, considering musical styles, genre and forms, and the contributions of composers through historical, analytical, and musical perspectives. Subdiscipline, Music - History and Literature.
subject: MUSC
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MUSC 354.

title: Record Production Techniques
description: Capstone in record production techniques.
subject: MUSC
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MUSC 567.

title: Concert Planning, Promotion and Production
description: Planning, production, and promotion of a weekly concert series on campus.
subject: MUSC
prereq: Prerequisites: C or higher in MUSC 582 or MUSC 593.

title: Greene Street Records Capstone
description: Operation and management of the student-run record label, Greene Street Records.
subject: MUSC
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MUSC 305 and MUSC 588.

title: Music Festival Capstone
description: Supervised experience at a large music festival in the fields of live audio, marketing, planning, promotion, operations, and safety.
subject: MUSC
prereq: Prerequisites: C or higher in MUSC 305; C or higher in MUSC 582 or MUSC 593; or instructor permission.

title: Music Industry Capstone
description: Culmination of Music Industry Studies with a focus on experiential learning or research, supervised by a faculty member. Studio Portfolio materials will be gathered to aid in matriculation to higher education or careers in the music industry. Can be taken twice for a credit for a maximum of six credit hours.
subject: MUSC
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MUSC 305; C or better in one of the following: MUSC 580, MUSC 582, or MUSC 593; successful review of a Music Industry Studies portfolio.

title: Topics in Performance and Literature

title: Orchestration
description: Instrumentation and orchestration; possibilities and limitations idiomatic to specific orchestral instruments and ensemble combinations. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC
prereq: Prerequisites: Undergraduate Students: C or better in MUSC 215; Graduate Students: Passing score on the Music Theory portion of the Music Graduate Diagnostic Exam or a grade of C or better in MUSC 523.

title: Composition
description: Private study in music composition; may be repeated. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MUSC 417 or equivalent experience.

title: Form and Analysis
description: Examination of fundamental principles of musical forms; analysis of representative tonal works. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC
prereq: Prerequisites: Undergraduate Students: C or better in MUSC 215; Graduate Students: a passing score on the music theory portion of the Graduate Music Diagnostic Exam or a grade of C or better in MUSC 523.

title: Studio Arranging and Composition
description: Instruction and practice in arranging and composing in various jazz and commercial music styles and genres. May be repeated for credit. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC

title: Techniques and Materials of Tonal Music
description: Study of the techniques and materials of tonal harmony and voice leading with integrated ear-training component. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC

title: Jazz MIDI
description: Advanced study of production, recording, and composing techniques via a DAW for the creation of collaborative musical projects, with an emphasis on home recording, creating notated song arrangements/orchestrations with virtual instruments, and managing audio projects in a remote/online environment.
subject: MUSC
prereq: Prerequisites: Undergraduate: C or better in MUSC 219 and MUSC 319, or instructor permission; Graduate: None.

title: Post-Tonal Music Theory
description: Studies in post-tonal theoretical techniques and analysis. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC
prereq: Prerequisites: Undergraduate Students: C or better in MUSC 215; Graduate students: passing score on the Music Theory portion of the Music Graduate Diagnostic Exam or a grade of C or better in MUSC 523.

title: Analytical Studies
description: Analytical techniques applied to music of a particular style, period, or genre. May be repeated for a total of 9 credits. Topic for any semester to be announced by title in the schedule of classes. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC
prereq: Prerequisites: Undergraduate Students: C or better in MUSC 215; Graduate Students: Passing score on the Music Theory portion of the Music Graduate Diagnostic Exam or a grade of C or better in MUSC 523.

title: Seminar in Music Theory
description: Advanced studies in music theory. Individual projects. Three meetings per week. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC
prereq: Prerequisites: Undergraduate Students: C or better in MUSC 215; Graduate Students: Passing score on the Music Theory portion of the Music Graduate Diagnostic Exam or a grade of C or better in MUSC 523.

title: Projects in Computer Music
description: Directed study in computer-music composition or research. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MUSC 336.

title: Music History for Educators
description: This online course focuses on the histories and contexts of Western art music, non-Western musics, popular, and American vernacular traditions. Methodologies for studying and applying these traditions for general education will overlap with current theories of music history pedagogy.
subject: MUSC

title: Song Literature
description: A study of the development of the solo art song, illustrated by major works of the song writers of all major nationalities. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC

title: Topics in Music History
description: Topic for any semester to be announced by title in the schedule of classes. May be repeated for a total of nine credits. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC

title: Survey of the Opera
description: A survey of the literature of Classic, Romantic, and modern opera, with special attention given to the recognition of the best-known works in each school. Recordings. Open to all students as an elective. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC

title: Black Sacred Music: Local and Global Perspectives
description: A socio-cultural survey of the Black sacred music tradition from its roots in West Africa to its emergence in African American oral culture through various styles of sacred and secular music in the US and abroad.
subject: MUSC
prereq: Prerequisites: Undergraduate Students: C or better in MUSC 354; Graduate Students: None.

title: Orchestra Literature
description: Symphony, concerto, tone poem, and other symphonic works from the Classic, Romantic, and modern repertory. Changing concepts of orchestration. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC

title: Survey of Chamber Music
description: The literature for small instrumental ensembles from the Baroque era to the present. The string quartet, divertimento, keyboard-accompanied sonata, etc. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC

title: World Music
description: Rhythms, scales, forms, and instrument types basic to all music. European and American folk song, African and Native American tribal music, Asian music. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MUSC 110 or equivalent.

title: American Music
description: Survey of the music composed in the United States from the colonial period to the present. The influences of European, African, Indian, and South American musical styles. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC

title: Piano Literature I
description: Standard piano literature including major masterpieces from 1700 to 1850. Emphasis on instrumental and stylistic developments, and historical and theoretical background for interpreting the literature of the piano. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC

title: Piano Literature II
description: Standard piano literature including major masterpieces from 1850 to present . Emphasis on instrumental and stylistic developments, and historical and theoretical background for interpreting the literature of the piano. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC

title: Introduction to Teaching Artistry
description: An exploration of the widely held understanding of the term teaching artist.
subject: MUSC

title: Teaching Methods and Practicum for Teaching Artists
description: The design, creation, and implementation of an effective classroom curriculum.
subject: MUSC

title: Advanced Audio Recording Techniques
description: Multi-microphone and ambisonic stereo recording techniques; multi-track recording, signal processing and audio production; digital audio. Studio and field experience. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MUSC 365.

title: Fundamentals of Sound Use for Media
description: Music for use in media; midi applications and synchronization methods using time code; direct-to-hard-disc tapeless audio recording software. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MUSC 565.

title: Recording Studio Techniques
description: Technology and techniques in the recording studio including use of equalizers, limiters, reverberators, compressors, the mixing console, multi-track recording, microphone techniques, and basic acoustics related to the instrumental and vocal recording process. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MUSC 565.

title: Intermediate Piano Accompanying
description: Approaches to specific problems in vocal and instrumental accompanying; supervised accompanying in class. Advanced work for experienced students. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC

title: Italian and Latin Diction
description: Techniques of pronunciation, phonetics, and international phonetic alphabet as applied to standard vocal repertory, with emphasis on Italian and Latin languages. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC

title: Digital Audio Technology
description: A study of the theory and practice of digital audio technology including analog to digital conversion, digital storage, error correction, transmission, basic digital signal processing, and synchronization.
subject: MUSC
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MUSC 365.

title: Advanced Audio Topics
description: A study of the theory and practice of audio topics such as digital signal processing, psychoacoustics, data compression, sound reinforcement systems, wireless transmission, large scale system integration, and emerging technologies.
subject: MUSC
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MUSC 565.

title: Performance Pedagogy I
description: Basic concepts, techniques and materials for teaching a specific instrument. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC

title: Pedagogy Laboratory
description: Directed teaching in laboratory and private settings. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC

title: Performance Pedagogy II
description: Basic concepts, techniques and materials for teaching a specific instrument intermediate studies. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MUSC 573.

title: Pedagogy Laboratory
description: Directed teaching in laboratory and private settings. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MUSC 573L.

title: Directed Teaching in Pedagogy I
description: Supervised teaching in a performance area. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC

title: Pedagogy Laboratory
description: Directed teaching in laboratory and private settings. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC

title: Teaching in Pedagogy II
description: Supervised teaching in a performance area. Course may be repeated for credit (6 credits total). Not auditable.
subject: MUSC

title: Pedagogy Laboratory
description: Practical experience in preparing lesson plans and teaching theory-performance classes for precollege piano students on electronic and acoustical instruments. May be repeated for credit. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC

title: Vocal Pedagogy
description: Anatomy and function of the singing voice with practical application to teaching. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC

title: German and English Diction
description: Techniques of pronunciation for singing in German and English. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MUSC 570.

title: French Diction
description: Techniques of pronunciation for singing in French. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MUSC 570.

title: Music Arts Entrepreneurship
description: Entrepreneurial skills and context for arts-based careers and business ventures. Students develop arts projects related to their interests.
subject: MUSC

title: The Alexander Technique
description: Theoretical/experimental exploration of major body systems and developmental movements to bring more articulation to the body and more awareness and physical ease in performance. For music and education students. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC

title: Music and Money
description: A survey of the for-profit and non-profit music economies and the broader policy that drives these economies. Restricted to Music majors.
subject: MUSC

title: Music and Worship
description: The selection and leadership of music in the church service; music for the rural church; selecting and directing anthems and service music for the nonprofessional church choir; the transition from psalmody to hymnody in the 18th century; the Anglican Chant and the Lutheran Chorale. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC

title: Workshop in Music
description: Selected topics in music. May be repeated as topic varies. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC

title: Organ Literature I
description: Organ literature and registration from antiquity to 1750. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC

title: Organ Literature II
description: Organ literature and registration 1750 to the present. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC

title: Repertories of Lute, Vihuela, and Guitar
description: Solo literature for plucked, fretted instruments from the Renaissance into the 20th century. National styles, traits, and technical innovations included. Not auditable. Admission to upper division guitar study.
subject: MUSC

title: Business of Music
description: This course analyzes the forms and structures of both major and independent record labels. Course concepts will be reinforced with case studies, journal articles, and guest speakers. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC

title: Arts Management
description: Management techniques for organizations with a musical component such as: orchestra, opera, ballet, artist series. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC

title: Seminar in Music Industry
description: Survey of current events and topics within the music industry, and analyses of music businesses through the use of case studies.
subject: MUSC

title: Music Leadership Practicum
description: A practicum to design and execute an entrepreneurial music leadership project in Columbia, South Carolina. Restricted to Music majors.
subject: MUSC
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in MUSC 580 and MUSC 582.

title: 21st Century Performer
description: Issues confronting the professional performing musician. Topics will include performance-based income models and opportunities, program development, and promotional and supporting materials. Restricted to School of Music students.
subject: MUSC

title: Arts Marketing
description: Arts marketing program challenges, arts organizations, building the successful private studio, marketing plans, social media and guerilla marketing, and market research.
subject: MUSC

title: Independent Music Teaching Business
description: A study of all aspects of the creation and maintenance of a viable independent music teaching business. Restricted to School of Music students.
subject: MUSC

title: Community Engagement Through Music
description: Community engagement as it relates to music, with a focus on developing practical skills in creating engaging, interactive performances for various audiences.
subject: MUSC

title: Music Business Internship
description: Supervised work experience as approved by area program director. May be repeated up to 6 credits. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC

title: Independent Study
description: This course is designed for graduate students who wish to pursue a study of various areas of music according to their particular needs. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC

title: Music Bibliography and Research
description: Reference works, discographies, periodicals, thematic catalogs, and other sources. Research methods and materials. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC

title: Vocal Coaching
description: Advanced study of stylistic, interpretive, linguistic, and other performance issues in solo operatic, oratorio, and recital repertoire for singers. Repeatable for credit. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC

title: Graduate Applied/Double Bass
description: Note: Courses consist of individual instruction including individualized assignments and regular private instruction and review. A-flute/piccolo; B-oboe/English horn; C-clarinet; D-bassoon; E-saxophone; F-French horn; G-trumpet; H-trombone; I-euphonium; J-tuba; K-percussion; L-harpsichord; N-classic guitar; O-organ; P-piano; R-violin; S-viola; T-violoncello; U-double bass; V-voice; W-service playing; Y-conducting; Z-jazz. Subdiscipline: Music (Applied Music)
subject: MUSC

title: Graduate Applied Music/ Voice
description: Course consists of individual instruction including individualized assignments and regular private instruction and review. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC

title: Graduate Applied Music/ Service Playing
description: Course consists of individual instruction including individualized assignments and regular private instruction and review. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC

title: Graduate Applied Music/ Conducting
description: Course consists of individual instruction including individualized assignments and regular private instruction and review. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC

title: Graduate Applied Music/Jazz
description: Course consists of individual instruction including individualized assignments and regular private instruction and review. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC

title: Analysis of Popular Music
description: This course explores analytical and interpretive approaches to urban popular music from 1950s, around the birth of rock, going well into the 21st century. “Popular music” here embraces many different repertories like rock, pop, hip-hop, R B, soul, and top-40 music, and is ultimately connected to Anglo-American mainstream music.
subject: MUSC

title: Advanced Jazz Theory
description: Harmonic, melodic, rhythmic, and structural concepts related to advanced jazz improvisation and composition. In-depth analysis of selected improvisations of artists and contemporary jazz styles.
subject: MUSC

title: Advanced Jazz Arranging
description: Advanced orchestration, compositional development, cross-ensemble writing, extended forms, expanded harmonic and rhythmic vocabulary in the various contemporary jazz styles.
subject: MUSC
prereq: Prerequisites: MUSC 520 or equivalent.

title: Analysis and Performance
description: Performance and interpretive considerations through the study of music on a technical basis. Observations of unity/variety and tension/repose, the interaction of music's materials, and the sources for growth and shape. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC

title: Composition
description: Original composition. May be repeated for credit. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC

title: Advanced Orchestration
description: Advanced orchestral arranging and score study, principles or score identification, and historical survey of orchestration practices and styles.
subject: MUSC

title: Public Music Theory
description: An introduction to the methods and media of public music theory including community engagement.
subject: MUSC

title: Survey of Analytical Techniques
description: An introduction to a variety of analytical methodologies and topics, including, but not limited to, Schenkerian analysis, Schoenbergian analysis, phrase rhythm, hypermeter, metric dissonance, topic theory, transformation theory. The course may be taken by master’s and doctoral students in music to fulfill theory or elective requirements.
subject: MUSC

title: Pedagogy of Music Theory
description: Concepts, techniques, and materials for teaching music theory. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC

title: Tonality in the Twentieth Century
description: Study of the theory and practice of tonality in the twentieth century, including late nineteenth-century chromaticism, modality, collectional music, polytonality, neo- Riemannian theory, and minimalism. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC

title: Music and Repetition
description: This course is a study of musical styles that rely heavily on repetition focusing on popular music, EDM, traditional musics from around the world, and minimalist and post-minimalist music from the last fifty years through reading, research, analysis, composition, and performance.
subject: MUSC

title: Style and Technique Since 1900
description: A study of the style and techniques of 20th and 21st-century composition through listening, research, analysis, and composition. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC

title: Topics in Music Theory
description: Analytical, critical, interpretative, and/or theoretical study that focuses on a particular style, period, genre, topic, and/or approach. Repeatable up to five times. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC

title: Schenkerian Analysis
description: Study of analytical concepts developed by Heinrich Schenker, and their application to analysis of tonal music.
subject: MUSC

title: Contrapuntal Techniques
description: Contrapuntal procedures from the 16th through the 20th centuries.
subject: MUSC

title: Contemporary Experimental Music
description: Survey of iconoclastic and visionary contemporary composers whose music and ideas fall mostly outside mainstream classical and popular genres.
subject: MUSC

title: Music Modernism
description: Study of musical modernism in the late 19th and early 20th centuries.
subject: MUSC

title: Advanced Conducting
description: Study of conducting problems in selected choral or instrumental works. 733A-choral; 733B-instrumental. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC
prereq: Prerequisites: One year of study in conducting.

title: Advanced Conducting/ Choral
description: Study of conducting problems in selected choral or instrumental works. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC
prereq: Prerequisites: One year of study in conducting or approval of instructor.

title: Advanced Conducting/ Instrumental
description: Study of conducting problems in selected choral or instrumental works. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC
prereq: Prerequisites: One year of study in conducting or approval of instructor.

description: Experience performing in a chamber music ensemble. Repeatable for credit. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC

title: Conductors Institute
description: Practicum in conducting and literature. Limited to participants in the Conductors Institute.
subject: MUSC

title: Advanced Projects in Computer Music
description: Advanced computer-music research or computer-generated tape composition.
subject: MUSC

title: Music Cognition
description: Study of the cognition of music. The field of music cognition explores how and why we make sense of music, ranging from the most basic perceptual aspects (e.g., perception of pitch and rhythm) to higher cognitive, cultural, and social processes (e.g., expectation, taste, and emotion).
subject: MUSC

title: Transcription and Analysis
description: Study of analysis through transcription of music. Genres covered include roots, folk, traditional, world, jazz, and popular.
subject: MUSC

title: Music Literature
description: Study of the literature of a specific performance area. Repeatable for credit, 6 hrs max. Not auditable.
subject: MUSC

title: Seminar in Music History
description: For graduate students in music and music education. Methods of historical research, and problems of aesthetics and criticism.
subject: MUSC

title: Topics in Music History
description: Selected topics such as French music in the 19th century, Richard Wagner, and mensural notation. May be repeated as the topic varies; 12 hrs max.
subject: MUSC

title: Music and Culture of Tudor England
description: An examination of the music and culture of Tudor England (c. 1458-1603).
subject: MUSC
prereq: Prerequisites: Passing score on the music history portion of the Graduate Music Diagnostic Exam.

title: Choral Literature
description: Choral literature from the Renaissance to the present. Study of smaller forms in the context of composers’ contributions to the literature.
subject: MUSC

title: Advanced Music Research
description: Development of advanced music research skills. Preparation for writing graduate research projects.
subject: MUSC
prereq: Prerequisites: MUSC 707.

title: Major Choral Works
description: A survey of major oratorios, cantatas, and masses.
subject: MUSC

title: Introduction to Ethnomusicology: History, Theory, Methods, and Education
description: Multifaceted overview of the field of ethnomusicology, world music teaching resources, ethnographic research methods, and contemporary scholarship in the field. Understanding of the broader role music plays in society as it relates to ethnicity, gender, class/caste, religion, cultural identity, politics, nationalism, cross-cultural aesthetics, tradition, change, musical innovation, and globalization.
subject: MUSC

title: Renaissance Music
description: Music from 1400 to 1600, including an introductory study on polyphonic styles of the later Medieval period; evolution of musical styles within the context of Renaissance Western European culture.
subject: MUSC

title: Music of the Baroque
description: Music from 1600 to 1750 including representative composers from Monteverdi’s generation through Bach and Handel.
subject: MUSC

title: Music of the Classical Period
description: The works of Gluck, Haydn, Mozart, Beethoven and their contemporaries; formation of the “Viennese” Classical idiom and its most important forms and genres.
subject: MUSC

title: Romantic Music
description: The music of the nineteenth and early twentieth centuries; the relationship of music to other arts; works from Schubert and Rossini to Mahler, Strauss, and Debussy.
subject: MUSC

title: Music Since 1900
description: Music from the early twentieth century to the present; major trends in contemporary music.
subject: MUSC

title: Writing about Jazz History
description: An in-depth examination of jazz history and jazz criticism, including critical review of jazz history textbooks, articles, and recordings.
subject: MUSC

title: Ensemble - Guitar
description: Experience in a musical ensemble such as orchestra, graduate chorus, or jazz. Repeatable for credit. Suffixes: B-Wind Ensemble, C-Graduate Vocal Ensemble, D-Percussion, E-Left Bank Jazz, L-Guitar, S-Symphony Orchestra, T-Concert Choir, V-Chamber Orchestra. Subdiscipline: Music (Musical Organizations)
subject: MUSC

title: Topics in Church Music
description: Selected topics such as hymnology, church-choir literature and techniques, and church-music administration. May be repeated for a maximum of 12 hours as topic varies.
subject: MUSC

title: Pedagogy of Group Piano
description: Methodology and survey of materials for beginning through intermediate piano study in groups.
subject: MUSC

title: Advanced Piano Accompanying
description: Continuation of MUSC 569 . Approaches to specific problems in vocal and instrumental accompanying; supervised accompanying in class.
subject: MUSC

title: Suzuki String Pedagogy I
description: An examination of Shin’ichi Suzuki’s philosophy and approach to Talent Education, parent education, laying the foundation (pre-twinkle) for studies in literature and techniques in Suzuki Violin books 1 and 2. Congruent with Suzuki Association of the American guidelines. Congruent with guidelines of the Suzuki Association of the Americans.
subject: MUSC

title: Suzuki String Pedagogy II
description: An examination of the teaching points in Suzuki Violin books 3 and 4, including scales, arpeggios, etudes, and other supplementary material. Congruent with Suzuki Association of the Americas guidelines.
subject: MUSC

title: Seminar in Performance Pedagogy I
description: Problems in piano pedagogy at the advanced level and directed teaching.
subject: MUSC

title: Seminar in Performance Pedagogy II
description: Problems in piano pedagogy at the advanced level and directed teaching. Course may be repeated for a total of 9 credit hours.
subject: MUSC

title: Topics in Piano Pedagogy and Literature
description: For pianists only. Topics such as Mozart sonatas, Beethoven sonatas, piano music of Debussy. May be repeated for credit as topic varies, 12 hrs max.
subject: MUSC

title: Special Topics in Piano Pedagogy
description: Content varies by title. May be repeated as title varies.
subject: MUSC

title: Advanced Vocal Pedagogy
description: Advanced study of the anatomy and function of the singing voice with application to the diagnosis and correctio of problems in singing.
subject: MUSC
prereq: Prerequisites: MUSC 577.

title: Advanced Diction
description: Pronunciation, placement, and rhythm of Italian, French, and German.
subject: MUSC

title: Opera Theater
description: Study of selected operatic characters from a historical, psychological, and physical perspective.
subject: MUSC

title: Role Preparation
description: Study of operatic role(s), with attention given to rhythmic accuracy, style, language/diction, translations, and interpretation.
subject: MUSC

title: Opera Production
description: Supervised preparation and production of an opera.
subject: MUSC

title: College Music Teaching
description: Trends in higher education, responsibilities of college teachers, strategies for effective teaching, the academic job search, and tenure and promotion processes.
subject: MUSC

title: Suzuki Practicum I
description: Practical application of the teaching points in Suzuki Violin books 1 and 2. Includes observations of lessons and supervised teaching.
subject: MUSC

title: Suzuki Practicum II
description: Practical application of the teaching points in Suzuki Violin books 3 and 4. Includes observations of lessons and supervised teaching.
subject: MUSC

title: Advanced Jazz Improvisation
description: Applications of harmonic, melodic, and rhythmic concepts for the advanced jazz improvisation student. Exploration of different jazz improvisation areas such as “free,” avant-garde, polytonal, and serial.
subject: MUSC

title: Special Topics in Music
description: Content varies by title. May be repeated for credit as topics vary for a total of 12 credits.
subject: MUSC

title: Composition Recital
description: Presentation of the student composer’s work in a suitable professional setting.
subject: MUSC

title: Opera Role
description: Public performance of a major opera role.
subject: MUSC

title: Concerto Recital
description: Public performance of a major concerto with orchestra or appropriate ensemble.
subject: MUSC

title: Chamber Recital
description: Performances of a public recital of chamber music.
subject: MUSC

title: Solo Recital
description: Performance of a public recital in the student’s performance area.
subject: MUSC

title: Thesis Preparation
description: Thesis.
subject: MUSC

title: Advanced Performance Pedagogy
description: Applied music teaching at the college level. Observation in two or more studios. Teaching experience on an individual basis under the direction of applied faculty members. Repeatable for maximum of four credits.
subject: MUSC

title: Research Methods in Music
description: Basic probability and statistics with applications to and examples in music. Topics include elementary probability and data distribution, statistical inference, linear regression, and correlation with application to music history, theory, psychology, and performance.
subject: MUSC

title: Doctoral Applied Music/Flute/Piccolo
