description: Images of the human person in contemporary philosophy, literature, psychology, and religion, and an evaluation of these images as norms for human conduct and social policy. Particular attention may be given to images found in specific philosophical traditions, including existentialism, Marxism, behaviorism, and mysticism.
subject: PHIL

title: Communicating Moral Issues
description: Moral issues confronting men and women in contemporary society and the challenges of communicating effectively about them. Topics will vary but may include access to health care, euthanasia, abortion, same sex marriage and the moral and environmental consequences of eating animals.
subject: PHIL

title: Science and Pseudo-Science
description: Attempts to distinguish science from pseudo-science; inquiry into such cases as astrology, psychoanalysis, and parapsychology.
subject: PHIL

title: Philosophy of Law
description: An immersive introduction to the nature and function of law, with special emphasis on the interpretation and application of the law in the judicial process. Readings in classical and contemporary philosophy of law will be considered.
subject: PHIL

title: Ancient Philosophy
description: An introduction to the work of ancient philosophers, with special emphasis on Plato and Aristotle.
subject: PHIL

title: Greek and Roman Philosophy after Aristotle
description: Problems such as hedonism, providence, belief and evidence, and mysticism, as they appear in the writings of Epicureans, Stoics, Skeptics, and Plotinus.
subject: PHIL

title: History of Medieval Philosophy
description: Major philosophical traditions in the Middle Ages.
subject: PHIL

title: Seventeenth and Eighteenth-Century Philosophy
description: An introduction to Continental and British philosophy running roughly from Descartes through Kant.
subject: PHIL

title: Nineteenth and Twentieth-Century Philosophy
description: An introduction to Continental and British philosophy since Kant through study of the works of representative philosophers. Particular emphasis is placed on the development of Idealism, Marxism, Existentialism and Phenomenology, and analytic philosophy.
subject: PHIL

title: American Philosophy
description: The principal movements of philosophical thought from Colonial times to the present, with special emphasis on the 19th and 20th centuries.
subject: PHIL

title: Existentialism
description: An introduction to existentialist themes in contemporary philosophy, literature, psychology, and religion. The writings of existentialists such as Kierkegaard, Nietzsche, Camus, Sartre, Buber, May, and Binswanger will be read and discussed.
subject: PHIL

title: Classical Origins of Western Medical Ethics
description: Examination of ancient Greek and Roman philosophical, medical, and literary works (in English) as sources for the origins of medical ethics. Priority enrollment for Medical Humanities students.
subject: PHIL

title: Between Magic and Method: Ancient Medicine
description: Introduction to ancient medicine: science and art, theory and practice, healing and predicting. Topics include Medicine before Hippocrates, Hippocratic medicine, holism, naturalism, medicine, religion and magic, medicine and scientific explanation, Hellenistic medicine and methodology, Galenic medicine.
subject: PHIL

title: Asian Religious Philosophy
description: A historical overview and critical introduction to the philosophical practices of Asian religions; an examination of the basic worldviews, thought frameworks, and foundational questions of the main schools of premodern Asian religious philosophy.
subject: PHIL

title: Ethics
description: A study of the moral principles of conduct and the basic concepts underlying these principles, such as good, evil, right, wrong, justice, value, duty, and obligation. The ethical works of influential philosophers are analyzed in terms of these concepts.
subject: PHIL

title: Medical Ethics
description: The concepts of Person and Justice as they relate to biomedical sciences and technologies.
subject: PHIL

title: Environmental Ethics
description: Examination of principles and arguments surrounding moral issues involving the environment.
subject: PHIL

title: Ethics of Science and Technology
description: Role of ethical judgments in directing or curtailing scientific research; case studies from natural and social sciences.
subject: PHIL

title: Business Ethics
description: Ethical problems in business; application to business situations of philosophical theories of individual, corporate, and governmental rights and responsibilities.
subject: PHIL

title: Engineering Ethics
description: An investigation of ethical issues in engineering and engineering-related technology. Topics include whistleblowing, employee/employer relations, environmental issues, issues related to advances in information technology, and privacy.
subject: PHIL

title: AI Ethics
description: Moral concepts such as justice, privacy, and equality applied to artificial intelligence technologies.
subject: PHIL

title: Law and Religion
description: An examination and critical assessment of the philosophical concepts, issues, and questions surrounding the relationship of church and state.
subject: PHIL

title: Social and Political Philosophy
description: An overview of major themes in political philosophy such as the nature of politics, obligation, community, representation, freedom, equality, and justice.
subject: PHIL

title: Crime and Justice
description: The fundamental concepts of a criminal justice system and their philosophical bases. Rights, privacy, responsibility, and the problem of justification of state control of private behavior through punishment and therapy.
subject: PHIL

title: Philosophy of Education
description: A critical examination of the theories of education of such philosophers as Plato, Rousseau, Dewey, Newman, and Whitehead. Emphasis is on the development of a philosophy of higher education.
subject: PHIL

title: Contemporary Marxism and Society
description: Recent Marxist-inspired critics of politics, science, technology, art, advertising, and other aspects of cultural life, with comparison both to Marx’s philosophical and economic writings and to other types of contemporary criticisms.
subject: PHIL

title: Feminist Philosophy
description: Introduces feminist philosophy and applications to philosophical problems.
subject: PHIL

title: Disobedience, Dissent, and Revolution
description: This course explores central philosophical issues related to resisting and opposing claimed state authority. Focus will be on classic and modern texts regarding the intentional, ethically-, or politically-motivated violation of law and on the justification of revolution.
subject: PHIL

title: Philosophy of Art
description: Philosophical problems relating to the arts, the nature of artworks, artistic meaning, aesthetic experience, and artistic values.
subject: PHIL

title: Philosophy and Film
description: Selected philosophical problems as they are presented in feature and documentary films.
subject: PHIL

title: Knowledge and Reality
description: Examination of skeptical attacks, critical defenses, and philosophical theories of what we know and what is to be taken as ultimate reality.
subject: PHIL

title: Mind and Nature
description: Philosophical theories about the nature of consciousness, the problem of qualia, phenomenal concepts, the explanatory gap hypothesis, higher-order consciousness, prospects for naturalistic accounts of consciousness.
subject: PHIL

title: Feedom and Human Action
description: An examination of the nature of human action, agency, ability, intentions, reason, and free will.
subject: PHIL

title: History and Philosophy of Science
description: Philosophy and history of science and their interaction from ancient Greece to the present. Emphasis on physics, astronomy, and chemistry.
subject: PHIL

title: Science, Technology and Society
description: Historical, ethical and epistemic development of science and technology in its relationship to society.
subject: PHIL

title: Formal Theories of Rationality and Their Philosophical Implications
description: Formal theories of rationality in the context of decision-making and games; uses of these formal theories to address traditional philosophical issues such as rationality, knowledge, choice, social welfare, cooperation, and communication.
subject: PHIL
prereq: Prerequisites: C or higher in any course that satisfies the ARP Carolina Core requirement.

title: Special Topics in Philosophy
description: Topics selected by the instructor for specialized study. Course content varies and will be announced in the schedule of classes by title.
subject: PHIL

title: Writing-Intensive: Ancient Philosophy
description: Writing-intensive study in philosophy of the pre-modern period. Course content varies and will be announced in the schedule of classes by title. As a writing-intensive course, substantial time will be devoted to developing and improving the student’s ability to write clear, well-reasoned, and compelling philosophical essays.
subject: PHIL

title: Writing-Intensive: Modern Philosophy
description: Writing-intensive study in philosophy of the modern period. Course content varies and will be announced in the schedule of classes by title. As a writing-intensive course, substantial time will be devoted to developing and improving the student’s ability to write clear, well-reasoned, and compelling philosophical essays.
subject: PHIL

title: Writing-Intensive: Field of Philosophy
description: Writing-intensive study in one or more fields of philosophy. Course content varies and will be announced in the schedule of classes by title. As a writing-intensive course, substantial time will be devoted to developing and improving the student’s ability to write clear, well-reasoned, and compelling philosophical essays.
subject: PHIL

title: Junior Seminar in Philosophy
description: Overview of philosophical theories and debates with attention to skills in discussion and presentation and in preparing and writing a research paper in philosophy. Topics selected by the instructor.
subject: PHIL

title: Independent Study
description: Contract approved by instructor, advisor, and department chair is required for undergraduate students.
subject: PHIL

title: Senior Seminar in Philosophy
description: Review of central topics in philosophy serving as a capstone course for senior majors in philosophy.
subject: PHIL

title: Senior Thesis
description: Directed research resulting in a written thesis. Senior philosophy major or double major, GPA of 3.30, permission of faculty member.
subject: PHIL

title: Internship in Philosophy
description: Supervised professional experience or research outside the classroom, involving a meaningful project or activity for the employing firm or organization and a scholarly project for the student. An Undergraduate Internship Contract is required.
subject: PHIL
prereq: Prerequisites: B or higher in two PHIL courses numbered 300 or above.

title: British Empiricism
description: A historical and critical survey of the British philosophers of experience. Principal concentration is on Locke, Berkeley, and Hume.
subject: PHIL
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in PHIL 304.

title: Continental Rationalism
description: A critical and historical study of the 17th-century European philosophers. The works of Descartes, Spinoza, and Leibniz are emphasized.
subject: PHIL
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in PHIL 304.

title: Analytic Philosophy
description: A critical study of recent and contemporary works in philosophical analysis, and an evaluation of the purposes, methods, and results of this movement.
subject: PHIL
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better on 3 hours in philosophy beyond the 100 level.

title: Phenomenology and Existentialism
description: A critical study of some fundamental themes in phenomenology and the philosophy of existence. Emphasis is placed on an intensive study of selected works of such writers as Kierkegaard, Jaspers, Husserl, and Heidegger.
subject: PHIL
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in PHIL 304 or PHIL 305.

title: Plato
description: An intensive study of selected Dialogues by Plato.
subject: PHIL
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in PHIL 301.

title: Aristotle
description: An intensive study of some of the more important of Aristotle's works.
subject: PHIL
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in PHIL 301.

title: Medieval Philosophy
description: A historical and critical study of the works of the leading medieval philosophers.
subject: PHIL
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in PHIL 303.

title: Hume
description: An intensive study of the philosophical writings of Hume, especially A Treatise of Human Nature.
subject: PHIL
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in PHIL 304.

title: Kant
description: An intensive study of the work of Kant, especially the Critique of Pure Reason.
subject: PHIL
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in PHIL 304.

title: Theory of Knowledge
description: An examination of some representative theories of truth, meaning, probability, and perception.
subject: PHIL
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in 3 hours in philosophy beyond the 100 level.

title: Symbolic Logic
description: A presentation and philosophical examination of the fundamentals of modern symbolic logic.
subject: PHIL
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in PHIL 115.

title: Philosophy of Science
description: A critical examination of methods and concepts of the sciences. Topics include scientific revolutions, the unity of science, experimentation, explanation, and evidence.
subject: PHIL
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in 3 hours in philosophy beyond the 100 level.

title: Philosophy of History
description: A philosophical examination of historical inquiry. Theories of historical development. The logical problems of historical explanation.
subject: PHIL
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in 3 hours in philosophy beyond the 100 level.

title: Ethical Theory
description: Survey of recent and historical developments in ethical theory with special emphasis on the meaning of ethical language and the forms of reasoning employed in discussing moral values.
subject: PHIL
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in PHIL 320.

title: Philosophy of Religion
description: A critical study of selected problems in the philosophy of religion. Emphasis is placed on problems relating to the existence of God, religious knowledge, and the language of religion.
subject: PHIL
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in 3 hours in philosophy beyond the 100 level.

title: Advanced Aesthetics
description: Detailed examination of the literature on aesthetics.
subject: PHIL
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in 3 hours in philosophy beyond the 100 level.

title: Philosophy of Language
description: An examination of concepts and problems such as meaning, reference, analyticity, definition, and the relation between logic and philosophy.
subject: PHIL
prereq: Prerequisites: C or higher in PHIL 114 or PHIL 511.

title: Philosophy of the Social Sciences
description: The goals of inquiry and problems such as objectivity, reduction, value freedom, and ideology.
subject: PHIL
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in 3 hours in philosophy beyond the 100 level.

title: Metaphysics
description: Major issues in classical and modern metaphysics. Topics include the idea of first philosophy, being, substance, the problem of universals, essentialism, causation, time and space, and metaphysical method.
subject: PHIL
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in PHIL 350 or PHIL 351 or PHIL 360.

title: Philosophy of Mind
description: The concept of mind, the mind-body problem, emotions and cognition, the possibility of artificial minds, theories of embodied cognition.
subject: PHIL
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in PHIL 350 or PHIL 351 or PHIL 360.

title: Mathematical Logic
description: Axiomatic development of logic and the set-theoretic foundations of mathematics.
subject: PHIL
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in PHIL 511.

title: Introduction to Semantics and Pragmatics
description: Introduction to the study of linguistic meaning, including the following topics: meaning, reference, and truth; the connections among language, thought, and reality; word meaning and sentence meaning; possible worlds and modality; thematic roles; meaning and context; presupposition and implicature; speech acts; formal semantics; and cognitive semantics.
subject: PHIL
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in any of LING 300, LING 301, LING 600, PHIL 114, PHIL 511.

title: Advanced Topics in Logic
description: Philosophical problems about logic, the development of philosophical logics, and the problems surrounding them.
subject: PHIL
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in PHIL 511.

title: Philosophy of Biology
description: Examination of major conceptual, theoretical, and methodological issues in biological science. Topics include reductionism, units of selection, adaptationism, relations between evolutionary and developmental biology and between biology and society.
subject: PHIL
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in 3 hours of Philosophy beyond the 100 level.

title: Hellenistic Philosophy
description: Survey of the major schools and trends in Hellenistic philosophy: Epicureans, Stoics, Academic Skeptics. Topics include eudaimonism, hedonism, monism, teleology, and the criterion of truth.
subject: PHIL
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in PHIL 301 or PHIL 302.

title: Virtues, Acts, and Consequences
description: Recent contributions to three central strands of ethical theory: virtue theory, deontology, and utilitarianism; historical roots and recent developments.
subject: PHIL
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in PHIL 320.

title: Concepts of Evidence
description: Systematic approaches to data analysis--Bayesian, Fisherian and decision theoretic--will be critically appraised. Applications of these theories to some problems of inductive logic: the paradoxes of confirmation, the role of simplicity, and the probability of inductive generalizations.
subject: PHIL
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in PHIL 350 or PHIL 351 or PHIL 360.

title: Jurisprudence
description: An examination of a number of philosophical problems about the law: the nature and function of rules, the difference between legal rules and other rules, the nature of reasoning from legal rules, the concept of a legal system, and the relation of law and morals.
subject: PHIL
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in PHIL 320 or PHIL 330.

title: Social Justice
description: Recent theories of distributive justice and their application to such issues as redistribution of wealth, reverse discrimination, and the conflict between liberty and equality. Authors include Rawls, Nozick, Hayek, and Popper.
subject: PHIL
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in PHIL 320 or PHIL 321 or PHIL 322 or PHIL 330 or PHIL 331.

title: Contemporary European Social Philosophy
description: An examination of European social philosophy associated with either the Frankfurt School of Social Research or contemporary French Poststructuralism.
subject: PHIL
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in 3 hours in philosophy beyond the 100 level.

title: Ecofeminism
description: An exploration of the connections between oppression of women and oppression of nature.
subject: PHIL
prereq: Prerequisites: 3 hours in philosophy beyond the 100 level.

title: Language and Interpretation in Contemporary European Philosophy
description: Selected contemporary European philosophical movements, their views on language, and their approach to interpretation: hermeneutics, structuralism, poststructuralism.
subject: PHIL
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in PHIL 114.

title: Renaissance Philosophy
description: Humanism (e.g., Petrarca), Platonism (e.g., Pico and Ficino), Aristotelianism (e.g., Pomponazzi), philosophies of nature (e.g., Telesio, Campanella, and Bruno), and Nicholas of Cusa, Erasmus, Montaigne, and Suarez.
subject: PHIL
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in PHIL 301 or PHIL 302 or PHIL 303.

title: Health Care Ethics
description: An exploration of the ethical dimensions of patient care in the clinical setting.
subject: PHIL
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in PHIL 320 or PHIL 321 or PHIL 322 or PHIL 330 or PHIL 331.

title: Advanced Special Topics in Philosophy
description: Advanced special topics in Philosophy. May be repeated as content varies by title.
subject: PHIL

title: Readings in Philosophy
subject: PHIL
prereq: Prerequisites: 6 hours in philosophy beyond the 100 level.

title: Studies in Continental Philosophy
description: Study of the works of one or more major contemporary continental philosophers.
subject: PHIL

title: Ethics and the Health Sciences
description: Students are introduced to formal and informal codes of professional conduct of various health science disciplines and understand the implications of these distinctions for interdisciplinary research, clinical practice, and administration.
subject: PHIL

title: Ethics in Criminal Justice
description: Classic and contemporary theories of ethics and their applications to criminal justice decision-making.
subject: PHIL

title: Philosophy of Mind
description: Topics and problems arising in the philosophy of mind.
subject: PHIL

title: Pragmatic Theory
description: Study of formal approaches to pragmatic phenomena such as focus, presupposition, and implicature; examination of deictic, contextual and perspectival expressions; survey of pragmatic frameworks such as Relevance Theory and Discourse Representation Theory; study of information structural properties of natural languages, including topic- comment structure, given-new contrasts, definiteness versus indefiniteness.
subject: PHIL
prereq: Prerequisites: LING 600 or LING 627.

title: Studies in Philosophy of Language
description: Examination of concepts such as meaning, reference, analyticity, and translational indeterminacy; evaluation of accounts of speech acts, the semantics of propositional attitudes, and metaphor and other pragmatic phenomena.
subject: PHIL

title: Semantic Theory
description: The formal study of linguistic meaning, including the following topics: Fregean truth-conditional semantics; lexical decomposition; predication and modification; lambda abstraction; generalized quantification; intentional and extensional contexts; tense, aspect, and modality; propositional attitudes; and indexicality.
subject: PHIL
prereq: Prerequisites: LING 600 or LING 627.

title: Contemporary Political Philosophy
description: Recent work in philosophy regarding political and social values, principles of justice, political quthority, institutions, and related subjects.
subject: PHIL

title: Epistemology
description: Survey of historical and recent trends in epistemology.
subject: PHIL

title: Metaphysics
description: Survey of historical and recent trends in metaphysics.
subject: PHIL

title: Case Study in the Philosophy of Science
description: Introduction to the method of studying historical cases in the philosophy of science. This course revolves around the sustained treatment of one or two such cases.
subject: PHIL

title: Teaching Philosophy
description: Materials, techniques, and problems of teaching philosophy. Repeatable for credit.
subject: PHIL

title: Independent Study
description: Requires permission of instructor.
subject: PHIL

title: Research Seminar
description: Student and faculty presentations of current research in specified subject areas. Content varies. May be repeated for credit.
subject: PHIL

title: Seminar in Environmental Ethics
description: Examination of the intellectual, cultural, and ethical frameworks within which environmental problems arise and are solved.
subject: PHIL

title: Modern Philosophies of Education
description: Critical comparison of present-day schools of thought in the nature, objectives, and functions of American education.
subject: PHIL
prereq: Prerequisites: EDFI 744 or equivalent.

title: Personal and Professional Development for Student Pharmacists I
description: The first course in the personal and professional development series imparts to the student pharmacist the knowledge, skills, abilities, behaviors, and attitudes necessary to provide patient education and demonstrate self-awareness and professionalism.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: first professional year standing.

title: Personal and Professional Development for Student Pharmacists II
description: The second course in the personal and professional development series imparts to the student pharmacist the knowledge, skills, abilities, behaviors, and attitudes necessary to provide patient education; patient and professional advocacy; collaborative and inclusive patient care; and to demonstrate self-awareness and professionalism.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: first professional year standing.

title: Foundations of Pathophysiology and Pharmacology I
description: An introduction to the basic principles and concepts of physiology, pathophysiology and pharmacology.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: First professional year standing.

title: Foundations of Pathophysiology and Pharmacology II
description: This is the second course in the series addressing the basic principles and concepts of physiology, pathophysiology and pharmacology.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: PHMY 602; first professional year standing.

title: Dosage Forms and Drug Delivery Systems
description: A study of the physiochemical principles of the formulation, preparation, properties, and performance of the pharmaceutical dosage forms. Clinical application of the various dosage forms in patient care will be discussed.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: First professional year standing.

title: Microbiology/Immunology
description: Introduction to the role of immunology in human disease and the properties, resistance patterns, diagnostic tools and clinical pearls of common (and not so common) bacterial, viral, fungal and parasitic infectious agents.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: Second professional year standing.

title: Pharmaceutical Biochemistry
description: The course covers the molecular and biochemical basis of human biology and physiology. The properties and functions of biological molecules and biochemical pathways are covered. Emphasis is placed on the molecular basis of human physiology, causes of human diseases and how drugs produce biological effects.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: First professional year standing.

title: Genomics, Pharmacogenomics, and Personalized Medicine
description: The course focuses on nucleic acids as the core theme and examines the pathways used for transmission and expression of genetic information. Topics also include the cell cycle (mitosis) and gametogenesis (meiosis). Students will learn to apply knowledge of human genetic variation that influences drug therapy decision making. Recombinant DNA technology is covered as applied to the study of human health such as personalized medicine, genetic testing, stem cell therapy, gene therapy, RNA silencing, and production of biopharmaceuticals. Antibodies are discussed from the perspective of their use in diagnostic testing, as well as the utilization of antibodies as therapeutics.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: First professional year standing.

title: Foundations of Medicinal Chemistry I
description: This course includes an introduction to the basic principles and concepts of medicinal chemistry and pharmacogenomics including the relationships of molecular structure to drug absorption, distribution, metabolism and excretion. The chemical rationale for drug therapeutic actions and side effects as well as the pharmacogenomics basis for individualized drug therapy is discussed.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: First professional year standing.

title: Foundations of Medicinal Chemistry II
description: In an integrated approach to first year pharmacy education, Foundations in Medicinal Chemistry II will serve as a complementary course to PHMY 618 Genomics, Pharmacogenomics, and Personalized Medicine. We will utilize the principles and concepts learned in general and organic chemistry and PHMY621 to introduce the general molecular properties of pharmaceutical agents. The course will consist of a survey of the medicinal chemistry of the remaining major drug classes not covered in PHMY 621 . Pharmacogenomic information will be emphasized where known and applicable to help explain variations in both therapeutic and side effects due to receptor structure, metabolism and/or other pharmacokinetic properties.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: PHMY 621; first professional year standing.

title: Introduction to Pharmacy Practice
description: Introduction to the professional issues relevant to the practice of pharmacy.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: First professional year standing.

title: Foundations of Pharmacy Practice
description: This course introduces professional issues relevant to the practice of pharmacy including access and utilization of drug information resources.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: First professional year standing.

title: Pharmacy Calculations
description: This course develops the mathematic skills and knowledge required in various pharmacy practice settings. Problem solving skills will be developed as students become proficient in basic math skills used to solve pharmacy calculations based word problems which are commonly seen in a variety of practice settings, including community, compounding, hospital, and nuclear pharmacy.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: First professional year standing.

title: Medical Terminology
description: A thorough knowledge of medical terminology is an essential building block in pharmacy education. This web-based course will aid the student in learning the language and terminology used in other pharmacy courses and all areas of pharmacy practice.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: First professional year standing.

title: Clinical Applications II
description: Case-based discussions that integrate and demonstrate applicability of other course material.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: First professional year standing.

title: Patient Care Fundamentals
description: This course introduces basic patient care fundamentals such as interpreting laboratory values, patient care planning processes, and clinical documentation. The course integrates basic sciences concepts, such as pharmacogenomics, with patient care. It also affords the student opportunities to practice medical literature searches, literature evaluation, and to recognize the social determinants of health in order to diminish disparities and inequities in access to quality care.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: first professional year standing.

title: Pharmacy Skill Lab II: Introduction to Community Pharmacy
description: Interactive laboratory session designed to provide students with a thorough understanding of community pharmacy practice skills, to include the knowledge of prescription and drug order processing necessary to function in community pharmacy practice at a basic level.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: First professional year standing.

title: Pharmacy Skills Lab I: Compounding and Applied Pharmaceutics
description: The art, science and technology of pharmacy compounding.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: First professional year standing.

title: Introduction to Drug Information
description: Basic principles of access and utilization of drug information resources.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: First professional year standing.

title: Transforming Healthcare
description: The course goal is to lay the foundations for beginning health professions students to understand the complexities of the health care system and the role of interprofessional collaboration to improve the system. Through an interprofessional context, students will explore the art and science of teamwork and communication skills, cultural competency, ethical issues, healthcare disparities and social determinants of health, as well as develop ways to improve healthcare systems and patient safety.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: First professional year standing.

title: Introductory Community Practice Pharmacy Experience
description: Introduction to the practice of the pharmacy in the community pharmacy setting. Forty hours of directed experience per week for four weeks.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: First professional year standing.

title: Personal and Professional Development for Student Pharmacists III
description: The third course in the personal and professional development series which imparts to the student pharmacist the knowledge, skills, abilities, behaviors, and attitudes necessary to provide patient education and demonstrate self-awareness and professionalism.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: second professional year standing.

title: Personal and Professional Development for Student Pharmacists IV
description: The fourth course in the personal and professional development series imparts to the student pharmacist the knowledge, skills, abilities, behaviors, and attitudes necessary to provide patient education; patient and professional advocacy; collaborative and inclusive patient care; and to demonstrate self-awareness and professionalism.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: second professional year standing.

title: Biopharmaceutics and Pharmacokinetics
description: Study of the pharmacokinetics of drug absorption, distribution, and elimination (metabolism and excretion). Introductory application of pharmacokinetics to drug interactions and dosage regimen design and adjustment, selected disease states and special populations.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: PHMY 607; second professional year standing.

title: Special Project, Dept. of Pharmaceutical Biomedical Sciences
description: An individualized program of study or research arranged by consultation between student and faculty member. Credit is variable and the course may be repeated for credit.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: second or third professional year standing or in fulfillment of graduate program requirement or honors thesis.

title: Cancer: Causes, Treatment, and Prevention
description: An overview of basic concepts in cancer epidemiology, genetics, and biology, and the therapeutic approaches utilized in the prevention and treatment of cancer.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: second or third professional year standing.

title: Pathophysiologyand Pharmacology I
description: The first course in a series that applies pathophysiology and pharmacology principles introduced in the foundations courses in the first year in an organ-based approach. The course is designed to integrate organ system pathophysiology and pharmacology with pharmacotherapy.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: Second professional year standing.

title: Pathophysiology and Pharmacology II
description: The second course in a series that applies pathophysiology and pharmacology concepts introduced in the foundations courses in the first year in an organ-based approach. The course is designed to integrate organ system pathophysiology and pharmacology with pharmacotherapy.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: PHMY 722; second professional year standing.

title: Self-Care and Complementary Medicines
description: A review of indications, contraindications, and cautions involved with the recommendation and dispensing of nonprescription (OTC) drug items and complementary medicines.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: First professional year standing.

title: Patient Care Applications I
description: This course develops critical thinking skills required for pharmacy practice through case-based discussions and care plan development designed to integrate and demonstrate applicability of current coursework and previous knowledge gained from prior course material in patient centered care.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: second professional year standing.

title: Patient Care Applications II
description: This course further develops critical thinking skills required for pharmacy practice through case-based discussions and care plan development designed to integrate and demonstrate applicability of current coursework and previous knowledge gained from prior course material in patient centered care.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: second professional year standing.

title: Clinical Immunology and Transplant
description: This course will introduce students to the principles of solid organ transplantation (SOT) and the role of a pharmacist in the continuum of care for transplant recipients through case-based learning and exposure to primary literature. Students will learn indications, common disease processes, and complications associated with kidney, pancreas, liver, lung, and heart transplantation in addition to the management of population-specific immunosuppression pharmacotherapy regimens.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: second or third professional year standing.

title: Special Project, Dept of Pharmacy and Outcome Sciences
description: An individualized program of study or research arranged by consultation between student and faculty member. Credit is variable and the course may be repeated for credit.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: second or third professional year standing or in fulfillment of graduate program requirement or honors thesis.

title: Clinical Applications III
description: Case-based discussions that integrate and demonstrate applicability of other course material in patient care.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: Second professional year standing.

title: Clinical Applications IV
description: Case-based discussions that integrate and demonstrate applicability of other course material in patient care.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: Second professional year standing.

title: Creating Pharmacy Leaders
description: This course is designed to teach students the leadership skills needed to influence people and organizations in an effort to create positive change within the practice of pharmacy.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: second or third professional year standing.

title: Introduction to Pharmacy Pathways and Careers
description: The course will emphasize aspects of pharmacy career development, to include understanding of the role of a professional, strategies in dealing with various types of patients, development of professional presentations, and familiarity with career pathways in pharmacy.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: second or third professional year standing.

title: Personal Finance
description: This course is designed to provide the health care practitioner with the knowledge and skills to manage their personal finances in an effective manner. After successfully completing this course, the student will be able to understand the process for making personal financial decisions; establish personal financial goals; evaluate the factors that influence personal financial planning; and apply strategies for attaining personal financial goals.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: second or third professional year standing.

title: Introduction to Pharmaceutical Industry
description: This course will provide students with an overview of the pharmaceutical industry with an emphasis on the drug development process, clinical research, business development, regulatory affairs, and marketing.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: second or third year professional year standing.

title: Alternative Medicine
description: This course is designed to expose the student to a wide variety of herbal medicines and dietary supplements. Discussions will also include regulation of herbal supplements, evaluation of the alternative medicine literature, and disease-directed discussions with case studies.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: second or third professional year standing.

title: Off the SCRIPT: Conversations in Health Care
description: This course will teach students to consider social determinants of health when interacting with patients. Students will characterize the role of various healthcare professions in reducing health-disparities and -inequities. Students will learn to communicate with and advocate for patients through interactions with content experts and development of interview-style podcasts.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: second or third professional year standing.

title: Advancing Rural/Underserved Care and Health
description: This elective course provides a variety of experiences for students to expand their understanding of healthcare disparities, rural healthcare and begin professional networking in a rural area of South Carolina. Students will develop and employ entrepreneurship skills to design a career plan for a pharmacist in a rural community. Students will develop and employ leadership skills to design an initiative to advance healthcare within a rural community. Students will also develop and employ research skills to design a proposal for evaluating their initiative to advance healthcare within a rural community.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: Second or third professional year standing.

title: Women’s Health Age-Related Hormone Therapy
description: This course offers valuable insights into hormonal changes that nearly all female patients will experience over the life span along with appropriate clinical utilization of drug therapy in this population. This course covers hormonal physiology, clinical presentations of imbalance, and therapeutic management approaches during key hormonal transitions. The main focus on this course will be related to peri-menopause, menopause, and post-menopause but discussion of issues impacting younger women with hormonal imbalance will be covered.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: P2 or P3 Standing.

title: Pharmacy Skills Lab V: Applied Community Pharmacy
description: Provide students with an advanced knowledge of prescription and drug order processing, and assist them in the development of appropriate skills necessary for provision of pharmaceutical care to patients in the community setting, through the use of medication profiles, patient counseling, personal interviews, and appropriate prescription and non-prescription drug information.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: PHMY 670; third professional year standing.

title: Pharmacotherapy I
description: This course is the first in a 4-semester sequence of courses providing an organ-based approach to pharmaceutical care and disease management. Students will learn about disease prevention and health promotion, as well as the drug and non-drug therapy of acute and chronic diseases seen in both hospitalized and ambulatory patients. Emphasis is placed on preventing, identifying, and solving drug-related problems. This course is coordinated with Pathophysiology and Pharmacology I.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: Second professional year standing.

title: Pharmacotherapy II
description: This course is the second in a 4-semester sequence of courses providing a systems-based approach to pharmaceutical care and disease management. Students will learn about disease prevention and health promotion, as well as the drug and non-drug therapy of acute and chronic diseases seen in both hospitalized and ambulatory patients. Emphasis is placed on preventing, identifying, and solving drug-related problems. This course is coordinated with Pathophysiology and Pharmacology II.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: PHMY 772; second professional year standing.

title: Pharmacy Political Advocacy
description: This class will focus on enabling students to become advocates for the pharmacy profession through review of current legislation that affects pharmacists and healthcare, as well as determining the impact of legislation on the profession. Students will learn how to affect legislation through contact with their representatives and through collaboration with pharmacy organizations. Concepts for providing to their representatives a concise review of the effect of legislation will also be reviewed.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: second or third professional year standing.

title: Developing a Medication Therapy Management Service
description: This course will prepare students to develop and implement a pharmaceutical a pharmaceutical care service into a community pharmacy setting. The focus will be on designing a Medication Therapy Management service; however other clinical pharmacy services will be discussed. Students will apply basic principles of small business administration and development to the creation of a pharmacy service. Students will participate in interactive lectures, written assignments, and formal presentations.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: third professional year standing.

title: Advanced Self-Care
description: This elective focuses on developing critical thinking, problem solving, and communication skills by incorporating principles of self-management covered in Self-care and Complementary Medicines ( PHMY 750 ) into evidence-based case discussions and presentations.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: second or third professional standing.

title: Outcomes Design and Assessment
description: Common statistical methods and study designs used in pharmacoepidemiology and outcomes research, as well as to issues related to the measurement, analysis, and interpretation of results of clinical trials and outcomes studies.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: Second professional year standing.

title: Post-graduate and Research Training
description: This course is designed to prepare students for and promote post-graduate training, with an emphasis on residency training, but also including PhD and fellowship training. All elements of preparation for post-graduate training will be covered; the class will provide information related to various types of research, discuss the ethical considerations related to research and provide the opportunity to develop and present a research protocol.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: second or third professional year standing.

title: Principles of Pharmacoepidemiology
description: Didactic lectures and discussions involving content from relevant textbooks and research literature on the principles of pharmacoepidemiology, research design, and statistical techniques.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: Second or third professional year standing.

title: Pediatric Pharmacotherapy
description: This challenging course is designed to provide the student with increased exposure to disease states, medication issues, and clinical decision-making skills specific to the pediatric population. Pediatrics is an integral component to most pharmacy careers, and gaining a greater understanding of the patient population’s challenges and nuances is necessary to become a confident and well-rounded practitioner. This elective also seeds to provide exposure to the pediatric pharmacy community, including experts in the subspecialty areas and organizations.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: second professional year standing.

title: Interprofessional Prevention of Childhood Obesity
description: This course introduces health professions students to concepts in community and public health focusing on childhood obesity. The course is in collaboration with the Junior Doctors of Health (JDOH) program. JDOH is an interprofessional service-learning program that works with children in low-income schools with the aim of long term prevention of childhood obesity. During the course, students will lead education and interactive sessions that are part of the JDOH curriculum to promote healthy diet and physical activity behaviors. Students from public health, social work, and pharmacy will participate in didactic class sessions together then will provide education regarding the prevention of childhood obesity as interprofessional teams to elementary students in local schools. Teams will then develop presentations regarding their experiences to present during the final class session.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: second or third professional year standing.

title: Pharmacy Skills Lab III: Introduction to Health-Systems Pharmacy
description: Introduction to Health-Systems Pharmacy is designed to develop essential pharmacy practice skills within a health-system setting. This course provides students with foundational knowledge and hands-on experience in key areas of health-system pharmacy, including pharmacy operations, sterile compounding, and dispensing processes.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: Second professional year standing.

title: Pharmacy Skills Lab IV: Applied Health-Systems Pharmacy
description: Applied Health-Systems Pharmacy builds upon foundational skills from Introduction to Health-Systems Pharmacy, further preparing students for roles within diverse health-system settings. This course expands students’ understanding of advanced pharmacy operations, introducing a range of concepts and practices integral to effective health-systems pharmacy.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: PHMY 790; second professional year standing.

title: Advanced Pharmacy Compounding I
description: This course will build upon the knowledge acquired in the first professional year courses Dosage Forms/Drug Delivery Systems and Compounding/Applied Pharmaceutics lab. The course will provide emphasis on practical application in the evaluation of unique dosage forms, pharmacotherapy, and special pharmaceutical needs of various patient populations. This course is self-paced and delivered in an online format utilizing prerecorded lectures.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: second professional year standing.

title: Advanced Topics in Pharmacy Compounding II
description: This course is an elective course that provides additional insight and understanding into a variety of pharmacy compounding topics, which include nutrient depletion, drug-induced nutrient depletion, metabolic demands of athletes, men’s health, and family medicine. This course is self-paced and delivered in an online format utilizing prerecorded lectures.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: second professional year standing.

title: Pharmacist Entrepreneurship
description: This course introduces students to the principles and practices of entrepreneurship within the pharmacy profession. Emphasis is placed on developing the skills and mindset necessary to identify opportunities, innovate, and create value in healthcare settings. Through guided application of design thinking and business modeling, students will work to address a real-world challenge in pharmacy practice and present a formal business pitch to a panel.
subject: PHMY

title: Introductory Institutional Practice Pharmacy Experience
description: Introduction to the practice of the pharmacy in the institutional setting. Forty hours of directed experience per week for four weeks.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: Second professional year standing.

title: Personal and Professional Development for Student Pharmacists V
description: The fifth course in the personal and professional development series which imparts to the student pharmacist the knowledge, skills, abilities, behaviors, and attitudes necessary to provide patient education and demonstrate self-awareness and professionalism.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: third professional year standing.

title: Personal and Professional Development for Student Pharmacists VI
description: The final course in the personal and professional development series which imparts to the student pharmacist the knowledge, skills, abilities, behaviors, and attitudes necessary to demonstrate patient education; patient and professional advocacy, collaborative and inclusive patient care; leadership; innovation and entrepreneurship; self-awareness and professionalism.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: third professional year standing.

title: Pathophysiology and Pharmacology III
description: This is the third in a 4-semester sequence of courses aimed at integrating the discussion of specific disease states and the mechanism of action of the pharmacologic agents used to treat those disease states. This knowledge will be integrated, utilized and applied in a parallel sequence of courses in Pharmacotherapy using an organ-system based approach emphasizing pharmaceutical care and disease management.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: PHMY 722, PHMY 723; third year professional standing.

title: Pathophysiology and Pharmacology IV
description: This is the fourth in a 4-semester sequence of courses aimed at integrating the discussion of specific disease states and the mechanism of action of the pharmacologic agents used to treat those disease states. This knowledge will be integrated, utilized an applied in a parallel sequence of courses in Pharmacotherapy using an organ-system based approach emphasizing pharmaceutical care and disease management.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: PHMY 822; third year professional standing.

title: Health Care Systems and Management
description: This course provides insight and understanding of the U.S. health care system and pharmacy's role within that system. Managerial skills and concepts are taught to allow pharmacists to maximize their contribution to this health care system and to the patients it serves.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: Third professional year standing.

title: Acute Care Therapeutics
description: A disease-oriented and organ-integrated approach to the care of patients with acute illnesses managed in the in-patient hospital setting. Students will learn common disease processes that affect the acutely ill hospitalized patient and the appropriate pharmacotherapy to treat these patients. This course focuses on developing critical thinking and problem solving skills, and integrating material from this course and previous courses in a case-based format.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: third professional year standing.

title: Owning and Managing an Independent Pharmacy
description: This course provides students with an in-depth understanding of the operational and financial aspects of starting, owning, and managing a community pharmacy. Students will gain knowledge and skills necessary to evaluate an existing pharmacy and look for opportunities to optimize workflow and operations. Learning opportunities will include on-site visits, meetings with pharmacy owners, and discussions within the course.
subject: PHMY

title: The What, Why, and How of Health Sciences Research
description: Students will be exposed to the process of designing a research experiment from idea conception through manuscript submission, including protocol development, IRB approval, abstract submission, grant funding, and peer review. Students will also familiarize themselves with technology commonly used in data collection, analysis, and manuscript writing. Weekly opportunities to engage with accomplished health science researchers will allow the student to be exposed to numerous research designs. The overall goal of this course is to make the student prepared to step in and work in a faculty mentored research study.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: second or third professional year standing.

title: Evidence Based Medicine
description: This course is designed to instruct students on the proper approach to critical evaluation of the medical literature. An evidence based approach to patient care hinges on the medical team’s ability to utilize the most current literature available. Students will learn the keys to critiquing the medical literature, including basic statistical and analytical concepts, in an open forum style, with application of literature information to clinical scenarios.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: second or third professional year standing.

title: Community Pharmacy Practice and Operations
description: This course provides a practical look at independent community pharmacy, including the many aspects involved in starting, owning and running a pharmacy business. Sessions will focus on critically appraising the unique aspects of independent pharmacy ownership/management or sustainable pharmacy services. Learning opportunities will include management skill development and on-site visits.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: second or third professional year standing.

title: Drug Interactions
description: This course is designed to prepare students to appropriately recognize, predict, assess, and manage drug interactions that are encountered in pharmacy practice.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: third professional year standing.

title: Design and Conduct of Clinical Research Studies
description: This course is designed to provide students with exposure to clinical research. The demand for pharmacist researchers in a variety of settings continues to increase, and clinical pharmacists are often called upon to engage in some research activities. Residency training provides practical application of research; however, most residents lack an in-depth exposure to the varied aspects of research prior to graduation. This class will provide students with information related to various types of research, discuss the ethical considerations related to clinical research, and provide students with the opportunity to develop and present a clinical research protocol. This course will help prepare students for post-graduate (residency, fellowship, or PhD) research endeavors.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: third professional year standing.

title: Clinical Applications V
description: Case-based discussions that integrate and demonstrate applicability of other course material in patient care.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: Third professional year standing.

title: Clinical Applications VI
description: A capstone course, where case studies of complex patients and additional application exercises will bring together concepts learned throughout the previous semesters in the professional program.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: Third professional year standing.

title: Patient Care Integration
description: This course will primarily be a case-based class designed to enhance basic pharmaceutical science principles in which students clinically apply knowledge learned from current and past coursework in the University of South Carolina College of Pharmacy curriculum. Students will be required to develop assessments and plans for provided cases. SOAP notes will be required as specified. Each case will be fully discussed in each class period to enhance understanding of the topic. In addition to case-based discussions, students will be introduced to clinical practice exercises, including but not limited to a formulary review assignment, in preparation for the transition to Advanced Pharmacy Practice Experiences (APPEs) following the third professional year. Finally, students will be required to engage in peer evaluations throughout the course.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: third professional year standing.

title: Medication Safety Management
description: This course is designed to teach students proper medication safety practices. As medications are prescribed, prepared, dispensed and administered safely and appropriately, patients’ lives are ultimately improved. However, health care providers are human and thereby fallible by nature. Knowledge of ways in which medication errors commonly occur and the modalities in place to prevent such errors is important to the practice of pharmacy. This course will allow students to learn the history of medication safety, institutional policies, and technological advances in medication safety.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: third professional year standing.

title: Advanced Pharmacy Practice Experience Readiness
description: This course is a capstone course that will primarily be an application of current and past coursework in the College of Pharmacy curriculum to enhance knowledge learned and prepare students for Advanced Pharmacy Practice Experiences (APPEs). Assignments, presentations, exams, and patient cases will allow students to apply previously learned knowledge and demonstrate mastery of skills. Students will be required to work both individually and in groups to complete course assignments.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: third professional year standing.

title: Pharmacotherapy III
description: This course is the third in a 4-semester sequence of courses providing a systems-based approach to pharmaceutical care and disease management. Students will learn about disease prevention and health promotion, as well as the drug and non-drug therapy of acute and chronic diseases seen in both hospitalized and ambulatory patients. Emphasis is placed on preventing, identifying, and solving drug-related problems. This course is coordinated with PHMY 822 , Pathophysiology and Pharmacology III.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: PHMY 772, PHMY 773; third professional year standing.

title: Pharmacotherapy IV
description: This course is the fourth in a 4-semester sequence of courses providing a systems-based approach to pharmaceutical care and disease management. Students will learn about disease prevention and health promotion, as well as the drug and non-drug therapy of acute and chronic diseases seen in both hospitalized and ambulatory patients. Emphasis is placed on preventing, identifying, and solving drug-related problems. This course is coordinated with PHMY 823 , Pathophysiology and Pharmacology IV.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: PHMY 873; third professional year standing.

title: Patient-Centered Approach to Medication Therapy Management
description: This elective prepares students to care for patients participating in medication therapy management. The focus will be on communicating with a patient during a MTM session to identify the patient’s current medication therapy problems and prevent future medication therapy problems. Students will apply therapeutic principles learned in the curriculum to improve the patient’s overall care, as related to the patient’s medication therapies.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: third professional year standing.

title: Advanced Drug Information
description: This course is designed to provide students with advanced drug information practice and critical literature evaluation skills. This course provides students with the requisite skills to facilitate life-long learning.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: Third professional year standing.

title: Senior Care Pharmacy Practice
description: This course will introduce students to various topics related to the care of older patients in any setting. Course content will complement the PHMY curriculum in regard to geriatric pathophysiology, pharmacology, socioeconomics, regulations, and pharmacotherapeutics. In addition, it will expand upon the various practices of senior care pharmacy. Practicing consultants and other topic experts will serve as lecturers. In addition to class discussions and lectures, students will participate in a consultant pharmacist shadowing experience and will conduct an interview with a senior patient.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: third professional year standing.

title: Integrated Pharmacy: Infectious Diseases Pharmacotherapy in Patient Care
description: This course is an integrated approach to infectious diseases pharmacotherapy that builds upon required PHMY courses. At the conclusion of the course, the student will have a working knowledge of infectious diseases pharmacotherapy and be able to develop a complete patient care plan given an infectious diagnosis. Additionally, the course will stress important drug interactions with antimicrobials, adverse events associated with antimicrobials, and the prominent role of pharmacists in a HIV clinic, antimicrobial stewardship program, and/or medical crisis.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: second or third professional year standing.

title: Delivering MTM Services
description: This course will utilize the “Delivering MTM Services in the Community” certificate training program developed by APhA and ASCP to provide students the essential skills necessary to become a successful MTM practitioner. Students will learn how to evaluate complicated medication regimens, identify medication-related problems, and make recommendations. Aspects of business development and management will also be discussed. Students will participate in interactive lectures, reading and writing assignments, critical thinking exercises, and patient counseling sessions.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: third professional year standing.

title: Pharmacy Law and Ethics
description: This course offers an in-depth exploration of federal and state laws and regulations that guide and regulate the practice of pharmacy. It covers the critical legal frameworks pharmacists must apply to ensure compliance and to maintain professional standards. The course also introduces essential professional and ethical issues relevant to pharmacy practice, highlighting their importance in protecting patient safety and sustaining public trust.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: Third professional year standing.

title: Pharmacotherapy Cases
description: This challenging course is designed to provide the student with the opportunity to gather, analyze, and synthesize medical information from complex patient cases into complete pharmacotherapeutic treatment plans designed to improve patient outcomes. This process will enhance the student’s ability to be an independent and active learner, foster the student’s individual and group problem solving abilities, promote the critical examination of medical problems, and develop student’s clinical skills and decision-making abilities.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: third professional year standing.

title: Advanced Endocrinology
description: This elective course is designed to provide the student with increased exposure to disease states and medications in the specialty of endocrinology. In addition, the student will have the opportunity to refine clinical knowledge, decision-making skills, and presentation ability within this course. This course will provide active learning opportunities involving clinical debates, drug information questions, and disease state presentations.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: third professional year standing.

title: Clinical Pharmacokinetics
description: Clinical application of basic pharmacokinetic principles to safe and effective patient management with emphasis on design of dosage regimens, therapeutic monitoring, and adjustment of therapy. Application illustrated and practiced through discussions and case examples.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: PHMY 710; second professional year standing.

title: Clinical Pharmacogenomics and Advanced Personalized Medicine
description: This course will incorporate didactic teaching from both the basic sciences and pharmacotherapy realms to prepare students to effectively engage with commonly encountered questions and pharmacy practice dilemmas related to pharmacogenomics. The course will also provide active learning through longitudinal projects involving pharmacogenomic literature analysis, and solving pharmacogenetics-related pharmacotherapy selection problems.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: second or third professional year standing.

title: Clinical Assessment
description: Development of clinical assessment skills necessary in the provision of pharmaceutical care to patients with a variety of disease states.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: Third professional year standing.

title: Telemental Health
description: The objective of this course is to introduce nursing, pharmacy and social work students to direct telemental health practice in health settings – the use of telecommunications technology to provide behavioral health sciences. This course provides health professions students with a detailed examination of the use of telehealth processes to transform the provision of healthcare. Through an interprofessional context, students will learn the history and status of telemental health activities; engage with stimulated and live telemental health learning experiences; understand the use of telemental health to improve healthcare access and population health; and embrace the changing models of care resulting from advancing telemental health technologies.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: third professional year standing.

title: Acute/General Medicine Pharmacy Practice Experience I
description: This required advanced pharmacy practice experience (APPE) provides clinical pharmacy activity on a variety of adult and pediatric medicine inpatient services including cardiology, critical care, emergency medicine, geriatrics, gastrointestinal, infectious disease, nephrology, OB-GYN, oncology, psychiatry, pulmonary and transplant. The APPE provides an exposure to a variety of disease states that allows the student to gain experience monitoring drug therapy and to participate in the therapeutic decision making process. Additional competencies to be achieved during this APPE include: (1) how to develop a problem list, (2) how to present a patient, (3) how to develop and implement a monitoring plan, (4) how to take a medication history, (5) how to respond to a drug information request, and (6) review the policies and procedures at the institution.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: Fourth professional year standing.

title: Acute/General Medicine Pharmacy Practice Experience II
description: This required advanced pharmacy practice experience (APPE) provides clinical pharmacy activity on a variety of adult and pediatric medicine inpatient services including cardiology, critical care, emergency medicine, geriatrics, gastrointestinal, infectious disease, nephrology, OB-GYN, oncology, psychiatry, pulmonary and transplant. The APPE provides an exposure to a variety of disease states that allows the student to gain experience monitoring drug therapy and to participate in the therapeutic decision making process. Additional competencies to be achieved during this APPE include: (1) how to develop a problem list, (2) how to present a patient, (3) how to develop and implement a monitoring plan, (4) how to take a medication history, (5) how to respond to a drug information request, and (6) review the policies and procedures at the institution.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: fourth professional year standing.

description: This required advanced pharmacy practice experience (APPE) provides clinical pharmacy activity on a variety of adult and pediatric medicine inpatient services including cardiology, critical care, emergency medicine, geriatrics, gastrointestinal, infectious disease, nephrology, OB-GYN, oncology, psychiatry, pulmonary and transplant. subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: fourth professional year standing.

title: Acute/General Medicine Pharmacy Practice Experience IV
description: This advanced pharmacy practice experience (APPE) provides additional clinical pharmacy activity on a variety of adult and pediatric medicine inpatient services including cardiology, critical care, emergency medicine, geriatrics, gastrointestinal, infectious disease, nephrology, OB-GYN, oncology, psychiatry, pulmonary and transplant. title: Acute/General Medicine Pharmacy Practice Experience VII
description: This advanced pharmacy practice experience (APPE) provides additional clinical pharmacy activity on a variety of adult and pediatric medicine inpatient services including cardiology, critical care, emergency medicine, geriatrics, gastrointestinal, infectious disease, nephrology, OB-GYN, oncology, psychiatry, pulmonary and transplant. The APPE provides an exposure to a variety of disease states that allows the student to gain experience monitoring drug therapy and to participate in the therapeutic decision making process. Additional competencies to be achieved during this APPE include: (1) how to develop a problem list, (2) how to present a patient, (3) how to develop and implement a monitoring plan, (4) how to take a medication history, (5) how to respond to a drug information request, and (6) review the policies and procedures at the institution.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: fourth professional year standing.

title: Ambulatory Care Pharmacy Practice Experience I
description: This required advanced pharmacy practice experience (APPE) involves the student in the daily provision of clinical pharmacy services in the ambulatory care setting including anticoagulation, geriatrics, HIV, Indian Health Services, oncology, pain management, and primary care. Responsibilities include providing drug therapy for disease states commonly encountered in the outpatient setting, providing drug regimen reviews, physical assessment and interviewing patients to elicit drug histories, health status, and adherence to therapy. A significant aspect of this rotation pertains to the long-term management of chronically ill patients.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: fourth professional year standing.

title: Ambulatory Care Pharmacy Practice Experience II
description: This advanced pharmacy practice experience (APPE) involves the student in the daily provision of clinical pharmacy services in the ambulatory care setting including anticoagulation, geriatrics, HIV, Indian Health Services, oncology, pain management, and primary care. Responsibilities include providing drug therapy for disease states commonly encountered in the outpatient setting, providing drug regimen reviews, physical assessment and interviewing patients to elicit drug histories, health status, and adherence to therapy. A significant aspect of this rotation pertains to the long-term management of chronically ill patients.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: fourth professional year standing.

Responsibilities include providing drug therapy for disease states commonly encountered in the outpatient setting, providing drug regimen reviews, physical assessment and interviewing patients to elicit drug histories, health status, and adherence to therapy. title: Ambulatory Care Pharmacy Practice Experience IV
description: This advanced pharmacy practice experience (APPE) involves the student in the daily provision of clinical pharmacy services in the ambulatory care setting including anticoagulation, geriatrics, HIV, Indian Health Services, oncology, pain management, and primary care. Responsibilities include providing drug therapy for disease states commonly encountered in the outpatient setting, providing drug regimen reviews, physical assessment and interviewing patients to elicit drug histories, health status, and adherence to therapy. A significant aspect of this rotation pertains to the long-term management of chronically ill patients.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: fourth professional year standing.

title: Community Pharmacy Practice I
description: This advanced pharmacy practice experience (APPE) builds upon the introductory pharmacy practice experience in community pharmacy. This APPE provides pharmacy experience in a variety of community settings including chain, clinic, compounding and independent. The emphasis is placed on patient counseling, immunizations, medication therapy management, and/or compounding.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: fourth professional year standing.

description: This advanced pharmacy practice experience (APPE) builds upon the introductory pharmacy practice experience in a hospital/health system setting. description: This advanced pharmacy practice experience (APPE) builds upon the introductory pharmacy practice experience in a hospital/health system setting. title: Elective Pharmacy Practice Experience I
description: Elective pharmacy practice experiences provide students with the opportunity to explore a variety of pharmacy practice settings outside of the four core curricular categories (acute/general medicine, ambulatory care, community, and hospital/health system). These elective experiences are typically in non-traditional pharmacy settings and include, but are not limited to, academia, administration, drug information, industry, informatics, international, investigational drug services, long term care, mail order, managed care, nuclear, poison center, research and veterinary medicine.
subject: PHMY
prereq: Prerequisites: fourth professional year standing.

description: Elective pharmacy practice experiences provide students with the opportunity to explore a variety of pharmacy practice settings outside of the four core curricular categories (acute/general medicine, ambulatory care, community, and hospital/health system). These elective experiences are typically in non-traditional pharmacy settings and include, but are not limited to, academia, administration, drug information, industry, informatics, international, investigational drug services, long term care, mail order, managed care, nuclear, poison center, research and veterinary medicine.
