description: A professional development-focused course that is a Corequisite: SCHC 493 .
subject: SCHC

title: HNRS: South Carolina Washington Semester Program: Internship
description: Supervised, full-time internship experience, enrichment activities and related academic coursework for students enrolled in the South Carolina Washington Semester Program and living in Washington D.C. This is a required course for the South Carolina Washington Semester Program.
subject: SCHC

title: HNRS: Honors Internship
description: Supervised internship experience and related academic coursework for students engaged in a short-term internship or micro-internship. Course is offered for variable credits, requiring 50 hours of work at internship site per credit hour.
subject: SCHC

title: HNRS: Undergraduate Research
description: Student research supervised by a faculty member. Emphasis is on the development of critical thinking, appropriate research skills, and writing.
subject: SCHC

title: HNRS: Honors Internship
description: Prerequisite: Sophomore standing or above; Honors College; approval of honors dean required.
subject: SCHC

title: HNRS: Senior Thesis/Project
description: Senior thesis research, creative project, or applied project under the direction of a USC faculty member. Completion of 3 credits with a C or better required for graduation from South Carolina Honors College. May be repeated. Additional credit hours may be taken with departmental approval.
subject: SCHC
prereq: Prerequisites: Passing grade in SCHC 390 and approved thesis director contract.

title: HNRS: Interdisciplinary Proseminars
description: A proseminar course with specific topics to be selected as need and interests dictate.
subject: SCHC

title: Life Science for Teachers I
description: Topics appropriate for elementary and middle-school curricula; phylogenetic organization of major kingdoms, characteristics of plants and animals, including humans; ecological principles; communities; energy needs, resources, flow and balance; heredity and adaptation.
subject: SMED

title: Energy, Motion, and Matter
description: Integrated study of the earth’s atmosphere for pre-service and in-service middle school teachers combining concepts from earth, life, and physical science leading to an understanding of the interaction of all systems.
subject: SMED
prereq: Prerequisites: introductory-level courses in life, earth, and physical sciences.

title: Interdependence of Living Systems
description: Integrated study of the biotic and abiotic environments combining life, earth, and physical science concepts to understand relationships in living systems. For pre-service and in-service middle school teachers.
subject: SMED
prereq: Prerequisites: introductory-level courses in life, earth, and physical sciences.

title: Origin and Evolution of Living and Non-Living Systems
description: Study of the earth system for pre-service and in-service middle school teachers, with emphasis on the origin, evolution, and interactions of the subsystems of the earth system.
subject: SMED
prereq: Prerequisites: introductory-level courses in life, earth, and physical sciences.

title: Data Analysis for Teachers
description: Introduction to statistics for elementary, middle, and high school teachers. The fundamentals of data collection, descriptive statistics, probability, and inference with special focus on methods of teaching statistical reasoning. For M.A.T. (excluding mathematics) / M.Ed. / M.T. and nondegree credit only. For M.A.T. (excluding mathematics) / M.Ed. / M.T. and nondegree credit only.
subject: SMED

title: Special Topics in Science for Teachers
description: Topics not likely to be incorporated into regular offerings, offered as seminars, workshops, and formal courses. Variable credit, depending on contact hours. May be taken more than once.
subject: SMED

title: Introductory Sociology
description: An introduction to sociological facts and principles: an analysis of group-making processes and products.
subject: SOCY

title: Social Structures
description: Selected theoretical orientations, methodological procedures, and illustrative substantive data pertaining to social structures.
subject: SOCY

title: Sex and Gender
description: Offers a sociological lens to develop critical ways of thinking about sex and gender as social processes in everyday lives. This course considers how sex and gender shape and affect the experiences of women, men, girls, boys, and individuals who live in the spaces in-between (those who are intersex or transgender) across a wide range of social institutions (family, work, education, politics, etc.).
subject: SOCY
prereq: Prerequisites: SOCY 101.

title: Race, Class, Gender, and Sexuality
description: Historical and contemporary power relationships in race, social class, gender, and sexual orientation.
subject: SOCY

title: Sociology of Families
description: Sociological perspectives related to various aspects of family behaviors, roles, and values.
subject: SOCY
prereq: Prerequisites: SOCY 101.

title: From Factory to Fast Food: Work in the New Economy
description: A sociological investigation of how changes in the U.S. labor market have shaped contemporary work conditions and how structural and social factors impact the quality of work for different groups.
subject: SOCY

title: Religion and Sociology
description: Religion isn’t just personal—it shapes politics, communities, health, and relationships. Examine how religion interacts with culture and society, and how society shapes religion.
subject: SOCY

title: Community Organization
description: An analysis of formal and informal organization, the interrelationships among public and private agencies, and means through which community action programs are initiated, coordinated, and maintained.
subject: SOCY

title: An Introduction to Social Inequality
description: A sociological analysis of the distribution of wealth and income in selected societies.
subject: SOCY

title: Social Demography
description: Introduction to key areas of population studies. Methodological approaches, time trends, regional differences, and contemporary policy issues.
subject: SOCY

title: Bureaucracy and Modern Society
description: Bureaucracies in the public and private sector, their internal dynamics and relationship to the social environment.
subject: SOCY

title: Sociology of Aging
description: Analysis of aging as a process of socialization and the status of older people in society, their roles in the community, demographic aspects of aging, and the impact of aging upon social institutions.
subject: SOCY

title: Global Population Issues
description: Overview of global population history, theory, statistics, and issues related to recent population trends.
subject: SOCY

title: Reproductive Governance and Resistance
description: An examination of reproductive health and rights in a global context.
subject: SOCY

title: Gender in Contemporary Families
description: An exploration of how gender shapes contemporary family life, focusing on roles, relationships, expectations, and inequalities in paid and unpaid labor across diverse families and contexts.
subject: SOCY

title: Introduction to Social Networks
description: An examination of the hidden power of social networks and their impact on everyday life. Explores how patterns of connections—among individuals, organizations, and even nation-state—shape everything from friendship and inequality to public health and global politics.
subject: SOCY

title: Comparative Early Childhood Policy in Industrialized Nations
description: An exploration of how countries around the world are working to improve the welfare of children, and what we in U.S. can learn from their policy innovations.
subject: SOCY

title: Individual and Society
description: Selected theoretical orientations, methodological procedures, and illustrative substantive data pertaining to the relations between the individual and society.
subject: SOCY

title: Sociology of Deviant Behavior
description: Theories, methodology, and substantive issues in the study of social deviance.
subject: SOCY

title: Sociology of Childhood
description: A consideration of the child in the family group, play group, school group, and community.
subject: SOCY

title: Sociology of Adolescence
description: Sociological perspectives and research findings related to adolescence.
subject: SOCY

title: The Social Psychology of Deviance
description: Introduction to the sociological study of deviance and social control.
subject: SOCY

title: Sociology of the Paranormal and Extraordinary Claims
description: Why do people believe in paranormal and other extraordinary claims such as ESP and UFOs? We explore the social forces and scientific principles that lead to such beliefs.
subject: SOCY

title: Introduction to Social Problems
description: Contemporary social issues such as poverty, health, the criminal justice system, globalization and the environment, their causes and possible solutions.
subject: SOCY

title: Juvenile Delinquency
description: Social factors in the development, identification, and treatment of delinquents.
subject: SOCY
prereq: Prerequisites: CRJU 101 OR SOCY 101.

title: Urban Sociology
description: Analysis of urban trends, characteristics, and functions of cities with reference to the social psychological factors in urban living. Attention is directed to the emergence of urbanism in the United States, with particular reference to the Southern region, and to institutions, problems, and city planning.
subject: SOCY

title: Sociology of Crime
description: Social factors in the development, identification, and treatment of criminals.
subject: SOCY

title: Collective Behavior
description: An analysis of crowds, publics, social movements, and the mass society in terms of their institutional and social psychological consequences.
subject: SOCY

title: Sociological Perspectives on Poverty in the U.S.
description: This is a course about the demographics, measurement, ideologies, and theories of U.S. poverty.
subject: SOCY

title: Sociology of Education
description: Analysis of educational institutions, organizations, processes, and their effects in contemporary society.
subject: SOCY

title: Sociology of Medicine and Health
description: Health and illness in relation to social institutions. The organization and professionalization of medicine and social barriers to medical care.
subject: SOCY

title: Society through Visual Media
description: Analysis of social phenomena and sociological questions through various forms of media, including films, TV, photography, and other visual media.
subject: SOCY
prereq: Prerequisites: SOCY 101.

title: Sociology of Sport
description: Theories, methods, and substantive issues in the study of sport in contemporary societies.
subject: SOCY

title: Sociological Research Methods
description: Qualitative and quantitative methods of sociological research.
subject: SOCY

title: Elementary Statistics for Sociologists
description: An introduction to concepts and application of quantitative methods, including descriptive and inferential statistics. Emphasis on analysis of empirical sociological data.
subject: SOCY

title: Sociological Theory
description: Examination of key ideas from classical, modern, and contemporary sociological theories.
subject: SOCY

title: Topics in Sociology
description: Reading and research on selected sociological topics. Course content varies and will be announced in the schedule of classes by title.
subject: SOCY

title: Independent Study
description: Contract approved by instructor, advisor, and department chair is required for undergraduate students.
subject: SOCY

title: Social Networks
description: Analysis of personal, social and organizational networks, their structural patterns, practical consequences, and principles of formation and change.
subject: SOCY

title: Family and Social Stratification
description: An analysis of the contemporary American family emphasizing social stratification, mobility, occupations, and urbanization.
subject: SOCY

title: Social Stratification
description: Theory and research in social stratification.
subject: SOCY

title: Social Structures in Communities
description: Interrelationships of major social structures within communities.
subject: SOCY

title: Social Organizations
description: Selected theoretical orientation, methodological procedures, and illustrative substantive issues pertaining to organizations.
subject: SOCY

title: Sociology of Social Control
description: Theories and issues relating to the definition of and response to crime and/or deviance.
subject: SOCY

title: Life Course Demographics
description: People's demographic lives, structural contexts, and social change. Emphasis on the socioeconomic context in which lives unfold.
subject: SOCY
prereq: Prerequisites: SOCY 310.

title: Sociology of Mental Health
description: Social factors in the development, identification, and treatment of mental illness.
subject: SOCY

title: Internal and International Migration
description: A survey of methods of analysis and research findings with emphasis on the social and economic concomitants of internal migration. Cultural, economic, and historical aspects of international migration. Effects of governmental policies on immigration and emigration. Examination of selected countries.
subject: SOCY

title: Urbanization
description: Analysis of urbanization using contemporary and historical data from developing societies. The demographic components of metropolitan growth and the changing structure of metropolitan communities.
subject: SOCY

title: Scientific Methods and Sociological Inquiry
description: Introduction to methods used to answer theoretical, empirical, and practical sociological questions, including scientific inquiry and research design.
subject: SOCY

title: Advanced Social Psychology
description: Advanced survey of social psychological perspectives and research on inequality, discrimination, power and status, cooperation and collective action, social norm and morality, networks and relationships.
subject: SOCY

title: Social Processes of Deviance Control
description: A systematic analysis of the interrelation among the creation, involvement, recognition, and control of deviance.
subject: SOCY

title: Selves and Social Transaction
description: A systematic analysis of interrelationships among social acts, selves, roles, transactions, and language.
subject: SOCY

title: Social Cognition and Cultural Change
description: An introduction to scholarship about culture as a feature of human cognition. Students examine theories and studies by social scientists about how humans learn and enact culture in social life.
subject: SOCY

title: Sociology of Law
description: Review of theoretical and empirical developments in the sociology of law, including classical and modern sociological theories of law and selected sociological themes of law in various social settings.
subject: SOCY

title: Sociology of Science
description: Interrelationships among society, culture, and contemporary science.
subject: SOCY

title: Sociology of Education and Inequality
description: Advanced inquiry into the relationship between education and inequality.
subject: SOCY

title: Advanced Sociological Theory
description: Theoretical perspectives on society and social behavior.
subject: SOCY

title: Real World Research Experience
description: Design and conduct of original research using sociological research methods to meet Carolina Core Integrative course requirement for the BA and the BS.
subject: SOCY
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in SOCY 101, SOCY 391, and SOCY 392.

title: Advanced Sociological Research Methods
description: Advanced survey of methods used in sociological research.
subject: SOCY

title: Sociology in Practice Internship Seminar
description: Experiential exploration of the connections between internships and sociology coursework through connection with local organizations
subject: SOCY
prereq: Prerequisites: B or better in SOCY 101 and two additional SOCY courses at the 300-level or higher; or departmental approval.

title: Selected Topics
description: Readings and research on selected sociological topics. Course and content varies and will be announced in the schedule of classes by title.
subject: SOCY
prereq: Prerequisites: SOCY 101.

title: Advanced Independent Study
description: Advanced Independent study. Contract approved by instructor, advisor, and department chair is required.
subject: SOCY
prereq: Prerequisites: SOCY 101.

title: Special Topics
description: Reading and research.
subject: SOCY
prereq: Prerequisites: SOCY 101.

title: Theoretical Foundations of Sociology
description: Survey of theoretical and empirical works of sociological scholars.
subject: SOCY

title: Theory Construction
description: Presentation and study of the major theory groups in contemporary sociology, including functionalism, exchange, and consistency theories. Anaysis of theoretical perspectives using criteria of logical consistency and adequacy of explanation. Techniques of building formal theory in sociology.
subject: SOCY

title: Selected Topics in Sociological Theory
subject: SOCY
prereq: Prerequisites: SOCY 710.

title: Survey of Research Methods
description: Survey of data-gathering techniques used in sociology including questionnaires, interviews, surveys, archival searches, experiments, and observational techniques.
subject: SOCY

title: Selected Sociological Topics in Methodology
description: Intensive focus on selected social research methods having applications to the study of sociology.
subject: SOCY

title: Culture and Cognition
description: An introduction to scholarship about culture as a feature of human cognition. Students examine, critique, and extend theories and studies by social scientists about how humans learn and enact culture in social life.
subject: SOCY

title: Selected Sociological Topics in Methodology
subject: SOCY
prereq: Prerequisites: SOCY 701.

title: Statistical Analysis in Sociology
description: Introduction to statistical analysis in sociology, including bivariate and multiple regression, correlation and analysis of variance.
subject: SOCY
prereq: Prerequisites: SOCY 700.

title: Topics in the Quantitative Analysis of Sociological Data
description: Recursive and non-recursive modelling, multiple regression using longitudinal data, event history analysis.
subject: SOCY
prereq: Prerequisites: SOCY 515.

title: Topics in the Analysis of Social Networks
description: Selected topics in the theory, measurement, and analysis of social networks.
subject: SOCY

title: Topics in Scaling and Measurement Methods
description: Selected topics in scaling and measurement of social science data emphasizing exploratory and descriptive techniques such as correspondence analysis, proximity scaling and contingency table representations.
subject: SOCY

title: Social Networks of Covert Groups: Theory and Practice
description: Review of theory and practical case studies for social networks that operate partially or fully in a covert manner.
subject: SOCY

title: Qualitative Methods for the Social and Behavioral Sciences
description: A survey of the theoretical foundations and practical applications of qualitative research methods in the social and behavioral sciences.
subject: SOCY

title: Selected Topics in the Quantitative Analysis of Sociological Data
subject: SOCY
prereq: Prerequisites: SOCY 700.

title: Social Demography
description: Classical and contemporary theories and analytical approaches to demography.
subject: SOCY

title: Sociology of the Family
description: Sociological theories of the family and social change.
subject: SOCY

title: Selected Topics in Demography
description: Introduction to selected research methodologies having applications to the study of demography.
subject: SOCY
prereq: Prerequisites: SOCY 515.

title: Population Perspectives of Gender, Sexuality and Health
description: Population perspectives and demographic approaches to the measurement of gender, sexuality, and health.
subject: SOCY

title: Social Structures and Inequality
description: Social inequality and stratification studied from a structural perspective, focusing on patterns in institutions that perpetuate inequality.
subject: SOCY

title: Race, Class, Gender, and Sexuality
description: Historical and contemporary dimensions of social inequality centered in race, social class, gender, and sexuality.
subject: SOCY

title: Selected Sociological Topics in Social Structures
subject: SOCY

title: Contemporary Group Processes
description: Theories and problems in contemporary groups processes; primary emphasis on theories of status, power, justice, emotion, and legitimacy.
subject: SOCY

title: Selected Sociological Topics in Social Psychology
subject: SOCY

title: Medical Sociology
description: Social and cultural meanings, determinants, and experiences of health and illness; organization of health care delivery system; impact of culture, roles, and relationships on patients and providers.
subject: SOCY

title: Selected Topics in Medical Sociology
description: Topics from Medical Sociology theory and research. May be repeated as content varies by title.
subject: SOCY

title: Sociology Proseminar
description: This seminar introduces graduate students in sociology to aspects of the sociological profession that are beyond the confines of sociological theory, methodology, and the discipline's substantive interests.
subject: SOCY

title: Teaching Sociology
description: An exploration of college teaching of sociology, including goals, means, and challenges.
subject: SOCY
prereq: Prerequisites: SOCY 515.

title: Introduction to Southern Studies 1580-1900
description: Examination of major social and cultural developments of American South from early exploration to 1900.
subject: SOST

title: Introduction to Southern Studies: The Twentieth Century
description: An topical examination of the American South ranging from Reconstruction to the Civil Rights Movement.
subject: SOST

title: Popular Musics of the U.S. South
description: Investigation of popular musics in the U.S. South from the nineteenth century to the present day. Various genres discussed within their sociocultural contexts, including Southern identities, technology, economics, media, and other factors.
subject: SOST

title: The Literary South
description: Principles, practices, and contexts of major literary works of the American South.
subject: SOST

title: Topics in the American South
description: Reading and research on selected interdisciplinary topics in Southern Studies. Course content varies and will be announced in the schedule of classes by title. May be repeated for credit under a different title.
subject: SOST

title: Topics in South Carolina
description: Reading and research on selected interdisciplinary topics about South Carolina. Course content varies and will be announced in the schedule of classes by title. May be repeated for credit under a different title.
subject: SOST

title: The Contemporary South
description: An investigation of Southern regional identity.
subject: SOST

title: Barbecue: Southern History and Culture on a Plate
description: An examination of barbecue as a historical, sociological, and cultural phenomenon in the South.
subject: SOST

title: Independent Study
description: Contract approved by instructor, advisor, and program director is required for undergraduates.
subject: SOST

title: Topics in Southern Studies
description: Reading and research on selected topics in Southern studies. Course content varies and will be announced in the schedule of classes by title.
subject: SOST

title: Topics in the American South
description: Selected topics related to the study of the American South. Course content varies and will be announced in the schedule of classes by title. May be repeated for credit as topics vary.
subject: SOST

title: Southern Discomfort: Public Health in the American South
description: Investigation of the unique health and disease profile of the American South, including regional disparities that remain unresolved despite a public health revolution. Topics range from endemic diseases of the antebellum period to the current HIV/AIDS crisis, and ethics of research.
subject: SOST

title: Introduction to Social Work
description: This course introduces students to the history and contemporary context of the social work profession, including generalist practice and the role of advocacy. It emphasizes responses to current needs and changing contexts.
subject: SOWK

title: Social Welfare Policies and Programs
description: This course introduces students to the historical and current context of social welfare policies and programs in the United States, including examination of social inequalities and the processes of social policy development.
subject: SOWK

title: Social Work With Children, Youth, and Families
description: This course introduces the context for social work with children, youth, and families as well as specific theories, policies, programs, and services. Areas examined include antipoverty policies and programs, child welfare, education, behavioral health, healthcare, disabilities, and juvenile justice.
subject: SOWK
prereq: Prerequisites: PSYC 101, SOCY 101, or SOWK 201.

title: Social Work With Older Adults and Families
description: This course introduces students to the context for social work with older adults, as well as specific theories, policies, programs, and services. Areas examined include the aging process, health and wellness, behavioral health, sexuality, work and retirement, bereavement, living arrangements and housing options, and social programs and policies targeting older adults.
subject: SOWK
prereq: Prerequisites: PSYC 101, SOCY 101, or SOWK 201.

title: Gender, Race and Social Justice
description: This course introduces anti-oppressive theoretical and applied approaches to understand and address intersections of gender and racial inequality. Social welfare policies and services available to women in contexts such as criminal justice, behavioral health, child welfare, and the military are examined.
subject: SOWK

title: Global Inequalities and Social Justice
description: This course introduces students to global dimensions of social inequality, beginning with the historical roots of global social inequality and moving through contemporary themes relevant to international social work practice from an anti-colonial perspective. Topics examined include human rights, gender-based violence, global health, climate change, and immigration/displacement.
subject: SOWK
prereq: Prerequisites: PSYC 101, SOCY 101, or SOWK 201.

title: Promoting Positive Youth Development through Sport, Recreation, and Play
description: Exploration of how sport, recreation, and play can promote positive youth development. Based on an ecological perspective and a risk and resilience framework.
subject: SOWK

title: Life Transitions: Loss and Grief
description: This course will examine the grief and loss process that is part of everyday life. This course surveys the knowledge and skills of human service practice with people as they cope with life transitions.
subject: SOWK
prereq: Prerequisites: PSYC 101, or SOCY 101, or SOWK 201.

title: Foundations of Social Work Practice
description: This course introduces students to the context and mission of social work. It emphasizes the interactions between personal and professional values, ethical and professional behavior, and beginning engagement and interpersonal skills.
subject: SOWK

title: Social Work Practice with Individuals and Families
description: This course introduces students to engagement, assessment, intervention, and evaluation with individuals and families. It includes specific models and techniques of practice with an emphasis on managing barriers to change, client strengths, and understanding diversity and difference in practice.
subject: SOWK
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in SOWK 311.

title: Social Justice and Social Movements
description: Introduction to perspectives on justice with the goal of developing a theoretically informed social justice framework and exploring the complexity of social justice. Selected social movements will be introduced and analyzed.
subject: SOWK

title: Spirituality in Social Work
description: Comprehensive introduction to spiritually sensitive social work; emphasizes reflective practice, assessment skills, and the integration of spirituality into professional social work.
subject: SOWK
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in SOWK 201 or SOWK 311.

title: Social Work Case Management
description: Introduction to generalist social work case management models and skills with an emphasis on diverse and vulnerable populations.
subject: SOWK

title: Trauma Informed Practice
description: Introduction to trauma-informed practice for various populations as well as specific theories and services. Areas examined include traumatic stress, grief reactions, cognitive, neurobiological/physiological, psychological, and ecological perspectives.
subject: SOWK

title: Social Policy Analysis
description: Knowledge, values, and skills for social work practitioners to analyze the political and ideological factors and outcomes of policy development, implementation, evaluation, and advocacy.
subject: SOWK
prereq: Prerequisites: SOWK 201, SOWK 222.

title: Diversity and Social Justice in Contemporary Society
description: Builds cultural competency through awareness, understanding, and skill necessary for proactive functioning in our diverse society with populations considered vulnerable and oppressed.
subject: SOWK

title: Theories for Understanding Individuals, Families, and Groups
description: This course focuses on theories for understanding individual, family, and group behavior to better serve clients across settings. It emphasizes diversity, difference, and the advancement of human rights and social, economic, and environmental justice.
subject: SOWK
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in SOWK 201.

title: Social Work and Scientific Inquiry
description: Foundational knowledge and skills for scientific inquiry and the use of quantitative and qualitative research to inform evidence-based practice.
subject: SOWK
prereq: Prerequisites: SOWK 201, SOWK 222 and select one course from the following: STAT 201 or higher, PSYC 227 or SOCY 220.

title: Refuge and Refugees
description: This course provides students with foundational knowledge about forced migration and the institutions of humanitarian aid that address forced migration. Students will learn about global theories of forced migration, humanitarian aid policy, empirical studies of humanitarian aid, refugee resettlement, and displacement, and evaluate biographies and literature on forced migration.
subject: SOWK

title: Special Topics in Social Work
description: An in-depth study of selected issues and social concerns related to being a generalist social worker. Content varies by title.
subject: SOWK

title: Introduction to Field Education
description: An initial application of generalist social work skills and theories taught in a controlled and planned classroom setting.
subject: SOWK
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in SOWK 311.

title: Independent Study
description: Requires permission of the department.
subject: SOWK

title: Current Issues in Social Welfare
description: A project-type study of selected issues, social concerns, and applications of behavioral implications for practice. May be repeated for credit when the topics covered or subject matter is different.
subject: SOWK

title: Social Work Practice with Groups
description: This course introduces students to engagement, assessment, intervention, and evaluation with treatment and task groups. It includes specific models and techniques of practice with an emphasis on group practice with diverse populations across practice settings.
subject: SOWK
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in SOWK 312.

title: Social Work Practice with Organizations and Communities
description: This course introduces students to engagement, assessment, intervention, and evaluation with organizations and communities. It includes specific models and techniques of practice with an emphasis on diversity, difference, and the advancement of human rights and social, economic, and environmental justice.
subject: SOWK
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in SOWK 411.

title: Advocacy for Social and Economic Justice
description: Knowledge and skills embedded in values of social and economic justice for all people across systems of all sizes that are essential in generalist practice.
subject: SOWK
prereq: Prerequisites: SOWK 322.

title: Theories for Understanding Organizations and Communities
description: This course focuses on theories for understanding large social systems to better serve clients across settings. It emphasizes diversity, difference, and the advancement of human rights and social, economic, and environmental justice.
subject: SOWK

title: Practicum I: Field Education
description: Application of generalist social work competencies, theories and code of ethics taught in the classroom in a community organizational setting.
subject: SOWK

title: Practicum II: Field Education
description: Further application of the generalist social work skills and theories taught in the classroom in a controlled and planned setting.
subject: SOWK

title: Practicum Seminar
description: Taken with the field practicum, this seminar facilitates student's integration of generalist practice knowledge with practice in a field agency.
subject: SOWK
prereq: Prerequisites: SOWK 382.

title: Capstone
description: Competent generalist social work practice demands integration of a wide range of theories, knowledge, skills, and values. This course is designed to draw on all previous courses and to engage students in integrating and applying all that they have learned.
subject: SOWK

title: Special Topics in Social Work
description: Study of special populations, settings, and/or problems encountered by social workers and other human service professionals, and interventions and skills for dealing with them. May be repeated as content varies by title.
subject: SOWK

title: Transforming Health Care for the Future
description: Foundation for beginning health professions students to gain an understanding of the complexities of the health care system through experiential activities conducted in interprofessional teams and the importance of interprofessional collaboration in order to improve the system.
subject: SOWK

title: Addressing Childhood Obesity through Community Approaches
description: Approaches for prevention of childhood obesity, using perspectives from public health, social work, exercise science, pharmacy, medicine, and behavioral nutrition. Training to teach diet/physical activity lessons in elementary school settings.
subject: SOWK

title: Professional Development Bridge
description: Bridge course designed to prepare newly enrolled Advanced Standing MSW students for their Advanced Practice year of study for the MSW program.
subject: SOWK

title: Writing for Professional Social Work Pratice
description: Prepare students to write effectively for professional practice in social work.
subject: SOWK

title: Richland County Sheriff's Department School Practicum
description: This seminar is part of a collaborative school social work/law enforcement practicum immersion study. Permission of the instructor and concurrent enrollment in an instructor-approved field practicum is required.
subject: SOWK

title: Nonprofit Leadership
description: This course provides students with foundational knowledge for leading and managing nonprofit or nongovernmental organizations. The student will learn about governance, human resources, financial management, marketing, and planning and evaluation. In this class, the student will use theory to understand effective organizational practices in a modern era. This course counts as a practice elective.
subject: SOWK

title: Family Interventions and Family Therapy
description: Social work practice with families, parents, and couples, focused on family interventions and family therapy with culturally diverse and vulnerable family populations. This course counts as a practice elective.
subject: SOWK

title: Trauma-Informed Practice with Children, Youth, and Families
description: Integration of trauma into practice with children, youth, and families to recognize its prevalence, realize its impact, and respond sensitively and competently.
subject: SOWK

title: Trauma-Informed Practice with Adults and Older Adults
description: Integration of trauma into practice with adults and older adults to recognize its prevalence, realize its impact, and respond sensitively and competently.
subject: SOWK

title: Collective Trauma and Recovery
description: Designed to focus on trauma-informed, trauma responsive, and culturally sensitive engagement, assessment, intervention, and advocacy regarding collective experiences of trauma such as natural and environmental disasters, mass shootings, community violence, and war.
subject: SOWK

title: Human Behavior and the Social Environment I
description: Study of institutions, communities, and organizations as social systems relevant to social work practice.
subject: SOWK

title: Diversity and Social Justice Issues for Social Work Practice
description: Diversity, strengths, needs, and responses of oppressed populations from a social justice perspective, with emphasis on experiential learning and implications for social work practice at all system levels.
subject: SOWK

title: Human Behavior and the Social Environment II
description: A systems approach is used to study the family and individual development. Cultural and structural variability are emphasized.
subject: SOWK

title: Systems Analysis of Social Work Practice
description: Integration and application of social work theories, skills, and values in preparation for the transition to professional practice.
subject: SOWK
prereq: Prerequisites: Full-time, Part-time, and Advanced Standing students: C or higher in SOWK 779, SOWK 783, and SOWK 792 or SOWK 793.

title: Social Work Practice with Individuals, Families and Small Groups
description: Methods of social work intervention with individuals, families and groups within the social environment.
subject: SOWK

title: Supervision and Case Consultation
description: An in-depth study of modalities for overseeing the delivery of direct services in social agencies. This course qualifies as a Practice Elective.
subject: SOWK

title: Social Work in an Educational Setting
description: Examination of school social work services from a multi-level, ecological approach including the community, the school, the family, and the students. This course qualifies as a Practice Elective.
subject: SOWK

title: Social Work Case Management
description: Practice of social work case management with a special emphasis on case management for vulnerable populations. This course qualifies as a Practice Elective.
subject: SOWK

title: Cognitive Behavioral Therapies
description: Knowledge and skills for practice with cognitive behavior therapies. This course qualifies as a Practice Elective.
subject: SOWK

title: Trauma-informed Social Work Practice
description: Integration and infusion of meaning of trauma into one’s practice so as to recognize its prevalence, realize its impact, and respond sensitively and competently. This course qualifies as a Practice Elective.
subject: SOWK

title: Motivational Interviewing for Social Work Practice
description: Motivational interviewing strategies and advanced practice competencies within in a variety of social work practice settings. This course qualifies as a Practice Elective.
subject: SOWK

title: Social Work Practice with Organizations and Communities
description: Social work practice in organizations and communities, especially skills in problem identification and solving.
subject: SOWK

title: Advanced Social Work Practice with Individuals
description: Advanced social work practice with individuals focused on engagement, assessment, intervention planning and implementation, and practice evaluation for diverse clients.
subject: SOWK
prereq: Prerequisites: C or higher in SOWK 722 and SOWK 732.

title: Overview of Social Work Practice with the Military, Veterans, and their Families
description: Foundation knowledge for practice with military and their families including information about military culture, values. An overview of military knowledge and history essential for working with this population, information about problems unique to the military, and the identification of treatment and community resources.
subject: SOWK

title: Military Mental Health and the Impact of Trauma
description: Designed to provide state-of-the-art information about problems and disorders encounter by veterans and military personnel, including information about the signature injuries associated with current and past conflicts, as well as information about problems encountered in family life.
subject: SOWK

title: Intervention Strategies in Military Behavioral Health
description: Knowledge about intervention strategies with military members, veterans of the military and their families, including important knowledge to help social workers interface effectively with other medical and behavioral health professionals.
subject: SOWK

title: International Social Work and Social Justice
description: This course provides a framework for understanding social work and social justice issues with an emphasis on vulnerable populations in the areas of survival, protection, and promotion of human rights in China, India, the Middle East, Africa, and Central and South America.
subject: SOWK

title: Social Welfare Policy Analysis
description: Analysis of social welfare policies, including their development, implementation, and evaluation.
subject: SOWK

title: Immigration Policy
description: This course examines immigration policy in the contemporary U.S. context. It explores current policy debates in light migration research and theory, and aims to address the following questions: Why do people migrate? How do they adapt once they arrive? How does immigration impact receiving societies?
subject: SOWK

title: Grant Writing
description: Planning and program development through grant writing that can be generalized to any setting and relevant to social, community, and economic development as well as other areas of social work practice.
subject: SOWK

title: Community Mental Health
description: Course develops skills for effective social work practice within dynamic and multifaceted mental health settings. Substantive areas include role play, research informed practice and practice informed research, and reflective/reflexive practice. The primary goal is to produce practitioners capable of influencing mental health related practice paradigms, programs, services, interventions, and policies. This course qualifies as a Practice Elective.
subject: SOWK

title: Crisis Intervention
description: This course introduces students to crisis theory and intervention models for response to a variety of individual, family, and community crises. This course focuses on crisis theories, the tasks associated with crisis care, methods of intervention and the responses to specific crises.
subject: SOWK

title: Evidence Based Parenting Interventions Child Adolescent Social, Emotional, Behavioral Challenges
description: Examines theories of practice and parenting interventions with strong empirical evidence for supporting positive youth development and preventing or ameliorating child and adolescent social, emotional, and behavioral challenges among diverse youth and parents.
subject: SOWK

title: Youth and Substance Use
description: Students are introduced to the unique dynamics of substance use among adolescent populations. Students gain foundational knowledge about adolescent development, substance use trends and prevalence among adolescent populations. Risk and protective factors are explored that impact adolescent substance use, and systems of care for this unique group.
subject: SOWK

title: Social Work Intervention in Substance Abuse
description: Knowledge and skills for substance abuse treatment for clients from diverse backgrounds with a focus on empirically based methods from a social work practice.
subject: SOWK

title: Advanced Social Work Practice with Organizations
description: Advanced social work practice with organizations, including advanced knowledge, skills, values, and theories to engage, assess, intervention, and evaluation organizations.
subject: SOWK
prereq: Prerequisites: C or higher in SOWK 722 and SOWK 732.

title: Social Work Practice and Developmental Disabilities
description: Explores values, addresses psychosocial issues and examines assessment and intervention tools important for practice with persons with disabilities, their families and the community.
subject: SOWK

title: Family Dynamics and Substance Abuse
description: An examination of the dynamics of substance abuse and addictions including classification of drugs, models of addiction, etiology, assessment and diagnosis and evidence-based treatment practices. Family dynamics, special populations (e.g., women, African Americans, Military), and process addictions (e.g., gambling, sexual addiction, eating disorders) are also covered.
subject: SOWK

title: Psychopathology and Psychodiagnostics for Social Work Practice with Children and Adolescents
description: This course introduces students to etiology, assessment, and diagnosis of child and adolescent mental health within the social work person-in-environment framework to understand the critical biopsychosocial influences on incidence and manifestation of the most commonly presented disorders and the differential effect of these factors on diverse and at-risk populations.
subject: SOWK

title: Psychopathology and Psychodiagnostics for Social Work Practice with Adults and Older Adults
description: This course introduces students to etiology, assessment, and diagnosis of adult and older adult mental health within the social work person-in-environment framework to understand the critical biopsychosocial influences on incidence and manifestation of the most commonly presented disorders and the differential effect of these factors on diverse and at-risk populations.
subject: SOWK

title: Loss, Grief, and Social Work Intervention
description: Losses encountered throughout the life cycle, normal and pathological grieving, and intervention techniques.
subject: SOWK

title: Independent Study
description: For advanced graduate students.
subject: SOWK

title: Sexuality Issues for Social Work Practice
description: Sexuality in the context of social functioning and its relationship to problems encountered by social work practitioners. Emphasis on problems of sexual oppression.
subject: SOWK

title: Advanced Social Work Practice with Families
description: Advanced generalist social work practice with diverse family systems across the life span, focused on engagement, assessment, intervention, and evaluation processes.
subject: SOWK
prereq: Prerequisites: C or higher in SOWK 722 and SOWK 732.

title: Special Topics in Social Work
description: An in-depth study of selected issues, social concerns, and application of behavioral implications for practice. May be repeated as content varies by title.
subject: SOWK

title: Interdisciplinary Perspectives on Child Abuse and Neglect
description: Current knowledge about child abuse and neglect, including typologies, etiology, effects, and current practice interventions.
subject: SOWK

title: Psychosocial Approaches to Gerontology
description: Introduction to gerontology from the fields of demography, psychology, sociology, social welfare, and economics.
subject: SOWK

title: Programs and Services for Older Adults
description: Examination of the policy/planning issues relating to older adults, including current trends in services, base for social service development, and evaluation of services for older adults.
subject: SOWK

title: Advanced Social Work Practice with Groups
description: Advanced social work practice with treatment and task groups, with a focus on engagement, assessment, intervention, evaluation, and policy practice.
subject: SOWK
prereq: Prerequisites: C or higher in SOWK 722 and SOWK 732.

title: Advanced Social Work Practice with Communities
description: Advanced generalist social work practice on a community system-level with a focus on engagement, assessment, intervention, and evaluation.
subject: SOWK
prereq: Prerequisites: C or higher in SOWK 722 and SOWK 732.

title: Advanced Theory for Social Work Practice
description: A theoretical background for specialized advanced social work practice incorporating social and behavioral science as a framework for analyzing evidence-based approaches for social work interventions.
subject: SOWK
prereq: Prerequisites: Full-time and Part-time Programs: C or better in SOWK 712, SOWK 714, SOWK 716, SOWK 781, and SOWK 791; Advanced Standing Program: None.

title: Advanced Analysis of Social Policy, Programs, and Services
description: Overview of the structure and functions of programs, policies, and systems in a specialized area of practice, including the history of policy development, the current policy environment, and the role of social workers and the social work profession in shaping and implementing policy in this area.
subject: SOWK
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in SOWK 777, SOWK 779, and SOWK 783.

title: Advanced Social Work Interventions
description: Advanced direct practice in a specialized area with a focus on engagement, assessment, intervention planning and implementation, and practice evaluation for diverse client systems at multiple levels. Instructor permission required for non-MSW program students.
subject: SOWK
prereq: Prerequisites: Full-time and Part-time Programs: SOWK 722, SOWK 732, SOWK 742, SOWK 777, SOWK 782; Advanced Standing Program: SOWK 777.

title: Field Instruction I: Generalist Social Work Practice
description: An agency-based study of the community social welfare system and the social agency's place in delivery of services; a beginning involvement in agency practice.
subject: SOWK

title: Field Instruction II: Generalist Social Work Practice
description: An agency-based study of the community social welfare system and the social agency's place in delivery of services; a beginning involvement in agency practice.
subject: SOWK
prereq: Prerequisites: Full-time and Extended-time Programs: C or better in SOWK 781.

title: Field Instruction III: Advanced Social Work Practice
description: The third unit of field instruction is designed to support students’ integration of knowledge learned while taking advanced year practice courses. This is a pass or fail course.
subject: SOWK
prereq: Prerequisites: Full-time and Extended-time Programs: C or higher in SOWK 722, SOWK 732, SOWK 742, and SOWK 782; Advanced Standing Program: None.

title: Field Instruction IV: Advanced Social Work Practice
description: The fourth unit of field instruction is designed to support students’ integration of knowledge learned while taking advanced year practice courses. This is a pass or fail course.
subject: SOWK
prereq: Prerequisites: Passing grade in SOWK 783.

title: Social Work Research Methodologies
description: Examination of social work research contexts, designs, and strategies.
subject: SOWK

title: Evaluation of Social Work Practice
description: Examines measurement tools, evaluation designs, and advanced practice skills that can be used to evaluate social work practice with clients.
subject: SOWK
prereq: Prerequisites: Full-time and Part-time Programs: C or higher in SOWK 791; Advanced Standing Program: None.

title: Evaluation of Social Work Programs
description: Examines methods that can be used to evaluate social work programs, policies, and practice.
subject: SOWK
prereq: Prerequisites: Full-time and Part-time Programs: C or higher in SOWK 791; Advanced Standing Program: None.

title: Intellectual Foundations of Social Welfare and Social Work I: Historical Roots
description: Examines across disciplines the theoretical and empirical foundations for social welfare and social work in historical, economic, social, and political contexts prior to the 20th century.
subject: SOWK

title: Intellectual Foundations of Social Welfare and Social Work II: Modern Developments
description: Examines across disciplines the theoretical and empirical foundations of social welfare and social work in historical, economic, social, and political contexts from the early 20th century to the present.
subject: SOWK
prereq: Prerequisites: B or better in SOWK 800.

title: Intellectual Foundations of Social Welfare and Social Work III: Conceptual Model-Building
description: Examines the process of developing theory-based welfare and social work scholarship from a variety of research approaches, focusing on conceptual model-building.
subject: SOWK
prereq: Prerequisites: B or better in SOWK 800 and SOWK 801.

title: History of the Profession of Social Work
description: Examines the theoretical and empirical foundations of the profession of social work in historical, economic, social, and political contexts from the early 20th century to the present.
subject: SOWK

title: Understanding and Using Social Science Theory
description: Introduces the concept of theory and its relation to empirical investigation. Introduces middle range social science theories widely used for research in social work and social welfare.
subject: SOWK

title: Knowledge for Social Change: Ontology, Epistemology, and Methodology in Social Science Research
description: Overview of a range of approaches to understanding what we can know as social scientists (ontology) and how we can know it (epistemology), and a deep dive into how these approaches play out in 1) understandings of scientific inquiry and core methodological concepts, 2) qualitative and quantitative methodological choices, and 3) the researcher’s role and positionality in advancing change-oriented knowledge claims.
subject: SOWK

title: Conducting a Systematic Literature Review for Publication
description: Step-by-step approach to conducting a systematic review of scientific literature and summarizing findings in the format of a journal manuscript for potential publication.
subject: SOWK

title: Qualitative Methods of Inquiry for Social Work Research
description: Foundations of qualitative methods in social research with emphasis on intensive interviewing and grounded theory.
subject: SOWK

title: Measurement and Instrument Design
description: Advanced study to evaluate and design measurement and instrumentation in social work research. Restricted to social work doctoral students.
subject: SOWK
prereq: Prerequisites: SOWK 891, SOWK 892.

title: Community - Engaged Research for Social Welfare and Social Change
description: Doctoral-level course covering conceptual foundations and key processes and skills of community-engaged research for understanding and promoting social welfare and social change. Emphasis on engagement with community, collaboration, challenges, and ethics. Assignments include practical community-engaged research experience.
subject: SOWK

title: Leadership for Social Change
description: Examines theoretical and practical foundations of providing leadership for social change through organizations, communities, public policies, and social norms.
subject: SOWK

title: Social Work Doctoral Professional Seminar
description: Examines issues related to making a successful transition from doctoral student to professional social work scholar. May be repeated for credit.
subject: SOWK

title: Social Work Education Practicum
description: A wide range of supervised classroom, field, and other learning experiences designed to prepare the student for work as a social work educator.
subject: SOWK
prereq: Prerequisites: SOWK 871.

title: Doctoral Social Work Practicum
description: Students acquire practical research experience, based on an individualized learning contract, under the supervision of a faculty member.
subject: SOWK

title: Analysis of Social Work Data
description: Approaches to the organization, analysis, interpretation, and utilization of data sets available from social agency records or from existing empirical research.
subject: SOWK

title: Advanced Analysis of Social Work Data
description: Analysis of complex data sets from social services agencies and other research sources.
subject: SOWK
prereq: Prerequisites: B or better in SOWK 890 or equivalent introductory statistics course approved by the Doctoral Program Committee.

title: Design and Critical Analysis of Social Work Research
description: Advanced study of research methods commonly employed in the development of knowledge for social work practice and education. Critique of published social work research using a standardized critique model.
subject: SOWK

title: Planning and Design of Dissertation Research
description: A seminar designed to provide intensive faculty supervision and peer consultation to the doctoral student in the preparation of the dissertation proposal. Repeatable- 2 credits required for the doctoral program.
subject: SOWK
prereq: Prerequisites: SOWK 890, SOWK 891, SOWK 892, SOWK 811.

title: Dissertation Preparation
subject: SOWK
prereq: Prerequisites: SOWK 894.

title: Beginning Spanish I
description: Introduction to grammar and practical vocabulary necessary for fundamental communication skills. Restricted to those who have never studied Spanish or placed by examination into SPAN 109. Credit may be received only for one of the following: SPAN 109, SPAN 110 ; SPAN 111 ; or SPAN 121 .
subject: SPAN

title: Beginning Spanish II
description: Introduction to grammar and practical vocabulary necessary for fundamental communication skills. Restricted to those who have completed SPAN 109 . Credit may be received only for one of the following: SPAN 109 , SPAN 110; SPAN 111 ; or SPAN 121 .
subject: SPAN

title: Intensive Beginning Spanish
description: Intensive introduction to grammar and practical vocabulary necessary for fundamental communication skills. Admission only to highly motivated beginning students who obtain the permission of the department. Credit may be received only for one of the following: SPAN 109 , SPAN 110 , SPAN 111, or SPAN 121 .
subject: SPAN

title: Elementary Spanish
description: Grammar and vocabulary necessary for fundamental communication skills. Assumes prior experience in Spanish. Admission only by proficiency examination. Credit may be received for only one of the following: SPAN 109 , SPAN 110 , SPAN 111 , or SPAN 121.
subject: SPAN

title: Basic Proficiency in Spanish
description: Practice and further development of essential listening, reading, speaking, and writing skills.
subject: SPAN
prereq: Prerequisites: D or better in SPAN 109 and SPAN 110, or SPAN 111, or SPAN 121 or by placement examination.

title: Intermediate Spanish I
description: Further development of listening, speaking, reading, and writing skills. Use of authentic cultural materials.
subject: SPAN
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in SPAN 122 or by Placement Exam.

title: Intermediate Spanish II
description: Continued development of the four skills practiced in SPAN 209 .
subject: SPAN
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in SPAN 209 or by Placement Exam.

title: Intensive Intermediate Spanish
description: Further development of listening, speaking, reading, and writing skills. Designed for highly motivated students. Credit not awarded for both SPAN 209 - SPAN 210 and SPAN 211.
subject: SPAN
prereq: Prerequisites: SPAN 122 or placement at 209 level on Phase II placement exam.

title: Selected Works of Hispanic Literature in English Translation
description: Selected major works, especially contemporary works, in all genres of Hispanic literature in English translation.
subject: SPAN

title: Spanish Language in Society
description: Introduction to basic concepts of sociolinguistics as they apply to the Spanish language.
subject: SPAN

title: Service Learning in Spanish
description: Contract approved by instructor, director, and department chair required. May be repeated. Maximum of 3 hours may apply towards major or minor.
subject: SPAN
prereq: Prerequisites: SPAN 210.

title: Advanced Spanish
description: In-depth study of advanced grammatical structures of Spanish to develop proficiency in all 4 skills and cultural competency. Gateway course for Spanish majors.
subject: SPAN
prereq: Prerequisites: B or better in SPAN 210, SPAN 211 or SPAN 230 or by placement.

title: Cultural Readings and Advanced Composition
description: Development of advanced composition skills in Spanish on a variety of topics related to cultural production of the Spanish-speaking world.
subject: SPAN
prereq: Prerequisites: B or better in SPAN 210, SPAN 211, or by placement.

title: Cultural Readings and Advanced Conversation
description: Cultural readings about the Spanish-speaking world, and advanced speaking skills practice through various strategies such as group discussions, debates, presentations.
subject: SPAN
prereq: Prerequisites: B or better in SPAN 210 or SPAN 211, or by placement.

title: Working with Spanish-Speaking Communities
description: Crosscultural approaches to interactions with persons of Hispanic origin in a variety of professional settings. Readings, speakers, media. Taught in Spanish. Departmental permission required for transfer students.
subject: SPAN

title: Spanish for Heritage Speakers
description: Intensive grammar practice, enhancement of reading and writing skills for individuals raised in a Spanish-speaking household but with little or no formal Spanish instruction. Restricted to heritage speakers, as defined in Bulletin description.
subject: SPAN
prereq: Prerequisites: Placement by Phase II Exam.

title: Introduction to Reading Hispanic Literary Texts
description: Approaches to reading literary texts through carefully selected readings from different genres.
subject: SPAN
prereq: Prerequisites: SPAN 303 or by placement. D or better for non-Spanish majors. C or better for Spanish majors and minors.

title: Business Spanish
description: Commercial organizations and business in Spanish-speaking countries, business correspondence, terminology, and techniques in commercial transactions. Standardized examinations available such as the Certificado de la Camara de Comercio de Madrid.
subject: SPAN
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in SPAN 302 or by placement.

title: Spanish Phonetics and Pronunciation
description: Analysis of and practice in pronunciation, listening comprehension, and dialect recognition based on study of the speech sounds, combinations, patterns, and processes of Spanish phonetics and phonology. Department permission required for transfer students.
subject: SPAN
prereq: Prerequisites: C+ or better in SPAN 302; placement at 300 level of Phase II placement exam.

title: Spanish Language Study Abroad
description: Intensive language practice in native environment with emphasis on oral skills. Instruction by native speakers; community contact, and home stay. May be repeated once for credit. Restricted to participants of the USC Spanish Summer Global Classroom Program.
subject: SPAN

title: Spanish for Healthcare Professionals
description: Health professionals' functional and lexical language ability, cultural information, etiquette and protocol necessary to interact with Spanish speakers.
subject: SPAN
prereq: Prerequisites: SPAN 302 or by placement.

title: Special Topics in Hispanic Literature
description: Course content treating cultural and literary themes varies.
subject: SPAN
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in SPAN 303 or by placement.

title: Special Topics in Hispanic Language and Culture
description: Course content varies and will be announced in the schedule of classes by title. May be repeated as content varies by title.
subject: SPAN
prereq: Prerequisites: C or higher in SPAN 302; Placement Exam score of SD or S7.

title: The Cinema of Spain
description: Investigation of Spanish cultures through the study of its films and the cinematic medium.
subject: SPAN
prereq: Prerequisites: SPAN 303 or by placement.

title: The Cinema of Latin America
description: Investigation of Latin American cultures through the study of films and the cinematic medium.
subject: SPAN
prereq: Prerequisites: Placement at 300 level on Phase II placement exam, grade of C+ or better in SPAN 303, or consent of instructor. Department permission required for transfer students.

title: Ecological Cultural Studies in Latin America
description: Examination of the relationships between the environment and culture through film, literature, visual arts, performances, and other works from Latin America.
subject: SPAN

title: Special Topics in Hispanic Studies
description: Intensive study of selected topics of the Hispanic world. Taught in English.
subject: SPAN

title: Independent Study

subject: SPAN

title: Spanish Culture
description: Survey of the cultures of Spain via readings, visual culture, and discussion.
subject: SPAN

description: Survey of the cultures of Latin America via readings, visual culture, and discussion.

title: Spanish Literature
description: A survey of literature, such as novels, short stories and poems, from Spain.
subject: SPAN
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in SPAN 312.

title: Latin American Literature
description: A survey of literature, such as novels, short stories and poems, from Latin America.
subject: SPAN

title: Advanced Spanish for Business and the Professions
description: Vocabulary, concepts, and oral/written skills necessary to communicate effectively in the social, cultural, or economic infrastructure of Hispanic countries. Introduction to the use of technology for the acquisition and processing of materials relevant to students’ professional goals.
subject: SPAN

title: Advanced Special Topics in Hispanic Literature
description: Course content varies and will be announced in the schedule of classes by title. May be repeated as content varies by title.
subject: SPAN

title: Advanced Special Topics in Hispanic Language and Culture
description: Course content varies and will be announced in the schedule of classes by title. May be repeated as content varies by title. This course will be taught in Spanish.
subject: SPAN
prereq: Prerequisites: D or better in SPAN 303 or by placement.

title: Advanced Special Topics in Hispanic Studies
description: Course content varies and will be announced in the schedule of classes by title. May be repeated as content varies by title. Taught in English.
subject: SPAN

description: A special seminar devoted to the in-depth study of selected subjects in Hispanic literature, culture, or language. Required for the intensive major in Spanish.
subject: SPAN

title: Contemporary Spain
description: Analysis and discussion of 20th and 21st-century Spanish history and the sociocultural forces that have contributed to define this country’s national identity. Taught in Spanish.
subject: SPAN

description: Analysis and discussion of contemporary Latin American history and the sociocultural forces that have contributed to define this area's national identities. Taught in Spanish.
subject: SPAN
prereq: Prerequisites: Placement at 300-level on Phase II placement exam or C or better in SPAN 303. Department permission required for transfer students.

description: Introduction to translation and practice of skills required for professional and technical Spanish/English translation.
subject: SPAN
prereq: Prerequisites: SPAN 409.

title: Introduction to Spanish Linguistics
description: Phonology, morphology, and syntax of modern Spanish.
subject: SPAN
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in SPAN 303.

title: The Structure of Modern Spanish

subject: SPAN

subject: SPAN

description: Topics will be identified by title in the schedule of classes. Course can be repeated for credit for a maximum of 6 hours.
subject: SPAN

subject: SPAN

description: Topics will be identified by title in the schedule of classes. Course can be repeated for credit for a maximum of 6 hours. Restricted to Graduate Students.

subject: SPAN

title: Public Communication
description: Introduction to theory and practice of oral communication in public, social, and institutional contexts. Includes foundational and cumulative training in the invention, performance, and critical analysis of oral communication, with emphasis on argumentation, persuasion, audience analysis, delivery, and ethical forms of engagement.
subject: SPCH

Training in invention, performance, and critical analysis of online spoken communication, including audience analysis, persuasion, delivery, and ethical engagement. Includes significant practice in preparing and presenting live online public communication.
subject: SPCH

subject: SPCH

subject: SPCH

subject: SPCH

subject: SPCH

subject: SPCH

title: Independent Study and Research
description: Contract approved by instructor, advisor, and department chair is required for undergraduate students.
subject: SPCH

subject: SPCH

subject: SPCH

subject: SPCH

subject: SPCH

subject: SPCH
prereq: Prerequisites: ENGL 101 and ENGL 102.

subject: SPCH
prereq: Prerequisites: ENGL 101 and ENGL 102.

subject: SPCH

subject: SPCH

subject: SPCH

subject: SPCH

subject: SPCH

subject: SPCH

subject: SPTE
