subject: WGST

title: The Family in Cross-Cultural Perspective
description: Kinship, systems of descent, marriage, and domestic organization in different cultures. Variations in childrearing practices, gender, and other aspects of social relations in kin groups.
subject: WGST

title: Gender and Politics
description: Impact of gender on the distribution of power in society; foundations for intersections of gender, race, social class, and sexuality and their economic, social, and political concomitants.
subject: WGST

title: Women and Science
description: The role of women and gender in the construction of western science from historical, philosophical and sociological perspectives.
subject: WGST

title: Women in Modern Europe
description: Survey of women in European history from the eighteenth to the twenty-first century. Focus on women’s citizenship beginning with Enlightenment idea of rights through developments in modern feminism.
subject: WGST

title: Gender and Globalization
description: Examines the dialectic between globalization and the social construction of gender. Topics include the global assembly line, transnational markets for domestic labor and sex workers, and global feminist alliances.
subject: WGST
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in ANTH 102 or WGST 112.

title: Cultures, Pregnancy, and Birth
description: Anthropological study of pregnancy and birth with a cross-cultural focus comparing the United States to other nations. Examination of cultural factors such as prenatal care, dietary practices, taboos, birth location, practitioners, and birthing styles.
subject: WGST

title: Global Women's Health
description: This course examines health concerns important in the lives of women around the world through an overview of contemporary issues and challenges in the field of global health, broadly construed.
subject: WGST

title: Special Topics in Women's and Gender Studies
description: Course content varies and will be announced in the schedule of courses by title. May be repeated as content varies by title.
subject: WGST

title: Independent Study
description: Contract approved by instructor, advisor, and director of women’s studies required for undergraduate students.
subject: WGST

title: Feminist Research Methods
description: Interdisciplinary overview of research methods used in feminist research.
subject: WGST
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in one of the following: WGST 112, WGST 304, WGST 307, or WGST 308.

title: Topics in Women's Studies
description: Course content varies and will be announced in the schedule of courses by title.
subject: WGST

title: Women's Studies Workshop
description: Selected small action-research project on selected issue(s) in women’s studies.
subject: WGST

title: Men and Masculinities
description: This course provides an overview of psychological, social, physical, and emotional issues related to men’s lives. Major topics include: gender construction, men and work, men and health, men in relationships, male sexualities, men in families, and masculinities in the media and popular culture.
subject: WGST

title: Women Love Power: Gender in Asia
description: A historical and global exploration of gender and sexuality in India and beyond.
subject: WGST

title: Women Writers
description: Representative works written by women.
subject: WGST
prereq: Prerequisites: C or higher in both ENGL 101 and ENGL 102.

title: LGTBQ+ Literature
description: This course will examine LGBTQ+ (lesbian, gay, bisexual, trans, queer, and other nonnormative sexual identities) literatures and cultures.
subject: WGST
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in ENGL 101 and ENGL 102.

title: LGBTQ History in the United States
description: Exploration of LGBTQ history and communities in America from the Colonial Era to the present, using lenses of gender and sexuality to understand historical events.
subject: WGST

title: Women and the Law
description: Constitutional and statutory case law dealing with gender equality issues. Topics include abortion, affirmative action, pornography, sexual harassment, fetal protection policies, employment discrimination, and women in the military.
subject: WGST

title: History of American Women
description: The social, political, and economic roles and changing status of women in America.
subject: WGST

title: Women’s Rhetoric
description: Study of rhetoric by and about women as manifested in speeches, essays, and other rhetorical artifacts.
subject: WGST
prereq: Prerequisites: ENGL 101 and ENGL 102.

title: Black Women Writers
description: An examination of literature by and about black women, including fiction, poetry, drama, and autobiography. This study will focus on issues that emerge from the creative representations of black women and the intersections of race, gender, sexuality, and class that interrogate what is both particular and universal experiences.
subject: WGST
prereq: Prerequisites: ENGL 101, ENGL 102.

title: Community Service Internship
description: Supervised experience addressing a community organization's needs and allowing the student to explore an aspect of the community related to women's and gender studies issues. Contract approval by advisor required.
subject: WGST
prereq: Prerequisites: C or better in WGST 112 or WGST 113; C or better in one additional WGST course at the 200-level or higher.

title: Race, Gender, and Graphic Novels
description: Representations of race and gender in comics with a special emphasis on the experiences of African Americans.
subject: WGST

title: Ecofeminism
description: An exploration of the connections between oppression of women and oppression of nature.
subject: WGST
prereq: Prerequisites: 3 hours in philosophy beyond the 100 level.

title: Issues in Women's Health
description: An exploration of women’s health and health care concerns from multiple perspectives.
subject: WGST

title: Adolescent Mentoring
description: Application of skills and theories of adolescent mentoring taught in the classroom to a supervised, structured mentoring field experience.
subject: WGST

title: Women, Gender, and Crime
description: Impact of gender-based relations on crime and the criminal justice system.
subject: WGST

title: Language and Gender
description: Approaches to gender and language emphasizing the social grounding of both; how language reflects sociocultural values and is a tool for constructing different types of social organization.
subject: WGST

title: Special Topics in Women's Gender Studies
description: Course content varies and will be announced in the schedule of courses by title. May be repeated as content varies by title.
subject: WGST

title: Maternal and Child Health
description: Public health issues, social and behavioral science, policies, programs, and services related to maternal and child health in the United States and other countries.
subject: WGST

title: Lesbian, Gay, Bisexual and Transgender (LGBT) Health
description: Health status and concerns of lesbian, gay, bisexual, and transgender communities. Includes an examination of measurement issues and methodological considerations in research, as well as intervention efforts targeting LGBT populations.
subject: WGST

title: Feminist Theories and Epistemologies
description: Examination of feminist theories and epistemologies from diverse disciplines and intellectual movements, providing an overview of historical developments in feminist discourse. Emphasis on debates surrounding such concepts as gender, identity, difference, power, and embodiment.
subject: WGST

title: Political Theory and Feminism
description: How contemporary feminist theory has responded to and reformulated traditional theories about the role and nature of women.
subject: WGST

title: Race, Class, Gender, and Sexuality
description: Historical and contemporary dimensions of social inequality centered in race, social class, gender, and sexuality.
subject: WGST

title: Engendering Global Capitalism
description: The origins of global capitalism, the nature of money and debt, the roles of gender, race and class in social formations, and the relationship between production and reproduction.
subject: WGST

title: Women in American Politics
description: Impact of gender in American politics; elections, representation, rights, social movements, legal institutions, and public policy. Explores class, race, and sexuality issues within gender.
subject: WGST

title: Women Explorers and Travelers
description: Examines in geographical and historical contexts the activities of various women travelers and explorers.
subject: WGST

title: Nutrition in Women's Health
description: An examination of the particular nutritional needs of women through the life cycle with emphasis on disease prevention and how nutrition is related to a woman’s health and wellness.
subject: WGST

title: Women's Studies Workshop
description: Selected topics in women’s studies that are grounded in community concerns with an emphasis on individual action-research. May be repeated for a total of 2 hours credit.
subject: WGST

title: Women, Work and Health: Global Perspectives
description: Intersections of women’s work and women’s health in diverse social, cultural, economic, geographic, and political contexts.
subject: WGST

title: Topics in British Women Writers
description: Selected topics related to works by British women authors from various periods, regions, or genres. May be repeated for credit as topics vary.
subject: WGST

title: Topics in American Women Writers
description: Selected topics related to works by American women authors from various periods, regions, or genres. May be repeated for credit as topics vary.
subject: WGST

title: Psychology of Women
description: Women’s diversity explored through research on personality, stereotypes, status and power, biological aspects, socialization, sexuality, relationships, mothering, work and achievement, violence against women, psychological disorders, and feminist therapies.
subject: WGST

title: African American Women in Nineteenth and Twentieth Centuries
description: This course will acquaint students with some of the secondary literature in African American women’s history from the late nineteenth century through the twentieth century. The course examines the impact of race, gender, and class on the lives of black women and explores the historical relationship between African American women, work, family, community, and politics.
subject: WGST

title: History of American Women
description: Selected research topics on the cultural, social, economic, and political roles and contributions of American women.
subject: WGST

title: Feminist Perspective in Social Work Practice
description: Examines the application of feminist theories, concepts, and principles to social work practice. Assesses women’s experiences in society and the impact of social, political, and economic structures. Investigates feminist interventions pertaining to individuals, families, organizations, communities, and the larger social environment.
subject: WGST

title: Gender and Culture
description: Different cultures’ ideas about gender and use of gender to organize social groups in a wide range of societies, including American subcultures.
subject: WGST

title: Population Perspectives of Gender, Sexuality and Health
description: Population perspectives and demographic approaches to the measurement of gender, sexuality, and health.
subject: WGST

title: Directed Reading and Research
description: Directed research and reading in subjects to be individually assigned.
subject: WGST

title: Special Topics in Women's Studies
description: A special topic focusing on an area within women’s studies not usually covered in other graduate courses in women’s studies.
subject: WGST

title: Seminar in Women's Studies
description: A capstone seminar applying women’s studies theories and methodologies to professional or discipline-based research projects.
subject: WGST
prereq: Prerequisites: WGST 701.
